Parity note: only one axis is translated at a time (the other translation component stays 0). The two-branch design (gap vs center distribution) is the non-obvious part — overlapping selections cannot be gap-distributed so it falls back to fixing endpoints and spreading centers.

---

### packages/element/src/cropElement.ts

All geometry for cropping an image element: resizing the crop window via transform handles (with aspect-ratio and flip support), and reconstructing the uncropped image element/dimensions. Works in image natural-pixel space mapped to on-canvas "uncropped" dimensions.

- `MINIMAL_CROP_SIZE = 10` — L32. Floor for crop width/height in canvas units.
- `cropElement(element, elementsMap, transformHandle, naturalWidth, naturalHeight, pointerX, pointerY, widthAspectRatio?)` — L34-L406. The main crop-drag handler. Returns `{ x, y, width, height, crop }`.
  - L44-L51: computes `uncroppedWidth/Height` from `getUncroppedWidthAndHeight`, the natural→uncropped scale factors, and `croppedLeft/croppedTop` (current crop offset expressed in uncropped/canvas units).
  - L64-L71: inverse-rotates the pointer by `-element.angle` about element center, so all subsequent math is in unrotated space.
  - L76-L83: initializes `crop` to existing crop or a full-image crop `{x:0,y:0,width:naturalWidth,height:naturalHeight,naturalWidth,naturalHeight}`.
  - L88-L89: `isFlippedByX/Y` from `element.scale[0]/[1] === -1`.
  - L94-L127: per-edge clamping. For each of `n/s/e/w` contained in the handle, computes the new width/height clamped between `MINIMAL_CROP_SIZE` and a flip-dependent max (you can't crop beyond the original image bounds; flip swaps which side the slack is on).
  - `updateCropWidthAndHeight(crop)` (L129-L132): maps `nextHeight/Width` back into natural pixels via the scale factors.
  - `adjustFlipForHandle(handle, crop)` (L136-L161): re-derives crop w/h, then shifts `crop.x`/`crop.y` by the delta of crop dimension change — direction depends on the handle edge and flip state (this is the "which corner stays anchored" bookkeeping in crop space).
  - L163-L378: the big `switch (transformHandle)`. Edge handles (`n/s/e/w`) when `widthAspectRatio` is set compute a `MAX_WIDTH`/`MAX_HEIGHT` from distance-to-left/right (or top/bottom) times 2 and lock the other dimension to the ratio, then center-shift the crop on the perpendicular axis. Corner handles (`ne/nw/se/sw`) decide which dimension is dominant by comparing `changeInWidth` vs `changeInHeight` signs, clamp to a flip-aware max, and lock the other to the aspect ratio. Every case calls `adjustFlipForHandle`.
  - L383-L389: `recomputeOrigin(...)` to get the new top-left origin.
  - L392-L397: **resets `crop` to `null`** when the new crop width/height are `isCloseTo` the natural dimensions (back to full image). Invariant: a full-coverage crop is represented as `null`.
- `recomputeOrigin(stateAtCropStart, transformHandle, width, height, shouldMaintainAspectRatio?)` (internal) — L408-L476. Computes the element's new `(x,y)` so the correct corner stays fixed during a crop-resize. Uses `getResizedElementAbsoluteCoords` at both old and new sizes, picks `newTopLeft` from which corner is anchored (`n/w/nw` anchor bottom-right; `ne` anchors bottom-left; `sw` anchors top-right). For aspect-ratio edge drags it re-centers on the perpendicular axis. Then performs a double rotation: rotate `newTopLeft` and `newCenter` into the original angle, then rotate top-left back by `-angle` about the rotated new center — this keeps the visual rotation pivot stable. Finally offsets by `stateAtCropStart.x - newBoundsX1` to convert from bounds space to element-origin space. Note: `startCenter` typed `any` (L423) and `Point` cast at L460.
- `getUncroppedImageElement(element, elementsMap)` — L479-L548. Reconstructs the full (uncropped) image element from a cropped one. Builds edge-direction unit vectors (`topEdgeNormalized`, `leftEdgeNormalized`) from rotated absolute corners, applies `adjustCropPosition` for flips, then walks back along the negative crop offset (scaled by `width/naturalWidth`) to find the uncropped rotated top-left, computes the uncropped center, and inverse-rotates to get the unrotated `(x,y)`. Returns a new element with `crop: null`, full `width/height`. Returns the element unchanged if no crop. Math note: this is vector-space (not matrix) — uses `vectorAdd/Scale/Normalize/Subtract` and `pointFromVector`.
- `getUncroppedWidthAndHeight(element)` — L550-L567. If cropped, `uncroppedWidth = element.width / (crop.width / crop.naturalWidth)` (and symmetric for height) — i.e. scale the displayed size up by the inverse of the crop fraction. Otherwise returns element's own w/h.
- `adjustCropPosition(crop, scale)` (internal) — L569-L591. For flipped axes, mirrors the crop origin: `cropX = naturalWidth - |cropX| - crop.width` (and Y analog). Returns `{ cropX, cropY }`.
- `getFlipAdjustedCropPosition(element, natural = false)` — L593-L629. Returns the crop origin adjusted for flips, either in natural pixels (`natural=true`) or scaled into uncropped/canvas units (dividing by `naturalWidth/width`). Returns `null` if no crop. Note slightly different flip formula than `adjustCropPosition` (`naturalWidth - crop.width - crop.x`, no `Math.abs`).

Parity note: two coordinate spaces are juggled — image *natural pixels* (`crop.x/y/width/height/naturalWidth/naturalHeight`) and *canvas* dimensions (`element.width/height`, "uncropped" sizes). The scale factors `naturalWidthToUncropped`/`naturalHeightToUncropped` bridge them. Flips (`scale === -1`) invert anchoring everywhere.

---

### packages/element/src/dragElements.ts

Handles translating elements while dragging: existing selections (with grid snap, group/frame inclusion, bound-element updates, and arrow-unbinding rules) and live-creating a new element by drag (with aspect ratio, resize-from-center, text auto-wrap, image initial dimensions).

- `dragSelectedElements(pointerDownState, _selectedElements, offset, scene, snapOffset, gridSize)` — L35-L171. Moves the current selection.
  - L46-L52: early-return guard — a single *bound* elbow arrow is not dragged directly (it follows its bindings).
  - L54-L67: filters out elbow arrows whose both bound endpoints aren't also in the selection (avoids dragging an elbow arrow whose anchors aren't moving).
  - L69-L85: builds `elementsToUpdate` as a `Set` (dedupe), and if any selected element is a frame-like, **adds all elements whose `frameId` is in the selected frames** so frame contents move with the frame. Comment notes the Set guards against double-updating frame children.
  - L87-L97: collects `origElements` from `pointerDownState.originalElements`; **bails entirely (`return`) if any original is missing** (e.g. duplicate-during-drag) to avoid undefined behavior.
  - L99-L104: `adjustedOffset = calculateOffset(getCommonBounds(origElements), offset, snapOffset, gridSize)`.
  - L110-L170: for each element, computes whether its start/end bound elements are themselves selected; for non-arrows it moves the element + its bound text via `updateElementCoords`, then `updateBoundElements(... simultaneouslyUpdated)`. For arrows it only moves when (selection > 1, OR drag exceeds `DRAGGING_THRESHOLD`, OR arrow has no bindings) — and **unbinds** start/end when that endpoint's bindable is not part of the selection (prevents arrows snapping weirdly when dragged off a shape). The `isArrow` variable at L111 is `!isArrowElement(element)` (inverted naming — actually "is NOT arrow").
- `calculateOffset(commonBounds, dragOffset, snapOffset, gridSize): {x,y}` (internal) — L173-L202. Adds drag + snap offset to the common-bounds top-left; if either snap axis is 0 it applies grid snapping (`getGridPoint`) on that axis only. Returns the delta relative to the original common-bounds origin. Invariant: snap offset takes precedence over grid; grid only applies on axes with no active snap.
- `updateElementCoords(pointerDownState, element, scene, dragOffset)` (internal) — L204-L220. Reads the element's *original* position from `pointerDownState.originalElements` (falls back to current element), adds `dragOffset`, and `scene.mutateElement` to the new `x/y`. Invariant: drag is always computed relative to the at-pointer-down position, not cumulative — avoids drift.
- `getDragOffsetXY(selectedElements, x, y): [number, number]` — L222-L229. Returns `[x - x1, y - y1]` where `(x1,y1)` is the common-bounds top-left — the pointer offset from selection origin.
- `dragNewElement({ ... }): void` — L231-L353. Creates/sizes a new element during the initial draw-drag. Destructured options object (L247-L268) including `newElement`, `elementType`, origin/current coords, `width/height`, `shouldMaintainAspectRatio`, `shouldResizeFromCenter`, `zoom`, `scene`, optional `widthAspectRatio`, `originOffset`, `informMutation`.
  - L269-L294: aspect-ratio logic. If `widthAspectRatio` provided, `height = width / widthAspectRatio`. Else uses `getPerfectElementSize` picking the dominant axis (whichever of |y-originY| vs |x-originX| is larger) so the cursor "sticks" to one side of the bounding box; signs propagate via `x<originX ? -width : width`. Negative heights are absoluted.
  - L296-L304: positions `newX/newY` (drag up/left flips origin); `shouldResizeFromCenter` doubles width/height and centers on origin.
  - L306-L329: text-element special case — height fixed to `newElement.height`, width clamped to a minimum via `getMinTextElementWidth`, and `autoResize:false` set once the horizontal drag exceeds `TEXT_AUTOWRAP_THRESHOLD / zoom` (zoom-aware threshold).
  - L331-L352: only mutates when `width !== 0 && height !== 0`; for images records `initialWidth/initialHeight`; applies `originOffset`; calls `scene.mutateElement(newElement, {...}, { informMutation, isDragging: false })`.

Parity note: drag offsets are always anchored to original positions (pointer-down snapshot) to prevent floating-point drift. `DRAGGING_THRESHOLD` gates arrow unbinding so a click-select on a bound arrow doesn't accidentally unbind it. Grid snap only applies on axes without an active alignment snap.

---

### packages/element/src/delta.ts

The change-tracking engine: a generic `Delta<T>` (forward/backward partial diffs), plus `AppStateDelta` and `ElementsDelta` that wrap deltas for app-state and element-map changes respectively. These power undo/redo (via `inverse`), history squashing (via `squash`), and applying changes (`applyTo`) including collaboration conflict resolution, z-index reordering, binding re-resolution, and text/arrow redraw. This is the heart of the store/history system.

#### `class Delta<T>` — L75-L495

A pure data holder of two partials: `deleted` (old values) and `inserted` (new values).

- `private constructor(deleted: Partial<T>, inserted: Partial<T>)` — L76-L79.
- `static create<T>(deleted, inserted, modifier?, modifierOptions?)` — L81-L100. Optionally runs a `modifier(partial, "deleted"|"inserted")` over one or both partials (`modifierOptions` selects which) before constructing.
- `static calculate<T>(prevObject, nextObject, modifier?, postProcess?): Delta<T>` — L110-L141. Returns `Delta.empty()` if reference-equal. Else iterates `getDifferences(prev,next)` filling `deleted[key]=prev[key]`, `inserted[key]=next[key]`, optionally runs `postProcess` then `create`. Comment (L126-L130) notes O(n^3) cost but justifies it (only on store recordings, only on changed elements, shallow first-level compare).
- `static empty()` — L143-L145. `new Delta({}, {})`.
- `static isEmpty<T>(delta): boolean` — L147-L151. True when both partials have zero keys.
- `static merge<T>(delta1, delta2, delta3 = empty())` — L156-L165. Object-spread-merges the three deltas' deleted/inserted partials (later wins).
- `static mergeObjects<T>(prev, added, removed = {}): T` — L170-L182. Clones `prev`, deletes `removed` keys, spreads `added`. Used for id-set merging.
- `static mergeArrays<T>(prev, added, removed, predicate?)` — L187-L200. Converts arrays to keyed objects (via `arrayToObject` + `predicate`), merges with `mergeObjects`, returns `Object.values`. Used to merge `boundElements` lists by id.
- `static diffObjects<T,K,V>(deleted, inserted, property, setValue)` — L205-L256. Post-process helper: for a reference-typed `property`, computes left/right key differences between the deleted-object and inserted-object, rebuilds each side via `setValue`, and either re-sets the property to just the differing keys or deletes the property from both partials when no real diff exists. Handles non-object equal primitives by deleting from both (L252-L255).
- `static diffArrays<T,K,V>(deleted, inserted, property, groupBy)` — L261-L312. Array analog of `diffObjects`: groups arrays by `groupBy`, finds left/right key differences, filters each array to only the differing members, re-sets or deletes the property.
- `static isLeftDifferent / isRightDifferent / isInnerDifferent / isDifferent<T>(object1, object2, skipShallowCompare=false): boolean` — L317-L384. Each calls `distinctKeysIterator` with join `"left"/"right"/"inner"/"full"` and returns whether any distinct key exists (`.next().value` truthiness).
- `static getLeftDifferences / getRightDifferences / getInnerDifferences / getDifferences<T>(...)` — L389-L436. Same iterator but materialized to a **sorted** array of differing keys.
- `private static *distinctKeysIterator<T>(join, object1, object2, skipShallowCompare=false)` — L445-L494. Generator. Returns immediately if reference-equal. Selects key set by join type (left=keys of o1, right=keys of o2, inner=intersection, full=union; `assertNever` on unknown). For each key with `value1 !== value2`, **skips** the key if both values are non-null objects that are `isShallowEqual` (unless `skipShallowCompare`); otherwise yields the key. Performance/parity note: comparison is shallow at the first level only — deep object changes that are shallow-equal at top level are treated as equal.

#### `interface DeltaContainer<T>` — L500-L522

Contract for app-level delta collections: `inverse(): DeltaContainer<T>`, `applyTo(previous, ...options): [T, boolean]` (boolean = visible change), `squash(delta): this`, `isEmpty(): boolean`.

#### `class AppStateDelta implements DeltaContainer<AppState>` — L524-L1013

Wraps a `Delta<ObservedAppState>`.

- `private constructor(delta)` / `static create(delta)` — L525-L529.
- `static calculate<T extends ObservedAppState>(prevAppState, nextAppState): AppStateDelta` — L531-L544. Builds delta via `Delta.calculate` with `orderAppStateKeys` modifier (stable key order for hashing) and `postProcess`.
- `static restore(dto)` — L546-L549; `static empty()` — L551-L553.
- `inverse(): AppStateDelta` — L555-L558. Swaps inserted/deleted.
- `squash(delta): this` — L560-L630. Specially merges the reference-typed id maps (`selectedElementIds`, `selectedGroupIds`, `lockedMultiSelections`) via `mergeObjects` on both deleted and inserted sides, only re-adding them to the merged partials if non-empty, then `Delta.merge`s the rest. Mutates `this.delta`.
- `applyTo(appState, nextElements): [AppState, boolean]` — L632-L710. Pulls deleted/inserted id maps out, merges selection/group/locked maps via `mergeObjects(current, inserted, deleted)`, reconstructs `selectedLinearElement` as a fresh `LinearElementEditor` if the inserted element still exists in `nextElements`, spreads the directly-applicable partial, then calls `filterInvisibleChanges` to decide the visible-change boolean. Wrapped in try/catch that rethrows in test/dev, else returns `[appState, false]`.
- `isEmpty()` — L712-L714.
- `private filterInvisibleChanges(prevAppState, nextAppState, nextElements): boolean` — L721-L871. **Mutates** `nextAppState` to strip state referencing deleted elements, and returns whether a *visible* change remains. Splits observed app-state into standalone props (`stripElementsProps`) and element-related props (`stripStandaloneProps`); a standalone diff is always visible. For element-related changed keys it switches per key: `selectedElementIds` → `filterSelectedElements`; `selectedGroupIds` → `filterSelectedGroups` (computing `nonDeletedGroupIds` lazily once); `croppingElementId`/`editingGroupId`/`selectedLinearElement` → null out if the referenced element is deleted/missing, else flag visible; `lockedMultiSelections`/`activeLockedId` → shallow/identity compare flags visible (with TODOs noting these may be redundant). `assertNever` default.
- `private static filterSelectedElements(selectedElementIds, elements, visibleDifferenceFlag)` — L873-L900. Drops ids whose element is deleted/missing; sets flag visible if any surviving id maps to a live element (or if the set was empty to begin with).
- `private static filterSelectedGroups(selectedGroupIds, nonDeletedGroupIds, visibleDifferenceFlag)` — L902-L927. Analog for groups against `nonDeletedGroupIds`.
- `private static stripElementsProps(delta): Partial<ObservedStandaloneAppState>` — L929-L948. Destructures away `editingGroupId, selectedGroupIds, selectedElementIds, selectedLinearElement, croppingElementId, lockedMultiSelections, activeLockedId`, returning the rest (the standalone props). Type-cast comments warn not to remove casts.
- `private static stripStandaloneProps(delta): Partial<ObservedElementsAppState>` — L950-L961. Inverse: removes `name, viewBackgroundColor`, returns element-related props.
- `private static postProcess<T>(deleted, inserted): [Partial<T>, Partial<T>]` — L967-L1001. Runs `Delta.diffObjects` for `selectedElementIds` (setValue → `true`), `selectedGroupIds` (→ prev ?? false), `lockedMultiSelections` (→ prev ?? {}); try/catch with rethrow in test/dev, `finally` returns the (mutated) pair.
- `private static orderAppStateKeys(partial)` — L1003-L1012. Returns a new object with keys inserted in sorted order (deterministic for hashing).

#### Types — L1015-L1026

- `type ElementPartial<TElement = ExcalidrawElement>` — L1015-L1016. `Omit<Partial<Ordered<TElement>>, "id" | "updated" | "seed">` — the diff-able element shape (id/updated/seed excluded from deltas).
- `type ApplyToOptions = { excludedProperties?: Set<keyof ElementPartial> }` — L1018-L1020.
- `type ApplyToFlags = { containsVisibleDifference: boolean; containsZindexDifference: boolean; applyDirection: "forward"|"backward"|undefined }` — L1022-L1026.

#### `class ElementsDelta implements DeltaContainer<SceneElementsMap>` — L1032-L2066

Holds three records keyed by element id: `added`, `removed`, `updated`, each a `Delta<ElementPartial>`.

- `private constructor(added, removed, updated)` — L1033-L1037.
- `static create(added, removed, updated, options={shouldRedistribute:false})` — L1039-L1084. When `shouldRedistribute`, re-buckets every delta into added/removed/updated by testing `satisfiesAddition/Removal` (else updated). In test/dev runs `validate` on each bucket. Returns the new instance.
- `static restore(dto)` — L1086-L1089.
- `private static satisfiesAddition = ({deleted,inserted}) => deleted.isDeleted === true && !inserted.isDeleted` — L1091-L1096. (An "add" goes from deleted→not-deleted.)
- `private static satisfiesRemoval = ({deleted,inserted}) => !deleted.isDeleted && inserted.isDeleted === true` — L1098-L1102.
- `private static satisfiesUpdate = ({deleted,inserted}) => !!deleted.isDeleted === !!inserted.isDeleted` — L1104-L1107.
- `private static satisfiesCommmonInvariants = ({deleted,inserted})` — L1109-L1124. Both versions are non-negative integers and differ. (Note misspelled name "Commmon".)
- `private static satisfiesUniqueInvariants = (elementsDelta, id)` — L1126-L1133. Exactly one of added/removed/updated holds the id.
- `private static validate(elementsDelta, type, satifiesSpecialInvariants)` — L1135-L1153. Throws if any delta in the bucket breaks common/unique/special invariants (only run in test/dev).
- `static calculate<T extends OrderedExcalidrawElement>(prevElements, nextElements): ElementsDelta` — L1163-L1261. Core diff. Returns empty if reference-equal. (1) For prev elements missing in next: synthesize a deletion (`inserted={isDeleted:true, version+1, versionNonce}`), bucket as `removed` (or `updated` if already deleted). (2) For next elements missing in prev: synthesize an addition (`deleted={isDeleted:true, version-1, versionNonce}`), bucket as `added` (or `updated` if the new element is itself deleted). (3) For elements present in both with differing `versionNonce`: `Delta.calculate` with `stripIrrelevantProps` + `postProcess`; if `isDeleted` flipped (both booleans), bucket as added/removed accordingly, else `updated`. Notable: uses `randomInteger()` for synthetic versionNonces, and `version±1` to keep version monotonic.
- `static empty()` — L1263-L1265.
- `inverse(): ElementsDelta` — L1267-L1284. Swaps each delta's inserted/deleted, AND **swaps the `removed` and `added` buckets** (passing `removed` as added arg) to preserve invariants.
- `isEmpty(): boolean` — L1286-L1292. All three records empty.
- `applyLatestChanges(prevElements, nextElements, modifierOptions?): ElementsDelta` — L1301-L1386. Refreshes captured delta values with the latest element state. The `modifier` (L1306-L1346) picks `prevElement` for "deleted" partials and `nextElement` for "inserted", and copies each key's latest value from that element — **except** `boundElements`, which is kept as the captured diff. Per-bucket it rebuilds deltas via `Delta.create(...modifier...)`, dropping deltas that become inner-equal. Returns a redistributed `ElementsDelta` (since `isDeleted` may have changed).
- `applyTo(elements, snapshot=StoreSnapshot.empty().elements, options?): [SceneElementsMap, boolean]` — L1388-L1465. The big apply. Clones `elements` into a new `nextElements` Map (no mutation of input). Builds an applier via `createApplier`, applies added→removed→updated, then `resolveConflicts` for bindings, merges all changed elements. On error returns `[elements, true]` (intentionally true — see comment L1437-L1439 about avoiding broken redo). Then in a second try block reorders by fractional index (`reorderElements`) and `redrawElements`; `finally` returns `[nextElements, flags.containsVisibleDifference]`.
- `squash(delta): this` — L1467-L1565. Merges another ElementsDelta into this one. For each id in the incoming added/removed/updated, finds any existing delta across all three buckets, merges `boundElements` arrays specially (`mergeBoundElements`, L1474-L1509, keyed by `x.id`), deletes the id from the other buckets, and re-files the merged delta into the correct bucket (for `updated` it keeps it in whichever bucket the prevDelta lived). Validates in test/dev.
- `private static createApplier = (prevElements, nextElements, snapshot, flags, options?) => (deltas) => Map` — L1567-L1610. Returns a function that reduces a delta record into a Map of next elements: for each id it gets the element (via `createGetter`), applies the delta (`applyDelta`), sets it into `nextElements` and the accumulator, and infers `flags.applyDirection` from version comparison (prev.version > next.version ⇒ "backward", else "forward") the first time.
- `private static createGetter = (elements, snapshot, flags) => (id, partial) => element` — L1612-L1645. Resolves an element by id: from `elements`, else from `snapshot` (setting `containsZindexDifference` and possibly `containsVisibleDifference`), else **synthesizes** a brand-new element via `newElementWith({id, version:1}, partial)` (handles remotely-added elements).
- `private static applyDelta(element, delta, flags, options?)` — L1647-L1706. Builds the directly-applicable partial from `delta.inserted` keys, **skipping** `boundElements` and any `options.excludedProperties`. Merges `boundElements` separately via `mergeArrays`. If no visible diff yet, strips `index` and runs `checkForVisibleDifference`. Sets `containsZindexDifference` if `delta.deleted.index !== delta.inserted.index`. Returns `newElementWith(element, partial, true)` (the `true` bumps version).
- `private static checkForVisibleDifference(element, partial): boolean` — L1711-L1732. Deleted+still-deleted ⇒ false; deleted→undeleted ⇒ true (add); live→deleted ⇒ true (remove); else `Delta.isRightDifferent(element, partial)`.
- `private resolveConflicts(prevElements, nextElements, applyDirection="forward")` — L1742-L1831. The collaboration/binding fixer. Defines an `updater` (L1748-L1791) that only ever mutates `nextElements` entries (via `newElementWith` if untouched, else `mutateElement`), bumping version forward/backward. Then: for every `removed` id calls `unbindAffected`; for every `added` id calls `rebindAffected`; for `updated` ids **only those whose changed props intersect `bindingProperties`** calls `rebindAffected` (skipping deleted). Computes `prevAffectedElements`, then `squash`es a freshly-calculated delta of the affected elements back into `this`. Returns the affected-elements map.
- `private static unbindAffected(prevElements, nextElements, id, updater)` — L1837-L1855. Calls `BoundElement.unbindAffected` and `BindableElement.unbindAffected` for both the before- and after-removal element instances (re-fetched each call via closures, since instances may change).
- `private static rebindAffected(prevElements, nextElements, id, updater)` — L1861-L1889. Unbinds the prev `BoundElement`, rebinds the next; for `BindableElement` it unbinds prev but only forwards the update for **text** elements (comment: arrows can't be auto-rebound to bindables, TODO #7348), and rebinds the next bindable.
- `static redrawElements(nextElements, changedElements)` — L1891-L1914. Creates a temp `Scene(nextElements, {skipValidation:true})` (because no real scene exists during history/server apply), then redraws text bounding boxes and bound arrows. try/catch rethrow in test/dev; `finally` returns `nextElements`.
- `private static redrawTextBoundingBoxes(scene, changed)` — L1916-L1962. Collects container+boundText pairs (both directions: a bound-text element points to its container, and a container points to its bound text), dedup by container id, then `redrawTextBoundingBox` for each pair (skipping if either is deleted).
- `private static redrawBoundArrows(scene, changed)` — L1964-L1976. For each non-deleted bindable changed element, calls `updateBoundElements(element, scene, {changedElements: changed})`. Comment flags this as expensive (a possible optimization target).
- `private static reorderElements(elements, changed, flags)` — L1978-L2012. Short-circuits if no z-index difference. Else orders all elements by fractional index (`orderByFractionalIndex`), finds which changed elements actually moved (`getRightDifferences` between unordered and ordered, `skipShallowCompare=true`), flags visible difference if any moved, and returns `arrayToMap(syncMovedIndices(ordered, moved))`. Performance note: only synchronizes actually-moved indices, with a fallback to fixing all invalid indices.
- `private static postProcess(deleted, inserted): [ElementPartial, ElementPartial]` — L2018-L2057. Diffs `boundElements` arrays by id; for `points` (linear/freedraw) it **does not diff** — it either keeps both captured point arrays as-is or deletes `points` from both partials when `!Delta.isDifferent(deletedPoints, insertedPoints)` (comment: can't ensure multiplayer order without per-point fractional indices). try/catch rethrow in test/dev; `finally` returns the pair.
- `private static stripIrrelevantProps(partial): ElementPartial` — L2059-L2065. Destructures away `id` and `updated`, returning the rest.

Parity/architecture notes for `delta.ts`:
- The whole module is the undo/redo + collaboration substrate; a Canvas/Svelte reimplementation that wants real history fidelity must reproduce: forward/backward partial diffs, shallow-first-level comparison semantics, deterministic key ordering, the added/removed/updated tri-bucket with strict invariants (`version` integer, monotonic, unique bucket per id), z-index resync via fractional indices, and binding re-resolution (`unbind`/`rebind`) plus text/arrow redraw on apply.
- `version`/`versionNonce`/`seed`/`updated`/`id` handling is deliberate: `id`,`updated`,`seed` are stripped from element deltas; `version`/`versionNonce` drive direction inference and conflict resolution.
- Error handling pattern throughout: try/catch that **rethrows in test/dev** but degrades gracefully in production, often via `finally`-return.


---

## Cluster: element__src__2

This cluster covers element duplication, the elbow-arrow routing engine (A* pathfinding), element-link URL helpers, embeddable iframe parsing, flowchart node generation/navigation, fractional-index synchronization, and frame membership/clipping logic.

---

### packages/element/src/duplicate.ts

Purpose: Deep-clones Excalidraw elements and orchestrates multi-element duplication (alt-drag / duplicate action), preserving groups, frames, bound text, container bindings, and z-order, then rewires bindings and frame membership.

- `duplicateElement<TElement extends ExcalidrawElement>(editingGroupId, groupIdMapForOperation: Map<GroupId, GroupId>, element: TElement, randomizeSeed?: boolean): Readonly<TElement>` — L61-L91. Deep-copies one element via `deepCopyElement`, assigns a fresh `randomId()`, refreshes `updated` via `getUpdatedTimestamp()`, optionally re-seeds (`randomInteger()` + `bumpVersion`), and remaps `groupIds` through `getNewGroupIdsForDuplication` using the shared `groupIdMapForOperation` so multiple elements share consistent new group ids. In test env stamps the original id via `__test__defineOrigId`. Side effect: mutates the copy in place. Invariant: original element untouched.
- `duplicateElements(opts): { duplicatedElements, duplicateElementsMap, elementsWithDuplicates, origIdToDuplicateId }` — L93-L422. The heavy orchestrator. `opts` is a union: `type:"everything"` (programmatic, optional `preserveFrameChildrenOrder`) or `type:"in-place"` (interactive; carries `idsOfElementsToDuplicate: Map` and `appState` with `editingGroupId`/`selectedGroupIds`); both share `elements`, optional `randomizeSeed`, and an `overrides` callback. Algorithm: normalizes element order (`normalizeElementOrder`, L188), maintains `processedIds` to avoid double-insertion when backtracking discontiguous groups (L158, L206-210), and walks elements once. For each unprocessed target it handles, in priority order: (a) selected-group duplication — gathers group elements, flattening frame children before the frame (L281-296); (b) frame-child skipping when the frame itself is being duplicated (L301-307); (c) frame duplication — copies children then the frame, inserting after the last frame/child index (L309-331); (d) bound-text containers — copies `[element, boundText]` together (L336-356); (e) bound-to-container text — copies `[container, element]` (L358-375); (f) default single-element copy (L380-383). Insertion position uses `findLastIndex` + `insertBeforeOrAfterIndex`. After the loop calls `fixDuplicatedBindingsAfterDuplication` (L388) and `bindElementsToFramesAfterDuplication` (L394), then applies `overrides` per duplicate (L400-414). Notable nested helpers: `copyElements<T>` (L197-239, reduces an element/array to duplicates, populating all the cross-reference maps `duplicateElementsMap`/`origIdToDuplicateId`/`duplicateIdToOrigElement`) and `insertBeforeOrAfterIndex` (L242-256, splices clones into `elementsWithDuplicates` at `index+1`, or pushes if past the end). Z-order invariant: clones are placed immediately after their originals/frames.
- `_deepCopyElement(val: any, depth: number = 0)` — L433-484. Internal klona-adapted recursive clone. Only clones plain `[object Object]` and arrays; returns primitives and unknown object types by reference. At `depth === 0` it skips non-serializable cache keys `shape` and `canvas` (L450-452) — performance/correctness invariant: those caches are rebuilt on render. In DEV warns on unexpected non-plain object types (L471-481).
- `deepCopyElement<T extends ExcalidrawElement>(val: T): Mutable<T>` — L495-499. Typed wrapper over `_deepCopyElement`. Does NOT regenerate id/nonce/seed; purpose is to break object references for immutability.
- `__test__defineOrigId(clonedObj: object, origId: string)` — L501-507. Defines a non-enumerable, non-writable `ORIG_ID` property for test traceability.

---

### packages/element/src/elementLink.ts

Purpose: Create and parse deep-links to a specific element or group, encoded as a query param on the current page URL.

- `defaultGetElementLinkFromSelection: Exclude<AppProps["generateLinkForSelection"], undefined> = (id, type) => string` — L13-L29. Builds a URL from `window.location.href`, sets `ELEMENT_LINK_KEY` searchparam to `id`, and returns it normalized via `normalizeLink`. Falls back to normalized raw URL on parse error (logs to console). Side effect: reads `window.location`.
- `getLinkIdAndTypeFromSelection(selectedElements: ExcalidrawElement[], appState: AppState): { id: string; type: "element" | "group" } | null` — L31-L66. If exactly one selected → returns that element id as `"element"`. If multiple and linkable → returns the first selected group id (`appState.selectedGroupIds`) or the element's first `groupIds` entry as `"group"`. Returns null otherwise.
- `canCreateLinkFromElements(selectedElements: ExcalidrawElement[])` — L68-L80. True if exactly one element, or multiple elements all in the same group (`elementsAreInSameGroup`).
- `isElementLink(url: string)` — L82-L92. True if URL parses and contains `ELEMENT_LINK_KEY` AND its host matches `window.location.host`. Returns false on parse failure.
- `parseElementLinkFromURL(url: string)` — L94-L104. Returns the `ELEMENT_LINK_KEY` value from the URL, or null on absence/parse failure.

---

### packages/element/src/embeddable.ts

Purpose: Parses, validates, and transforms external links into embeddable iframe data (YouTube, Vimeo, Google Drive, Figma, val.town, Twitter/X, Reddit, GitHub Gist, MS Forms, Giphy, generic), with a per-link cache and a sandbox/allowlist security model.

- Module constants: `embeddedLinkCache: Map<string, IframeDataWithSandbox>` (L23, dedup/perf cache keyed by original link). Regexes `RE_YOUTUBE` (L25-26, captures path-type + id), `RE_VIMEO`, `RE_FIGMA`, `RE_GH_GIST` / `RE_GH_GIST_EMBED`, `RE_MSFORMS`, `RE_TWITTER` / `RE_TWITTER_EMBED`, `RE_VALTOWN`, `RE_GENERIC_EMBED`, `RE_GIPHY`, `RE_REDDIT` / `RE_REDDIT_EMBED` (L25-57). `ALLOWED_DOMAINS: Set<string>` (L133-150) and `ALLOW_SAME_ORIGIN: Set<string>` (L152-165) — security allowlists (supports `*.simplepdf.eu` wildcard).
- `parseYouTubeLikeTimestamp(url: string): number` — L59-L86. Extracts a start-time in seconds from `t`/`start` params; handles bare integers and `1h2m3s` compound format via regex `^(?:(\d+)h)?(?:(\d+)m)?(?:(\d+)s)?$`. Returns 0 when absent/unparseable.
- `parseGoogleDriveVideoLink(url: string): { fileId; resourceKey?; timestamp? } | null` — L88-L131. Validates host is `drive.google.com`, extracts fileId from `/file/d/<id>` path or `id` query param (`/open`, `/uc`), validates id charset `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`, preserves `resourcekey`, and normalizes timestamp.
- `createSrcDoc(body: string)` — L167-L169. Wraps body in `<html><body>…</body></html>`.
- `getEmbedLink(link: string | null | undefined): IframeDataWithSandbox | null` — L171-L400. The core resolver. Returns cached result if present. Computes `allowSameOrigin` via `matchHostname(link, ALLOW_SAME_ORIGIN)`. Dispatches by regex: YouTube (sets embed URL, `enablejsapi=1`, start time, portrait `315x560` for shorts vs `560x315`, type `"video"`, L190-223); Vimeo (validates numeric id, `560x315`, attaches `URIError` on invalid format, L225-249); Google Drive video (builds `/preview` URL, `560x315`, L251-279); Figma (`embed?embed_host=share`, `550x550`, generic, L281-300); val.town (rewrites `/v`→`/embed`, L302-318); MS Forms (appends `embed=true`, L320-322); Twitter/X, Reddit, GitHub Gist (all produce `type:"document"` with `srcdoc(theme)` callbacks injecting blockquote/script widgets, escaping via `escapeDoubleQuotes`, L324-386); else falls through to generic `560x840` default (L388-399). Side effect: populates `embeddedLinkCache`. Notable: aspect ratios are intrinsic sizes `{w,h}`, not pixel dimensions.
- `createPlaceholderEmbeddableLabel(element: ExcalidrawIframeLikeElement): ExcalidrawElement` — L402-437. Creates a centered Helvetica text element labeling an iframe/embed. Font size is `max(min(width/2, width/text.length), width/30)` (L413-416) — non-obvious clamping that scales label to box width. Wraps text to `element.width - 20`, centered both axes, inherits angle.
- `matchHostname(url, allowedHostnames: Set<string> | string): string | null` — L439-472. Strips `www.`, matches bare domain against `ALLOWED_DOMAINS`; tries first-subdomain wildcarding (`^([^.]+)` → `*`) for entries like `*.simplepdf.eu`. With a string arg does a single bare-domain comparison.
- `maybeParseEmbedSrc(str: string): string` — L474-500. Extracts a real URL from pasted Twitter/Reddit/Gist blockquote/script embed code, or Giphy clip → `giphy.com/embed/<id>`, or generic iframe/blockquote `src`/`href`; returns input unchanged if no match.
- `embeddableURLValidator(url, validateEmbeddable): boolean` — L502-535. Honors a host-supplied validator that may be a function (boolean/undefined → undefined falls through to default), a boolean, a RegExp, or an array of RegExp/domain. Default: `!!matchHostname(url, ALLOWED_DOMAINS)`.

---

### packages/element/src/fractionalIndex.ts

Purpose: Keeps each element's fractional `index` string in sync with array order; detects/validates invalid indices and regenerates them only where needed, using `@excalidraw/fractional-indexing`.

- `class InvalidFractionalIndexError extends Error` — L20-22. `code = "ELEMENT_HAS_INVALID_INDEX"`.
- `validateFractionalIndices(elements, { shouldThrow, includeBoundTextValidation, ignoreLogs?, reconciliationContext? })` — L46-126. Walks elements checking `isValidFractionalIndex(index, predecessor, successor)` for monotonicity (L72); optionally validates bound-text index strictly greater than container index (L83-94). Accumulates error messages, logs once with stacktrace (unless `ignoreLogs`), and throws `InvalidFractionalIndexError` if `shouldThrow`. Helper `stringifyElement` (L64-65) emits `index:id:type:isDeleted:version:versionNonce`.
- `orderByFractionalIndex(elements: OrderedExcalidrawElement[])` — L133-152. In-place `sort` comparing `a.index`/`b.index` lexicographically, breaking ties by `id`; if either element lacks an index, defensively keeps array order (returns 1). Mutates input array.
- `syncMovedIndices(elements, movedElements: ElementsMap): OrderedExcalidrawElement[]` — L158-199. Computes contiguous moved-index groups (`getMovedIndicesGroups`), generates candidate indices (`generateIndices`), validates them strictly (throws on invalid), then mutates only the moved elements via `mutateElement`. On any failure falls back to `syncInvalidIndices`. Side effect: mutates elements.
- `syncInvalidIndices(elements): OrderedExcalidrawElement[]` — L206-218. Detects invalid groups (`getInvalidIndicesGroups`) and mutates elements in place with corrected indices. WARN (in docstring): may modify non-moved elements in edge cases.
- `syncInvalidIndicesImmutable(elements): SceneElementsMap | undefined` — L225-237. Same as above but produces new element instances via `newElementWith` in a fresh map (no mutation).
- `getMovedIndicesGroups(elements, movedElements): number[][]` — L244-272 (internal). Builds contiguous index groups for moved elements where group[0] is the lower-bound index (i-1) and last item is the upper-bound index. Performance: single linear pass.
- `getInvalidIndicesGroups(elements): number[][]` — L279-377 (internal). Auto-detects invalid contiguous runs. Caches lower/upper bounds (`lowerBound`/`upperBound` + their indices) to avoid re-scanning; nested `getLowerBound` (L289-309, scans left, cache-aware) and `getUpperBound` (L312-339, scans right for next strictly-greater index, cache-aware) return `[index, position]`. WARN: first/last items of a group are the found bounds and need not be contiguous.
- `isValidFractionalIndex(index, predecessor, successor)` — L379-411 (internal). False if no index or `validateOrderKey` throws (format check). Then enforces `predecessor < index < successor`, or open-ended comparisons for first/last/only element.
- `generateIndices(elements, indicesGroups): Map<ExcalidrawElement, { index: FractionalIndex }>` — L413-442 (internal). For each group shifts off the lower-bound index and pops the upper-bound index, calls `generateNKeysBetween(lowerIndex, upperIndex, count)` and assigns the generated keys to the interior elements.
- `isOrderedElement(element): element is OrderedExcalidrawElement` — L444-456 (internal). True iff `element.index` is truthy (presence-only, not validity).

---

### packages/element/src/flowchart.ts

Purpose: Generates and navigates "flowchart" node graphs — creating evenly-spaced bound nodes via elbow arrows in a direction, and keyboard navigation between linked nodes.

- Module constants: `VERTICAL_OFFSET = 100`, `HORIZONTAL_OFFSET = 100` (L46-47); type `LinkDirection = "up"|"right"|"down"|"left"` (L44).
- `getLinkDirectionFromKey(key: string): LinkDirection` — L49-62. Maps arrow keys (`KEYS.ARROW_*`) to directions; default `"right"`.
- `getNodeRelatives(type: "predecessors"|"successors", node, elementsMap, direction): ExcalidrawBindableElement[]` — L64-132 (internal). Scans all elements for elbow arrows whose opposite-end binding targets `node` (start for successors, end for predecessors). For each, computes the edge point (last arrow point for predecessors, `[0,0]` for successors) in global space and derives a `Heading` via `headingForPointFromElement` against `aabbForElement(node)`. Filters by `compareHeading` matching the requested direction. Invariant: relative must be an `ExcalidrawBindableElement` (asserted).
- `getSuccessors(node, elementsMap, direction)` — L134-140 (internal); `getPredecessors(node, elementsMap, direction)` — L142-148 (exported). Thin wrappers over `getNodeRelatives`.
- `getOffsets(element: ExcalidrawFlowchartNodeElement, linkedNodes, direction): {x, y}` — L150-238 (internal). Computes placement offset for a new node. First tries a "clear lane" (full vertical/horizontal offset if no linked node overlaps the element's span, L165-192). Otherwise fans nodes out alternately left/right (or up/down) using parity math `(n+1)%2===0 ? ((n+1)/2)*offset : (n/2)*offset*-1` (L197-226). Geometry detail: `_HORIZONTAL_OFFSET = HORIZONTAL_OFFSET + element.width`, `_VERTICAL_OFFSET = VERTICAL_OFFSET + element.height`.
- `addNewNode(element, appState, direction, scene): { nextNode, bindingArrow }` — L240-290 (internal). Computes offsets from successors+predecessors, creates a new node (`newElement`) cloning style props, asserts it's a flowchart node, and creates a binding elbow arrow via `createBindingArrow`.
- `addNewNodes(startNode, appState, direction, scene, numberOfNodes): ExcalidrawElement[]` — L292-369. Distributes `numberOfNodes` evenly centered on the start node. For left/right, lays nodes vertically (`totalHeight = VERTICAL_OFFSET*(n-1) + n*height`, centered start at `startNode.y + height/2 - totalHeight/2`); for up/down, lays them horizontally analogously (L305-332). Each gets a `createBindingArrow`. Returns interleaved `[node, arrow, node, arrow, …]`.
- `createBindingArrow(startBindingElement, endBindingElement, direction, appState, scene)` — L371-502 (internal). Computes a start anchor point offset by `PADDING = 6` from the source edge per direction (L383-404) and an end point relative to start (L406-430). Creates an elbowed `newArrowElement` with `points: [(0,0),(endX,endY)]`, binds both ends with `bindBindingElement(..., "orbit", ...)`, then runs `LinearElementEditor.movePoints` and `updateElbowArrowPoints` to route. Returns merged `{...bindingArrow, ...update}`. Coordinate detail: end point is expressed relative to the arrow's start (subtracts startX/startY).
- `class FlowChartNavigator` — L504-633. Keyboard navigation state machine. Fields: `isExploring`, `sameLevelNodes: ExcalidrawElement[]`, `sameLevelIndex`, `direction: LinkDirection|null`, `visitedNodes: Set<id>`.
  - `clear()` — L514-520. Resets all state.
  - `exploreByDirection(element, elementsMap, direction): id | null` — L522-632. If direction changed, clears. Adds current to visited. If already exploring same direction with multiple same-level nodes, cycles `sameLevelIndex` (L553-562). Otherwise gathers successors+predecessors in `direction`; if any, sets them as the same-level set and returns the first (L564-584). Else searches the other three directions for an unvisited linked node to jump to (L598-629). Returns null when nothing found. Returns null immediately if element isn't bindable.
- `class FlowChartCreator` — L635-708. Holds pending nodes during creation. Fields: `isCreatingChart`, `numberOfNodes`, `direction: LinkDirection|null = "right"`, `pendingNodes: PendingExcalidrawElements|null`.
  - `createNodes(startNode, appState, direction, scene)` — L641-700. If direction changed, resets to a single new node (`addNewNode`); else increments `numberOfNodes` and regenerates the full set (`addNewNodes`). If the start node has a `frameId` and every pending node is within/overlapping that frame, mutates pending nodes' `frameId` to match (L677-699). Invariant asserts the frame is an `ExcalidrawFrameElement`.
  - `clear()` — L702-707. Resets state.
- `isNodeInFlowchart(element, elementsMap): boolean` — L710-725. True if any `"arrow"` element has start/end binding referencing the element's id.

---

### packages/element/src/frame.ts

Purpose: All frame-membership geometry and operations — intersection/containment tests, adding/removing elements from frames, clip decisions, and frame naming.

- `bindElementsToFramesAfterDuplication(nextElements, origElements, origIdToDuplicateId)` — L45-68. After duplication, rewires each new element's `frameId` to the duplicated frame's id (or null) using the orig→dup id map. Side effect: `mutateElement`.
- `isElementIntersectingFrame(element, frame, elementsMap): boolean` — L70-86. True if any of the element's line segments intersect any of the frame's line segments (`getElementLineSegments` + `doLineSegmentsIntersect`). O(n*m) segment test.
- `getElementsCompletelyInFrame(elements, frame, elementsMap)` — L88-99. Elements fully within the frame selection bounds (via `getElementsWithinSelection`, excluding frame-containing groups), filtered to non-frame elements that are either frameless or already belong to this frame.
- `isElementContainingFrame(element, frame, elementsMap): boolean` — L101-110. True if element bounds contain the frame bounds (`boundsContainBounds`) — e.g. a big background element.
- `getElementsIntersectingFrame(elements, frame)` — L112-120. Filters elements intersecting the frame.
- `elementsAreInFrameBounds(elements, frame, elementsMap): boolean` — L122-141. True if the common bounds of `elements` are fully inside the frame's absolute coords. Pure AABB containment.
- `elementOverlapsWithFrame(element, frame, elementsMap): boolean` — L143-153. OR of in-bounds, intersecting, or containing.
- `isCursorInFrame(cursorCoords, frame, elementsMap): boolean` — L155-170. Point-in-AABB test of cursor against the frame's absolute coords (`isPointWithinBounds`).
- `groupsAreAtLeastIntersectingTheFrame(elements, groupIds, frame)` — L172-191. True if no group elements, or any group element is in-bounds/intersecting.
- `groupsAreCompletelyOutOfFrame(elements, groupIds, frame)` — L193-214. True if no group elements, or none are in-bounds/intersecting.
- `groupByFrameLikes(elements): Map<id, ExcalidrawElement[]>` — L221-235. Maps each frameId to its children (includes empty frames).
- `getFrameChildren(allElements: ElementsMapOrArray, frameId): ExcalidrawElement[]` — L237-248. Linear collection of elements whose `frameId` matches.
- `getFrameLikeElements(allElements): ExcalidrawFrameLikeElement[]` — L250-256. Type-guarded filter.
- `getRootElements(allElements)` — L266-276. Returns frames plus elements that are frameless or whose frame parent doesn't exist in the set.
- `getElementsInResizingFrame(allElements, frame, appState, elementsMap): ExcalidrawElement[]` — L278-370. Recomputes frame membership during resize. Starts from current children, unions completely-in-frame elements and frame-containing children; removes ungrouped children no longer intersecting; preserves grouped children if any group member stays in the frame (`groupsToKeep` set); re-adds fully-contained individuals/groups via `selectGroupsFromGivenElements`. Filters out bound text (excludes `isTextElement && containerId`). Complex group-cohesion logic across L301-365.
- `getElementsInNewFrame(elements, frame, elementsMap)` — L372-385. Completely-in-frame elements minus partial groups (`omitPartialGroups` ∘ `omitGroupsContainingFrameLikes`).
- `omitPartialGroups(elements, frame, allElementsMap): ExcalidrawElement[]` — L387-426. Drops elements whose group is not fully within the frame; memoizes per-group decision in `checkedGroups` so all members of an omitted group are dropped consistently.
- `getContainingFrame(element, elementsMap): ExcalidrawFrameLikeElement | null` — L428-437. Returns the element's frame from the map, or null.
- `filterElementsEligibleAsFrameChildren(elements, frame): ExcalidrawElement[]` — L442-493. Returns elements that could become children of `frame`: excludes frames and other frames' children; for grouped elements, includes the whole group if any member overlaps the frame (processed once per shallowest group id); for ungrouped, includes if it overlaps. `shallowestGroupId = element.groupIds.at(-1)`.
- `getCommonFrameId(elements): frameId | null` — L495-511. Returns the single shared frameId of all elements, or null if any is a frame, frameless, or differs.
- `getFrameChildrenInsertionIndex(elements, frameId): number | null` — L513-528. Scans from the top; returns the frame's own index, or one past the highest child, else null. Z-order placement helper.
- `addElementsToFrame<T extends ElementsMapOrArray>(allElements, elementsToAdd, frame): T` — L536-631. Adds elements (and their bound text) to a frame and reorders them just below the frame (or above its highest child). Skips frames/other-frame children and elements already in a different frame; mutates `frameId`; rebuilds order via `getFrameChildrenInsertionIndex` then `syncMovedIndices`. Early-returns unchanged if nothing to add or all already belong (`commonFrameId === frame.id`, the in-frame-drag case). Returns array or Map matching input type.
- `removeElementsFromFrame(elementsToRemove: ReadonlySetLike, elementsMap)` — L633-669. Sets `frameId = null` on each element and its bound text (grouped by frame for bookkeeping). Side effect: `mutateElement`.
- `removeAllElementsFromFrame<T>(allElements, frame): readonly T[]` — L671-678. Removes all of a frame's children.
- `replaceAllElementsInFrame<T>(allElements, nextElementsInFrame, frame): T[]` — L680-690. Remove-all then add-back; returns a sliced copy.
- `updateFrameMembershipOfSelectedElements<T>(allElements, appState, app): T` — L693-737. Recomputes membership for selected elements (expanding to group members when an editing group is active); removes those no longer `isElementInFrame`. Does not return new elements (mutates via `removeElementsFromFrame`).
- `omitGroupsContainingFrameLikes(allElements, selectedElements?)` — L743-780. Filters out any element whose top-most group anywhere contains a frame-like element. Builds `rejectedGroupIds` once for perf; optional `selectedElements` narrows what is filtered.
- `getTargetFrame(element, elementsMap, appState): ExcalidrawFrameLikeElement | null` — L786-809. Resolves the frame an element is being added to/removed from. For text, uses its container. If element and its frame are both selected → the containing frame; if selected and being dragged → `appState.frameToHighlight`; else the containing frame.
- `isElementInFrame(element, allElementsMap, appState, opts?): boolean` — L813-905. Determines if an element is (still) in a frame. Marked "huge bottleneck for large scenes" (L811). Returns true early when membership won't change (not selected, not dragging, or both element+frame selected). For ungrouped → `elementOverlapsWithFrame`. For grouped → consults/populates `opts.checkedGroups` cache, gathers all group members, handles editing-group-drag specially (returns true if group overlaps highlight, removes selected from consideration), returns false if any group member is frame-like, true if any overlaps the frame. Side effect: writes `checkedGroups`.
- `shouldApplyFrameClip(element, frame, appState, elementsMap, checkedGroups?): boolean` — L907-965. Decides whether to clip an element to a frame. False if frame clipping disabled. Clips individuals that intersect or contain the frame; for grouped elements outside the frame, clips if a group member is "in" the frame (uses static membership `element.frameId === frame.id` when not dragging, else `isElementInFrame`). Populates `checkedGroups`.
- Constants `DEFAULT_FRAME_NAME = "Frame"`, `DEFAULT_AI_FRAME_NAME = "AI Frame"` — L967-968.
- `getDefaultFrameName(element)` — L970-973; `getFrameLikeTitle(element)` — L975-977. Frame-element → "Frame", else "AI Frame"; title falls back to default when `name === null`.
- `getElementsOverlappingFrame(elements, frame, elementsMap)` — L979-994. Elements whose AABB intersects the frame's AABB (`doBoundsIntersect`), excluding elements bound to a different frame.
- `frameAndChildrenSelectedTogether(selectedElements): boolean` — L996-1007. True if >1 selected and some selected element's frame is also selected.

---

### packages/element/src/elbowArrow.ts

Purpose: The full elbow-arrow engine — converts endpoint/segment/binding updates into orthogonal (right-angle) routed point sequences using an A* search over a non-uniform grid built from element bounding boxes; also handles fixed-segment editing, renormalization, and release.

Internal types (L69-108): `GridAddress = [number, number] & {_brand}`; `Node` (A* node: `f,g,h,closed,visited,parent,pos: GlobalPoint,addr: GridAddress`); `Grid` (`row,col,data:(Node|null)[]`); `ElbowArrowState`; `ElbowArrowData` (routing inputs: `dynamicAABBs`, dongle positions, global points, headings, `commonBounds`, hovered start/end elements).

Constants: `DEDUP_TRESHOLD = 1` (L110); `BASE_PADDING = 40` (exported, L111); `MAX_POS = 1e6` (L902).

- `handleSegmentRenormalization(arrow, elementsMap)` — L113-280 (internal). Merges colinear consecutive segments and removes too-short segments while keeping `fixedSegments` indices consistent. Walks points in global space; when two consecutive segments share a heading (`compareHeading`) it removes the duplicate point and updates fixed-segment indices (L137-176); then removes segments shorter than `DEDUP_TRESHOLD`, collapsing two points (L180-230). If no fixed segments survive, fully reroutes via `routeElbowArrow`; otherwise returns normalized update. DEV-asserts `validateElbowPoints`.
- `handleSegmentRelease(arrow, fixedSegments, elementsMap)` — L282-460 (internal). Triggered when a fixed segment is deleted. Reconstructs the sub-path between the previous and next fixed segments by rerouting (`getElbowArrowData` → `routeElbowArrow`), stitches old-prefix + restored + old-suffix points, re-indexes remaining fixed segments accounting for the segment-count delta (L405-420), then simplifies colinear/反 segments (flatMap at L422-452 emits `[]` to drop, `[p,p]` to split). Throws if restored points < 2.
- `handleSegmentMove(arrow, fixedSegments, startHeading, endHeading, hoveredStartElement, hoveredEndElement): ElementUpdate` — L465-704 (internal). Handles dragging a single fixed (middle) segment. Identifies the actively-modified segment via an XOR test on which axis changed (L473-491). Adds padding when moving the first/last segment against a bound element (`BASE_PADDING` or half segment length when too short, L504-558). Works in global coords: overrides the moved segment's points and aligns neighboring fixed segments; prepends/appends extra points when the move involves the very first/last segment (shifting all fixed-segment indices). Returns normalized update with `startIsSpecial/endIsSpecial = false`.
- `handleEndpointDrag(arrow, updatedPoints, fixedSegments, startHeading, endHeading, startGlobalPoint, endGlobalPoint, hoveredStartElement, hoveredEndElement): ElementUpdate` — L706-900 (internal). When endpoints move but segment count must be preserved. Tracks `startIsSpecial`/`endIsSpecial` flags (extra padding points injected near bound elements). Rebuilds the point list: copies interior points, computes the second/second-to-last connection points (orthogonal projection onto heading), conditionally inserting `BASE_PADDING` dongle pairs when the heading matches the adjacent segment's orientation and an element is hovered. Re-derives fixed-segment start/end from the new point indices. Returns normalized update.
- `updateElbowArrowPoints(arrow, elementsMap, updates: {points?, fixedSegments?, startBinding?, endBinding?}, options?: {isDragging?, isBindingEnabled?, isMidpointSnappingEnabled?}): ElementUpdate<ExcalidrawElbowArrowElement>` — L907-1167 (EXPORTED, the public entry point). Returns early if <2 points. In non-PROD, asserts a battery of invariants (point count, segments horizontal/vertical, first/last segments not fixed — L926-977). Builds `updatedPoints` (when exactly 2 update points, replaces only start/end; L981-991). Resolves bindings/elements, then dispatches: (0/1) renormalize-only when no updates (L1057-1081); short-circuit no-op (L1084-1096); (2) no fixed segments → full reroute (`routeElbowArrow`) (L1101-1120); (3) fixed-segment release (L1124-1126); (4) manual segment move (L1131-1140); (5) resize passthrough (L1145-1147); (6) endpoint drag with fixed segments (L1156-1166). This is the orchestration core; the whole pipeline is `routeElbowArrow → removeElbowArrowShortSegments → getElbowArrowCornerPoints → normalizeArrowElementUpdate`.
- `getElbowArrowData(arrow, elementsMap, nextPoints, options?)` — L1192-1428 (internal). Computes everything routing needs: global start/end points (`getGlobalPoint`, honoring binding fixed points and drag snapping), hovered elements (during drag via `getHoveredElement`, else from bindings), start/end headings (`getBindPointHeading`), element AABBs offset by binding gap (`getBindingGap * 2` or `*6` when arrowheads present, L1310-1335), a `boundsOverlap` check (whether endpoints sit inside each other's padded AABB, L1336-1356), `commonBounds` (`commonAABB`), `dynamicAABBs` (`generateDynamicAABBs`), and dongle positions. Returns the `ElbowArrowData` plus `boundsOverlap`, `startElementBounds`, `endElementBounds`.
- `routeElbowArrow(arrow: ElbowArrowState, elbowArrowData: ElbowArrowData): GlobalPoint[] | null` — L1439-1507 (internal). Builds the grid (`calculateGrid`), maps dongle/start/end points to grid nodes, closes the true start/end nodes so the path can't step on them, then runs `astar`. On success maps node positions to points and re-attaches the real start/end global points around the dongles.
- `offsetFromHeading(heading, head, side): [number,number,number,number]` — L1509-1524 (internal). Returns a per-side offset tuple `[up,right,down,side...]` placing the larger `head` offset on the heading's side.
- `astar(start, end, grid, startHeading, endHeading, aabbs): Node[] | null` — L1537-1646 (internal). A* with a `BinaryHeap` keyed on `f`. Key non-standard cost shaping: `bendMultiplier = m_dist(start,end)` (Manhattan distance); each direction change adds `bendMultiplier^3` to g (L1611-1615); the heuristic adds `estimateSegmentCount * bendMultiplier^2` to h (L1620-1631) — so the search aggressively prefers fewer bends. Rejects neighbors whose half-point lies inside any AABB (L1578-1590) and rejects reverse-direction moves and re-entering start/end against their headings (L1599-1609). Uses `rescoreElement` when re-scoring a visited node.
- `pathTo(start, node): Node[]` — L1648-1658 (internal). Reconstructs path by walking `parent` pointers.
- `m_dist(a, b): number` — L1660-1661 (internal). Manhattan distance `|dx|+|dy|`.
- `generateDynamicAABBs(a, b, common, startDifference?, endDifference?, disableSideHack?, startElementBounds?, endElementBounds?): Bounds[]` — L1668-1844 (internal). Produces two always-touching dynamic bounding boxes (one per endpoint) with minimum extents, used as A* obstacles/lanes. Heavy branching geometry computes each box edge by comparing the two element boxes' relative positions and splitting the gap at the midpoint or applying the directional padding (L1685-1744). The "side hack" (L1746-1841) detects diagonal corner configurations (bottom-left/top-left/top-right/bottom-right via `vectorCross` sign) and splits the two boxes along a computed corner X or Y so the route bends cleanly around the corner. Non-obvious computational geometry — central to elbow aesthetics.
- `calculateGrid(aabbs, start, startHeading, end, endHeading, common): Grid` — L1853-1908 (internal). Builds a NON-uniform grid from the set of distinct horizontal/vertical coordinates contributed by the AABB edges, the common bounds, and the start/end coordinates (axis chosen by heading orientation). Sorts coords, then materializes a `row*col` array of `Node`s at each intersection. This is why pathfinding is cheap — only meaningful grid lines exist.
- `getDonglePosition(bounds, heading, p): GlobalPoint` — L1910-1924 (internal). Projects point `p` onto the bound edge indicated by heading (the "dongle" where the arrow exits the element padding box).
- `estimateSegmentCount(start, end, startHeading, endHeading): number` — L1926-2039 (internal). The A* heuristic's bend estimator: a large switch on (endHeading × startHeading) returning 0/1/2/3/4 expected segments based on relative node positions. Drives the `bendMultiplier^2` heuristic term.
- `getNeighbors([col,row], grid): [Node|null × 4]` — L2044-2050 (internal). Up/right/down/left neighbors (order matters: index→heading mapping).
- `gridNodeFromAddr([col,row], grid): Node | null` — L2052-2061 (internal). Bounds-checked lookup into `grid.data[row*col + col]`.
- `pointToGridNode(point, grid): Node | null` — L2066-2081 (internal). Linear scan for the grid node whose pos exactly equals `point`.
- `commonAABB(aabbs): Bounds` — L2083-2088 (internal). Min/min/max/max union of bounds.
- `getBindableElementForId(id, elementsMap): ExcalidrawBindableElement | null` — L2092-2102 (internal). Map lookup guarded by `isBindableElement`.
- `normalizeArrowElementUpdate(global: GlobalPoint[], nextFixedSegments, startIsSpecial?, endIsSpecial?): ElementUpdate` — L2104-2156 (internal). Re-bases global points to the first point's origin (so points become local relative to a new `x,y`), clamps offsets/points to ±`MAX_POS` (1e6), recomputes width/height via `getSizeFromPoints`, and nulls out empty fixedSegments. Logs an error if the arrow exceeds reasonable bounds. Output is the canonical element update shape.
- `getElbowArrowCornerPoints(points): GlobalPoint[]` — L2158-2184 (internal). Keeps only corner points — drops a point when the orientation (horizontal vs vertical, decided by which delta is larger) doesn't change across it. Always keeps endpoints.
- `removeElbowArrowShortSegments(points): GlobalPoint[]` — L2186-2202 (internal). For ≥4 points, drops interior points within `DEDUP_TRESHOLD` of their predecessor.
- `neighborIndexToHeading(idx): Heading` — L2204-2214 (internal). 0→UP,1→RIGHT,2→DOWN,else LEFT (matches `getNeighbors` order).
- `getGlobalPoint(arrow, startOrEnd, fixedPointRatio, initialPoint, element?, elementsMap?, isDragging?, isBindingEnabled=true, isMidpointSnappingEnabled=true): GlobalPoint` — L2216-2251 (internal). During drag with binding enabled, snaps to element outline (`bindPointToSnapToElementOutline`); when bound, returns the fixed point on the element (`getGlobalFixedPointForBindableElement`); else returns the raw point.
- `getBindPointHeading(p, otherPoint, hoveredElement, origPoint, elementsMap, zoom?): Heading` — L2253-2279 (internal). Delegates to `getHeadingForElbowArrowSnap`, computing the element AABB padded on all four sides by `distanceToElement`.
- `getHoveredElement(origPoint, elementsMap, elements, zoom?)` — L2281-2293 (internal). Wraps `getHoveredElementForBinding` with `maxBindingDistance_simple(zoom)`.
- `gridAddressesEqual(a, b): boolean` — L2295-2296 (internal). Component-wise equality.
- `validateElbowPoints<P extends GlobalPoint|LocalPoint>(points, tolerance = DEDUP_TRESHOLD): boolean` — L2298-2310 (EXPORTED). True iff every consecutive pair shares an axis within `tolerance` (i.e. each segment is orthogonal). Used as an invariant guard throughout.

Coordinate-space note (whole file): the engine repeatedly converts between LOCAL points (relative to `arrow.x,arrow.y`) and GLOBAL points. Routing, AABB math, and A* all operate in global space; `normalizeArrowElementUpdate` is the single place that re-localizes points and recomputes `x/y/width/height`. Parity reimplementations must preserve the bend-cost shaping (`^3` g-penalty, `^2` heuristic) and the non-uniform grid construction to reproduce route choices.


---

## Cluster: element__src__3

This cluster covers element grouping, arrow heading/cone geometry, image element helpers, the package barrel + scene-version hashing, the (very large) linear-element editing state machine, element mutation primitives, and the element constructor factories.

---

### packages/element/src/groups.ts

Group selection logic: computing which group IDs to select when elements are selected, mapping groups↔elements, and group membership predicates. Heavily memoized for the interactive canvas hot path.

- **`selectGroup(groupId, appState, elements)` → `Pick<InteractiveCanvasAppState, "selectedGroupIds" | "selectedElementIds" | "editingGroupId">`** (L23-L63). Builds a `Record<id,true>` of all elements whose `groupIds` include `groupId`. If fewer than 2 members, the group is not a real group: if it was selected/editing it returns it cleared (`[groupId]: false`, `editingGroupId: null`), else returns appState unchanged. Otherwise returns the group marked selected plus all member element IDs merged into `selectedElementIds`. Pure.
- **`selectGroupsForSelectedElements` IIFE** (L65-L208). Closure with memo cache fields `lastSelectedElements`, `lastElements`, `lastReturnValue`.
  - **inner `_selectGroups(selectedElements, elements, appState, prevAppState)` → `SelectGroupsReturnType`** (L76-L158). Memo short-circuit when `elements`, `selectedElements`, and `editingGroupId` are reference-equal to last call (L82-L89). Otherwise: for each selected element, take the *last* (outermost-but-respecting-edit) group id — if `editingGroupId` is in the element's `groupIds`, slice the array before that index so nested editing only selects the inner group (L93-L106). Then walks all non-deleted elements building `selectedElementIdsInGroups` and a `groupElementsIndex` (groupId→element ids) (L109-L131). Singleton groups (`< 2` members) get unselected (L133-L140). Result wraps `selectedElementIds` through `makeNextSelectedElementIds` for referential-equality preservation (L148-L154). Caches and returns.
  - **outer `selectGroupsForSelectedElements(appState, elements, prevAppState, app)`** (L164-L199). Resolves selected elements via `app.scene.getSelectedElements` (preferred, optimized) or fallback `getSelectedElements`. Empty → returns cleared groups/editing + `makeNextSelectedElementIds`. Otherwise delegates to `_selectGroups`.
  - **`.clearCache()`** (L201-L205) nulls the three memo fields. Invariant: cache must be cleared when scene mutates outside reference tracking.
- **`isSelectedViaGroup(appState, element)`** (L214-L217). True if `getSelectedGroupForElement` returns non-null — used to suppress per-element selection borders when its group is selected.
- **`getSelectedGroupForElement(appState, element)`** (L219-L228). Filters out the `editingGroupId`, then finds the first of the element's `groupIds` present in `selectedGroupIds`.
- **`getSelectedGroupIds(appState)` → `GroupId[]`** (L230-L235). Entries of `selectedGroupIds` filtered to `isSelected===true`.
- **`selectGroupsFromGivenElements(elements, appState)`** (L239-L266). Reduces over elements, taking each element's outermost group (slicing before `editingGroupId`), and folds `selectGroup` results into a running `nextAppState`. Returns the resulting `selectedGroupIds`.
- **`editGroupForSelectedElement(appState, element)` → `AppState`** (L268-L280). Enters edit mode for the element's first (innermost) group: sets `editingGroupId` to `groupIds[0]`, clears `selectedGroupIds`, selects only that element.
- **`isElementInGroup(element, groupId)`** (L282-L283). `groupIds.includes`.
- **`getElementsInGroup(elements, groupId)`** (L285-L296). Iterates `elements.values()` (works on Map or array), collects members.
- **`getSelectedGroupIdForElement(element, selectedGroupIds)`** (L298-L301). First of element's `groupIds` that is selected.
- **`addToGroup(prevGroupIds, newGroupId, editingGroupId)`** (L303-L317). Inserts `newGroupId` *before* `editingGroupId` (so the new group nests outside the editing group) or pushes to end. Invariant: group order = inner→outer.
- **`removeFromSelectedGroups(groupIds, selectedGroupIds)`** (L319-L322). Filters out any selected group ids.
- **`getMaximumGroups(elements, elementsMap)` → `ExcalidrawElement[][]`** (L324-L349). Buckets elements by their outermost group id (or own id if ungrouped); each bucket also includes the element's bound-text element (via `getBoundTextElement`). Returns array of "maximal groups". Note: ungrouped elements become singleton groups keyed by id; `Map<String,...>` uses boxed `String` keys (cosmetic).
- **`getNonDeletedGroupIds(elements)` → `Set<string>`** (L351-L367). Collects all group ids across non-deleted elements; defensive `?? []` fallback on `groupIds`.
- **`elementsAreInSameGroup(elements)`** (L369-L384). Flattens all `groupIds`, counts occurrences; true iff some single group id occurs in *every* element (`maxGroup === elements.length`).
- **`isInGroup(element)`** (L386-L388). `groupIds.length > 0`.
- **`getNewGroupIdsForDuplication(groupIds, editingGroupId, mapper)`** (L390-L406). Remaps group ids for the indices *before* `editingGroupId` (or all if not editing) via `mapper` — so duplication generates fresh inner group ids while keeping the outer editing group shared.
- **`getSelectedElementsByGroup(selectedElements, elementsMap, appState)` → `ExcalidrawElement[][]`** (L410-L481). Buckets selected (non-bound-to-container) elements into per-group arrays and per-element arrays. Helpers `addToElementsMap` (L423-L432) and `addToGroupsMap` (L435-L444) both append bound text. `handleSingleSelectedGroupCase` (L449-L461) respects nested grouping order when exactly one group is selected and all elements are in it: if the element has groups nested inside the selected one, it buckets by the next-inner group id, else treats as individual. Returns `groups.values()` concatenated with `elements.values()`.

---

### packages/element/src/heading.ts

Computes a cardinal "heading" (one of 4 unit vectors) from a point relative to a bindable element's bounding box — core geometry for elbow-arrow routing and binding focus. No coordinate-space conversion (operates in world/global space).

- **Constants** `HEADING_RIGHT [1,0]`, `HEADING_DOWN [0,1]`, `HEADING_LEFT [-1,0]`, `HEADING_UP [0,-1]` and `type Heading` union (L31-L35). NOTE: y-down screen convention — DOWN is `[0,1]`.
- **`vectorToHeading(vec)` → `Heading`** (L37-L49). Snaps an arbitrary vector to the nearest of 4 cardinal directions by comparing signed components against `|x|`/`|y|`: `x > |y|`→RIGHT, `x <= -|y|`→LEFT, `y > |x|`→DOWN, else UP. Quadrant decision via abs comparison (diagonal ties resolve UP/LEFT).
- **`headingForPoint<P>(p, o)`** (L51-L54). `vectorToHeading(vectorFromPoint(p, o))` — heading of `p` as seen from origin `o`.
- **`headingForPointIsHorizontal<P>(p, o)`** (L56-L59). Convenience: heading is horizontal.
- **`compareHeading(a, b)`** (L61-L62). Component equality.
- **`headingIsHorizontal(a)`** (L64-L65). Equals RIGHT or LEFT.
- **`headingIsVertical(a)`** (L67). Negation of horizontal.
- **`headingForPointFromDiamondElement(element, aabb, point)` → `Heading`** (internal, L69-L226). Diamond-specific heading. Computes midpoint via `getCenterForBounds`. In dev/test, invariants assert non-zero size and that `point` ≠ midpoint (L76-L85). Builds the 4 diamond vertex midpoints (top/right/bottom/left), each rotated by `element.angle` about midpoint then scaled toward midpoint by `SHRINK=0.95` (rounded-corner tolerance) (L87-L149). Uses **vector cross products** to test which "corner" or "side" sector `point` falls into; for side sectors it picks top/right/bottom/left depending on whether the diamond is wider than tall (`element.width > element.height`) (L151-L225). Heavy 2D geometry — important for parity.
- **`headingForPointFromElement<Point>(element, aabb, p)` → `Heading`** (L231-L281). General (non-diamond) case. `SEARCH_CONE_MULTIPLIER = 2`. Delegates to diamond variant for diamonds (L240-L242). Otherwise scales the 4 aabb corners outward from midpoint by 2× (`pointScaleFromOrigin`) to form 4 large triangular "search cones" sharing the midpoint, then uses `triangleIncludesPoint` to classify `p` into UP/RIGHT/DOWN/LEFT (L244-L280). Comment (L228-L230) documents the cone algorithm. Coordinate-space: global points; `aabb` is the close-fitting (possibly rotated-aware) bounds.
- **`flipHeading(h)` → `Heading`** (L283-L287). Negates each non-zero component.

---

### packages/element/src/image.ts

Helpers for `ExcalidrawImageElement`: async image loading into a cache, SVG normalization, and type-narrowing collectors.

- **`loadHTMLImageElement(dataURL)` → `Promise<HTMLImageElement>`** (L21-L32). Wraps `new Image()` with onload/onerror; resolves the decoded image, rejects on error. Side effect: sets `image.src`.
- **`updateImageCache({ fileIds, files, imageCache })` → `Promise<{ imageCache, updatedFiles, erroredFiles }>`** (L36-L89). For each unique fileId with file data, stores an in-progress entry `{ image: <promise>, mimeType }` immediately (L67) so concurrent callers see initialization underway, then awaits and overwrites with the resolved `image` (L69-L71). `MIME_TYPES.binary` throws "Only images…" (L56-L58). Errors are caught and recorded in `erroredFiles` (cache still considered updated). Returns the (mutated) `imageCache`, `updatedFiles`, `erroredFiles`. NOTE in header (L34-L35): always overwrites cache even if populated — filter upstream to optimize. Invariant: `imageCache` is mutated in place.
- **`getInitializedImageElements(elements)` → `InitializedExcalidrawImageElement[]`** (L91-L96). Filters via `isInitializedImageElement`.
- **`isHTMLSVGElement(node)` → `node is SVGElement`** (L98-L102). Case-insensitive `nodeName === "svg"` test (XML/HTML case sensitivity note).
- **`normalizeSVG(SVGString)` → `string`** (L104-L153). Parses SVG via `DOMParser`; throws "Invalid SVG" on `parsererror` or missing `<svg>` (L107-L109). Ensures `xmlns` (L111-L113). Strips `%`/`auto` width/height to avoid scaling issues at different zooms (L115-L125). If width/height missing, defaults to 50 or extracts from `viewBox` via regex `/\d+ +\d+ +(\d+(?:\.\d+)?) +(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)/` (the 3rd/4th numbers) (L129-L144). Ensures a `viewBox` exists (L147-L149). Returns `svg.outerHTML`.

---

### packages/element/src/index.ts

Barrel/re-export module for the `@excalidraw/element` package, plus a few scene-version/hash utilities defined inline.

- **`getSceneVersion(elements)`** (L15-L16). `@deprecated` — sums all `element.version`. Marked unsafe (collisions); use `hashElementsVersion`.
- **`hashElementsVersion(elements)` → `number`** (L21-L27). djb2-style hash (`hash = (hash<<5)+hash + versionNonce`, seed 5381) over each element's `versionNonce`; order-sensitive; coerced to unsigned 32-bit via `>>> 0`. Accepts Map or array via `toIterable`.
- **`hashString(s)` → `number`** (L34-L41). djb2 over `charCodeAt` code units (not code points — acceptable for versioning). Unsigned 32-bit.
- **`getVisibleElements(elements)`** (L43-L46). Filters non-deleted and not `isInvisiblySmallElement`.
- **`getNonDeletedElements<T>(elements)`** (L48-L51). Filters `!isDeleted`.
- **`isNonDeletedElement<T>(element)` → `element is NonDeleted<T>`** (L53-L55). Type guard.
- **Re-exports (L57-L102):** `export *` from ~40 sibling modules (align, binding, bounds, collision, comparisons, containerCache, cropElement, delta, distance, distribute, dragElements, duplicate, elbowArrow, elementLink, embeddable, flowchart, arrows/focus, fractionalIndex, frame, groups, heading, image, linearElementEditor, mutateElement, newElement, positionElementsOnGrid, renderElement, resizeElements, resizeTest, Scene, selection, shape, showSelectedShapeActions, sizeHelpers, sortElements, store, textElement, textMeasurements, textWrapping, transform, transformHandles, typeChecks, utils, zindex, arrows/helpers, arrowheads).

---

### packages/element/src/linearElementEditor.ts

The linear-element (line/arrow/elbow-arrow) interactive editing state machine: a `LinearElementEditor` value class holding per-edit-session state, plus a large set of static methods that mutate elements during point drag/add/delete, midpoint insertion, binding, and bounding-box computation. The single most geometry-heavy file in the cluster (~2500 lines). All point math is local↔global with element rotation about its bbox center.

Module-private:
- **`getNormalizedPoints({ points })` → `{ points: LocalPoint[]; offsetX; offsetY }`** (L97-L116). Shifts all points so `points[0]` becomes `[0,0]`; returns the removed offset. Invariant enforced across codebase: linear element first point is `[0,0]`.
- **type `PointMoveOtherUpdates`** (L118-L123): optional `startBinding`/`endBinding`/`moveMidPointsWithElement`/`suggestedBinding`.

Class `LinearElementEditor` (L125-L2111):
- **Fields** (L126-L162): `elementId` (branded), `selectedPointsIndices`, `initialState` (formerly `pointerDownState`: holds `prevSelectedPointsIndices`, `lastClickedPoint`, `origin`, `segmentMidpoint {value,index,added}`, `arrowStartIsInside`, `altFocusPoint`), `isDragging`, `lastUncommittedPoint`, `lastCommittedPoint`, `pointerOffset {x,y}`, `hoverPointIndex`, `segmentMidPointHoveredCoords`, `hoveredFocusPointBinding`, `draggedFocusPointBinding`, `elbowed`, `customLineAngle`, `isEditing`. `pointerDownState` is a deprecated `never` alias.
- **constructor(element, elementsMap, isEditing=false)** (L164-L205). Sets `elementId`; if `points[0]` is not `[0,0]` logs an error and self-heals via `mutateElement(... getNormalizeElementPointsAndCoords)` (L172-L179). Initializes all fields; `elbowed = isElbowArrow && element.elbowed`.
- **static `POINT_HANDLE_SIZE = 10`** (L211). Hit-test/handle radius in screen px (divided by `zoom.value` for world thresholds).
- **`getElement<T>(id, elementsMap)`** (L217-L226). Map lookup cast to linear element or null.
- **`handleBoxSelection(event, appState, setState, elementsMap)`** (L228-L289). When editing a linear element with an active marquee, computes which point indices fall inside the selection rect (in global coords via `getPointsGlobalCoordinates`); shift-key keeps previously selected; elbow arrows only allow endpoints (filters out interior indices, L270-L278). Calls `setState`.
- **`handlePointerMove(event, app, scenePointerX, scenePointerY, linearElementEditor)`** (L291-L440). Used during initial multi-point creation (drags the *last* point).
