Computes `customLineAngle` from pivot (L310-L312). With discrete-angle modifier uses `_getShiftLockedDelta`; else `createPointAt` minus `pointerOffset` (grid-aware unless CTRL/CMD) (L317-L343). Computes `deltaX/deltaY`, runs `pointDraggingUpdates` for the last index, applies via `movePoints` (L345-L375), derives `suggestedBinding`, raises arrow z-index over bindable via `moveArrowAboveBindable` (L383-L396). PERF guard: returns `null` (no state update) when nothing meaningfully changed (L398-L406). Builds `altFocusPoint` projection if start-binding exists (L407-L434).
- **`handlePointDragging(event, app, scenePointerX, scenePointerY, linearElementEditor)`** (L442-L678). Drags already-selected points. For elbow arrows it remaps any interior selected point to the end index (only endpoints draggable, L467-L477). Validates `lastClickedPoint`, falls back to last point on corruption (L479-L494). Computes pivot, `singlePointDragged`, `customLineAngle`, start/end-selected flags (L497-L508). Discrete-angle path only when single point dragged (L513-L538). Runs `pointDraggingUpdates` → `movePoints` (L540-L570). Derives `suggestedBinding` only if start xor end selected; raises z-index (L572-L594). Resizes bound text via `handleBindTextResize` (L596-L600). For elbow arrows recomputes selected indices to `[0]` or `[last]` (L602-L612). Computes hovered midpoint coords, alt-focus bindable, returns new editor with `isDragging:true` (L614-L677).
- **`handlePointerUp(event, editingLinearElement, appState, scene)` → `LinearElementEditor`** (L680-L769). If dragging an endpoint and the path forms a loop (`isPathALoop` within zoom tolerance), converts a line element to polygon state (`toggleLinePolygonState`) and snaps the dragged endpoint to coincide with the opposite endpoint (closes the loop) (L699-L736). Resets hover/midpoint state; computes next `selectedPointsIndices` honoring shift-toggle semantics vs. single-click-deselect (L744-L760); clears `isDragging`, `customLineAngle`, `origin`, `arrowStartIsInside`.
- **`getEditorMidPoints(element, elementsMap, appState)` → `(GlobalPoint|null)[]`** (static arrow fn, L771-L820). Returns per-segment midpoints (null where segment too short). Early-out `[]` for non-elbow, non-editing, >2-point, no-bound-text elements (L779-L786). Iterates segments, pushing `null` for short segments else `getSegmentMidPoint(index+1)`.
- **`getSegmentMidpointHitCoords(linearElementEditor, scenePointer, appState, elementsMap)` → `GlobalPoint|null`** (L822-L893). Hit-tests scene pointer against segment midpoints within `threshold=(POINT_HANDLE_SIZE+1)/zoom`. Returns null if a real point is under cursor (non-elbow) or for ≥3-point non-editing non-elbow lines. Short-circuits using already-hovered coords if still within threshold (L858-L871).
- **`isSegmentTooShort<P>(element, startPoint, endPoint, index, zoom, elementsMap)`** (L895-L934). Elbow: distance·zoom < `POINT_HANDLE_SIZE/2`. Otherwise distance·zoom < `POINT_HANDLE_SIZE*4`; for rounded multi-point lines computes true **curve arc length** via `deconstructLinearOrFreeDrawElement` + `curveLength` (asserts the element is curve-built) (L914-L931).
- **`getSegmentMidPoint(element, index, elementsMap)` → `GlobalPoint`** (L936-L978). Elbow: arithmetic center of `points[index-1]/[index]` offset by element x/y (L941-L950). Else deconstructs into lines or curves (asserts exactly one kind), returns `pointCenter` of the line segment or `curvePointAtLength(segment, 0.5)` for the curve (L952-L977).
- **`getSegmentMidPointIndex(linearElementEditor, appState, midPoint, elementsMap)`** (L980-L1006). Finds which editor midpoint equals `midPoint` (via `arePointsEqual`), returns `index+1` (segment index) or -1.
- **`handlePointerDown(event, app, store, scenePointer, linearElementEditor, scene)` → `{ didAddPoint, hitElement, linearElementEditor }`** (L1008-L1161). Determines if a segment midpoint was hit (→ records its index for possible insertion). Alt+editing with no uncommitted point adds a new point at cursor and schedules history capture (`store.scheduleCapture`) (L1056-L1096). Otherwise hit-tests a point under cursor (`getPointIndexUnderCursor`); sets `hitElement` if a point/midpoint hit (so the element isn't deselected) (L1098-L1109). Computes the rotated `targetPoint` for the clicked point and `pointerOffset` (cursor minus point, in scene coords) (L1111-L1158). Records `prevSelectedPointsIndices`, `lastClickedPoint`, `origin`, `arrowStartIsInside`.
- **`arePointsEqual<Point>(point1, point2)`** (L1163-L1174). Null-safe `pointsEqual`.
- **`handlePointerMoveInEditMode(event, scenePointerX, scenePointerY, app)` → `LinearElementEditor|null`** (L1176-L1253). During point-by-point creation. If alt not held, deletes a trailing uncommitted point and clears `lastUncommittedPoint` (L1196-L1206). With discrete angle + ≥2 points, computes locked delta from the second-to-last anchor (L1210-L1220); else `createPointAt` (grid off for elbow) (L1221-L1231). Either moves the existing uncommitted last point or `addPoints` a new one (L1233-L1248); returns editor with updated `lastUncommittedPoint`.
- **`getPointGlobalCoordinates(element, p, elementsMap)` → `GlobalPoint`** (L1255-L1271). Local point → global: adds element x/y then rotates about bbox center `(cx,cy)` by `element.angle`.
- **`getPointsGlobalCoordinates(element, elementsMap)` → `GlobalPoint[]`** (L1273-L1289). Same transform applied to all points.
- **`getPointAtIndexGlobalCoordinates(element, indexMaybeFromEnd, elementsMap)`** (L1291-L1312). Negative index counts from end. Returns rotated global coord; falls back to element origin if point missing.
- **`pointFromAbsoluteCoords(element, absoluteCoords, elementsMap)` → `LocalPoint`** (L1314-L1336). Inverse transform: elbow arrows skip rotation (just subtract x/y); others counter-rotate by `-angle` about center then subtract x/y.
- **`getPointIndexUnderCursor(element, elementsMap, zoom, x, y)`** (L1338-L1364). Iterates point handles **right→left** (right-rendered points win overlap ties), returns first within `(POINT_HANDLE_SIZE+1)` screen px (distance·zoom), else -1.
- **`createPointAt(element, elementsMap, scenePointerX, scenePointerY, gridSize)` → `LocalPoint`** (L1366-L1384). Snaps pointer to grid, counter-rotates about bbox center by `-angle`, subtracts element x/y → local point.
- **`getNormalizeElementPointsAndCoords(element)`** (L1393-L1401). Returns normalized points + adjusted `x/y` (element origin shifted by removed offset). Used by constructor self-heal.
- **`duplicateSelectedPoints(appState, scene)` → `AppState`** (L1405-L1479). For each selected point inserts a new point at the midpoint to the next point (or copy + offset for the last point). When a point is added at the end, nudges it `+30,+30` so the bbox visibly grows without shifting the line (hack noted L1456-L1457). Returns appState with the new selected indices.
- **`deletePoints(element, app, pointIndices)`** (L1481-L1523). Filters out indices; if polygon and deleting start/end/uncommitted, re-pins `points[0]` to the (new) last point to keep the polygon closed (L1495-L1508). Re-normalizes then `_updatePoints`.
- **`addPoints(element, scene, addedPoints)`** (L1525-L1552). Appends points; polygon keeps `points[0]` pinned to last; normalize → `_updatePoints`.
- **`movePoints(element, scene, pointUpdates, otherUpdates?, options?)`** (L1554-L1639). Core point-move. For polygons, mirrors first↔last point update so they stay coincident (L1571-L1589). Handles the "move start point" invariant: moving point 0 would break the `[0,0]` invariant, so it offsets all other points (and element x/y) by the negative delta, transparently (comment L1591-L1596). Elbow arrows only keep the 2 endpoints (L1602-L1607); others apply per-index updates with optional `moveMidPointsWithElement` to leave interior points untouched. Delegates to `_updatePoints` with `isDragging`/binding flags.
- **`shouldAddMidpoint(linearElementEditor, pointerCoords, appState, elementsMap)`** (L1641-L1684). False for elbow arrows or missing element. Requires an un-added segment midpoint with a valid `origin`; when not editing, requires drag distance ≥ `DRAGGING_THRESHOLD/zoom` before allowing midpoint creation.
- **`addMidpoint(linearElementEditor, pointerCoords, app, snapToGrid, scene)`** (L1686-L1735). Inserts a new point at `segmentMidpoint.index`, mutates element, returns updated `pointerDownState` (`segmentMidpoint.added=true`, `lastClickedPoint=index`) and selects the new point.
- **private `_updatePoints(element, scene, nextPoints, offsetX, offsetY, otherUpdates?, options?)`** (L1737-L1798). The single mutation funnel. Elbow arrows: just set `points` + bindings via `scene.mutateElement` with dragging/binding flags (L1755-L1775). Non-elbow: recomputes the bbox center delta between old and new point coords (`getElementPointsCoords`), rotates `(offsetX,offsetY)` about the center-delta by `element.angle`, and shifts element `x/y` so the element doesn't visually jump when point 0 moves (L1777-L1796). Notable rotation/center math for parity.
- **private `_getShiftLockedDelta(element, elementsMap, referencePoint, scenePointer, gridSize, customLineAngle?)`** (L1800-L1840). Computes the angle-locked delta from a reference point to the (grid-snapped) pointer. Elbow: raw delta. Else `getLockedLinearCursorAlignSize` (snaps to discrete angle), then counter-rotates by `-angle`.
- **`getBoundTextElementPosition(element, boundTextElement, elementsMap)` → `{x,y}`** (L1842-L1877). Positions bound text at the geometric middle of the polyline: if odd point count uses the middle *vertex*, if even uses the middle *segment midpoint*; centers the text box on it. Deletes bound text if <2 points (L1851-L1853).
- **`getMinMaxXYWithBoundText(element, elementsMap, elementBounds, boundTextElement)` → `[x1,y1,x2,y2,cx,cy]`** (L1879-L1981). Expands the element AABB to include the rotated bound-text box. Rotates the text-box corners by `-angle` (counter-rotate into the element's unrotated frame) and, based on which orientation quadrant the rotated top edge falls in, mins/maxes the appropriate corners into the bounds. Dense rotation-quadrant geometry — important for parity.
- **`getElementAbsoluteCoords(element, elementsMap, includeBoundText=false)`** (L1983-L2013). Generates the element's RoughJS-style shape via `ShapeCache.generateElementShape`, takes the first (curve) path, extracts min/max from its path ops (`getMinMaxXYFromCurvePathOps`), offsets by element x/y → AABB + center. If `includeBoundText` and bound text exists, expands via `getMinMaxXYWithBoundText`. NOTE: bounds derive from the actual rendered curve, not raw points.
- **`moveFixedSegment(linearElement, index, x, y, scene)`** (L2015-L2098). Elbow-arrow only. Determines if the segment at `index` is horizontal; updates/creates a `FixedSegment` whose start/end pin one axis to the dragged coordinate and the other to the existing point (L2035-L2059); re-sorts fixed segments by index; mutates element; returns updated hovered-midpoint state pointing at the new segment center.
- **`deleteFixedSegment(element, scene, index)`** (L2100-L2110). Removes the fixed segment with matching `index`.

Module-private helpers after the class:
- **`normalizeSelectedPoints(points)` → `number[]|null`** (L2113-L2121). Dedupe (Set), drop null/-1, sort ascending; null if empty.
- **`pointDraggingUpdates(...)` → `{ positions: PointsPositionUpdates; updates?: PointMoveOtherUpdates }`** (L2123-L2501). The binding-aware point-move computation. Builds `naiveDraggingPoints` (each selected point + delta, `isDragging:true`), tolerating stale indices via `?? points.at(-1)` (L2140-L2153). Plain lines short-circuit (L2156-L2160). For arrows: computes start/end binding strategy via `getBindingStrategyForDraggingBindingElementEndpoints` (L2167-L2180). Elbow arrows return naive positions + a `suggestedBinding` (optionally snapped to mid) (L2182-L2209). When neither endpoint dragged but bound, recomputes bound endpoints via `updateBoundPoint` (L2213-L2266). When both/neither dragged equally, returns naive (L2268-L2272). The main path computes next `startBinding`/`endBinding` (mode null=unbind, focus point via `calculateFixedPointForNonElbowArrowBinding`), `suggestedBinding` (orbit mode or no COMPLEX_BINDINGS flag → outline-snap midpoint) (L2274-L2354). Builds a simulated `nextArrow`, handles the special case of an arrow dragged so both ends bind to the *same* element (`startIsDraggingOverEndElement`/`endIsDraggingOverStartElement`, gated by `COMPLEX_BINDINGS` flag and `bindMode`) (L2356-L2458), recomputes the non-dragged bound endpoint, and emits only the changed point indices in `positions` (L2460-L2500). The densest binding/geometry logic in the file.
- **`determineCustomLinearAngle(pivotPoint, draggedPoint)`** (L2503-L2507). `Math.atan2(dy, dx)` — the angle of the dragged segment for shift-lock baseline.

---

### packages/element/src/mutateElement.ts

Low-level element mutation primitives that bump version metadata for collaboration; does NOT trigger React re-render (use `scene.mutateElement` for that).

- **type `ElementUpdate<TElement>`** (L24-L27). `Omit<Partial<TElement>, "id"|"updated">`.
- **`mutateElement<TElement>(element, elementsMap, updates, options?)` → `TElement`** (L37-L144). In-place mutation. Elbow arrows: when updates are empty (normalization), or `points`/`fixedSegments` change, recomputes routing via `updateElbowArrowPoints` and forces `angle:0` (L53-L72). Non-elbow with `points`: derives width/height via `getSizeFromPoints` (L73-L75). Then per-key apply with change detection: skips equal primitives; for objects always updates *except* `groupIds`/`scale` special cases (L80-L97); for `points` does element-wise comparison (loops from the end via `while(--index)`, NOTE: index 0 not compared) and skips if unchanged (L98-L119). Sets `didChange`; if nothing changed returns element unmutated (L126-L128). Invalidates `ShapeCache` when width/height/fileId/points changed (L130-L137). Bumps `version` (`updates.version ?? +1`), `versionNonce` (`randomInteger()`), `updated` timestamp (L139-L141). Side effects: mutates `element`, mutates `ShapeCache`.
- **`newElementWith<TElement>(element, updates, force=false)` → `TElement`** (L146-L178). Immutable variant: returns a *new* object with updates applied; same change detection (objects always change unless null). Returns same reference if nothing changed and not forced. Always bumps version/versionNonce/updated on a real change.
- **`bumpVersion<T>(element, version?)`** (L185-L193). In-place: `version = (version ?? element.version)+1`, new `versionNonce`, new `updated`. Returns element.

---

### packages/element/src/newElement.ts

Element constructor factory functions producing fully-initialized `ExcalidrawElement` variants, plus text dimension/position adjustment helpers.

- **type `ElementConstructorOpts`** (L53-L76). `MarkOptional` over the generic element minus `id/type/isDeleted/updated`, with a long list of optional style/layout fields.
- **`_newElementBase<T>(type, opts)`** (internal, L78-L156). Builds the common element shape: applies `DEFAULT_ELEMENT_PROPS` defaults, `width/height=0`, `angle=0`, empty `groupIds`, `seed = randomInteger()`, `version=1`, `versionNonce=0`, `isDeleted:false`, fresh `updated`. Dev sanity check: logs an error if any of x/y/width/height exceeds ±1e6 (temporary guard, L103-L123). Returns a typed generic element.
- **`newElement(opts)` → `NonDeleted<ExcalidrawGenericElement>`** (L158-L163). Thin wrapper over `_newElementBase`.
- **`newEmbeddableElement(opts)`** (L165-L171). `_newElementBase("embeddable")`.
- **`newIframeElement(opts)`** (L173-L181). `_newElementBase("iframe")`.
- **`newFrameElement(opts)`** (L183-L198). Base + `type:"frame"` + `name` (default null), passed through `newElementWith` (to bump version).
- **`newMagicFrameElement(opts)`** (L200-L215). Same for `"magicframe"`.
- **`getTextElementPositionOffsets(opts, metrics)`** (internal, L217-L237). Computes x offset (center→w/2, right→w, else 0) and y offset (middle→h/2, else 0) from text/vertical alignment + measured metrics. Used to reposition text so its anchor stays fixed when its size changes.
- **`newTextElement(opts)` → `NonDeleted<ExcalidrawTextElement>`** (L239-L291). Resolves font family/size/lineHeight defaults, normalizes text, **measures** it via `measureText(getFontString(...))`, computes alignment offsets and adjusts `x/y` so the anchor is preserved, sets width/height to measured metrics, `originalText`, `autoResize` (default true). Wraps in `newElementWith`.
- **`getAdjustedDimensions(element, elementsMap, nextText)` → `{x,y,width,height}`** (internal, L293-L371). Recomputes text element bounds for new text. If not auto-resizing, keeps width. Center+middle non-contained auto-resize case repositions by the *difference* in measured metrics (L317-L334). Otherwise computes old vs new absolute coords (`getElementAbsoluteCoords`/`getResizedElementAbsoluteCoords`), halves the corner deltas, and applies rotation-aware repositioning via `adjustXYWithRotation` so the visible anchor edge stays put under rotation (L335-L362). Guards against non-finite x/y.
- **`adjustXYWithRotation(sides, x, y, angle, deltaX1, deltaY1, deltaX2, deltaY2)` → `[number,number]`** (internal, L373-L418). Adjusts x/y for a resize given which sides (n/e/s/w) moved, accounting for element rotation: applies `cos`/`sin` of `angle` to distribute the corner deltas. Key rotation math for resize parity — when both opposite sides move it's a simple sum; single-side moves apply the `(1±cos)`/`±sin` distribution.
- **`refreshTextDimensions(textElement, container, elementsMap, text=textElement.text)`** (L420-L440). Returns null if deleted; wraps text (`wrapText`) when contained or not auto-resizing (width from container max width or element width), then returns `{ text, ...getAdjustedDimensions(...) }`.
- **`newFreeDrawElement(opts)`** (L442-L456). Base + `points`/`pressures` (default `[]`) + `simulatePressure`.
- **`newLinearElement(opts)` → `NonDeleted<ExcalidrawLinearElement>`** (L458-L485). Base + empty `points`, null bindings/arrowheads; if it's a line element, adds `polygon` (default false) and returns the narrowed line type.
- **`newArrowElement<T extends boolean>(opts)`** (L487-L525). Conditional return type: `elbowed` true → `ExcalidrawElbowArrowElement` with `elbowed:true`, `fixedSegments:[]`, `startIsSpecial/endIsSpecial:false`; else a plain arrow with `elbowed:false`. Both default null bindings + optional arrowheads.
- **`newImageElement(opts)` → `NonDeleted<ExcalidrawImageElement>`** (L527-L546). Base with `strokeColor:"transparent"` (comment: future per-SVG stroke), `status` (default "pending"), `fileId` (default null), `scale` (default `[1,1]`), `crop` (default null).

---

Notable cross-file geometry/coordinate-space details for parity:
- Linear element points are stored in **local space with `points[0] === [0,0]`** as a hard invariant; global coords are derived by `+element.x/y` then rotation about the **AABB center** `((x1+x2)/2,(y1+y2)/2)` by `element.angle`. Moving point 0 is implemented by offsetting all other points and element x/y in the opposite direction (linearElementEditor `movePoints`/`_updatePoints`).
- Heading geometry uses a **y-down** convention (`HEADING_DOWN = [0,1]`) and resolves headings via search cones (2× scaled AABB corners + `triangleIncludesPoint`) for non-diamonds and per-vertex cross-product sectors (with 0.95 shrink) for diamonds.
- AABB of linear elements is derived from the **rendered RoughJS curve path ops**, not raw points (`getElementAbsoluteCoords` via `ShapeCache`).
- Hit-test thresholds are screen-space: `POINT_HANDLE_SIZE(=10)` compared against `pointDistance * zoom.value`.
- Version bookkeeping uses **djb2** hashing (`hashElementsVersion`/`hashString`) coerced to unsigned 32-bit.


---

## Cluster: element__src__4

This cluster covers the geometry-on-grid layout helper, the static/interactive element rasterizer, the resize/rotate transform engine, the resize hit-test, the reactive `Scene` document model, box-selection logic, and the roughjs shape generator + cache. All math is world-space (scene) unless explicitly noted; the renderer converts to device pixels via `window.devicePixelRatio`.

---

### packages/element/src/positionElementsOnGrid.ts

Lays out a flat list (or list of atomic groups) of elements into a roughly-square grid centered on a point — used after AI/flowchart generation. (Author comment: "TODO rewrite (mostly vibe-coded)".)

- **`positionElementsOnGrid<TElement extends ExcalidrawElement>(elements: TElement[] | TElement[][], centerX: number, centerY: number, padding = 50): TElement[]`** — L7-L112. Normalizes input: if `elements[0]` is an array, treats each sub-array as an atomic unit; otherwise wraps each element into its own singleton unit (L21-L23). Column count = `Math.max(1, Math.ceil(Math.sqrt(numUnits)))` (L29) → near-square grid. Slices units into rows of `numColumns` (L33-L35). For each row computes per-unit bounds via `getCommonBounds(unit)`, row width (sum of unit widths + inter-unit padding, no trailing pad), and max unit height (L40-L71). Total grid height = sum of row max-heights + `(rows.length-1)*padding` (L74-L75). Starting `currentY = centerY - totalGridHeightWithPadding/2` to center vertically (L78); each row's `currentX = centerX - rowWidth/2` to center horizontally (L85). For each unit, offset = (currentX - originalMinX, currentY - originalMinY), applied to every element via `newElementWith` (immutable copy) translating only `x`/`y` (L87-L105). Advances `currentX` by unit width + padding; `currentY` by row max-height + padding (L104-L109). Returns NEW elements (does not mutate). Invariant: relative positions within an atomic unit are preserved (single shared offset). Side effects: none (pure, allocates copies).

---

### packages/element/src/renderElement.ts

Rasterizes a single element to an offscreen per-element canvas (cached) then blits it to the target 2D context with rotation/scale/crop, plus opacity computation and freedraw outline segmentation; this is the core of the static-scene render pipeline.

- **`isPendingImageElement(element, renderConfig): boolean`** — L85-L90. True if element is an initialized image whose `fileId` is not yet in `renderConfig.imageCache`.
- **`getCanvasPadding(element): number`** — L92-L106. Per-type offscreen-canvas padding so strokes/arrowheads aren't clipped: freedraw → `strokeWidth*12`, text → `fontSize/2`, arrow → 40 (if any arrowhead) else 20, default 20. Note L99 bug: `element.endArrowhead || element.endArrowhead` (should likely be start||end).
- **`getRenderOpacity(element, containingFrame, elementsPendingErasure, pendingNodes, globalAlpha = 1): number`** — L108-L132 (exported). Combines frame opacity × element opacity / 10000 × globalAlpha (both stored 0-100 → product /10000 normalizes to 0-1). If the element, a pending flowchart node match, or its containing frame is pending-erasure, multiplies again by `ELEMENT_READY_TO_ERASE_OPACITY/100`. Returns 0-1 alpha.
- **interface `ExcalidrawElementWithCanvas`** — L134-L147. Cache record: `{ element, canvas, theme, scale, angle, zoomValue, canvasOffsetX, canvasOffsetY, boundTextElementVersion, imageCrop, containingFrameOpacity, boundTextCanvas }`. Used for cache-invalidation decisions.
- **`cappedElementCanvasSize(element, elementsMap, zoom): { width; height; scale }`** — L149-L202. Computes offscreen canvas pixel size and a draw scale, clamped to browser limits: `AREA_LIMIT = 16777216` (16M px), `WIDTH_HEIGHT_LIMIT = 32767`. Uses abs bounds via `getElementAbsoluteCoords`; for linear/freedraw uses `distance(x1,x2)`/`distance(y1,y2)` (rotated-aware extents) instead of `element.width/height`. Base size = `dim*devicePixelRatio + padding*2`. Scale starts at `zoom.value`; rescaled down if width/height×scale exceeds WIDTH_HEIGHT_LIMIT (L186-L191) or if area×scale² exceeds AREA_LIMIT via `sqrt(AREA_LIMIT/(w*h))` (L194-L196). Floors final dims. PERF/parity-critical: ties raster resolution to DPR×zoom.
- **`generateElementCanvas(element, elementsMap, zoom, renderConfig, appState): ExcalidrawElementWithCanvas | null`** — L204-L339. Creates an offscreen canvas sized by `cappedElementCanvasSize`; returns null if 0 width/height. For linear/freedraw, computes `canvasOffsetX/Y` = positive distance between `element.x/y` and bounds min (×DPR×scale) and translates context so negative-direction points fit (L228-L245); default offset is `(-100, 0)`. Translates by `padding*scale`, scales by `devicePixelRatio*scale`, then `drawElementOnCanvas` via a rough canvas (L247-L256). For arrows with a bound text label (L264-L321): builds a SQUARE `boundTextCanvas` of side `maxDim*DPR*scale + padding*scale*10` so rotation doesn't clip, draws the arrow canvas centered+rotated by `element.angle`, then rotates back, translates to the bound-text center, and `clearRect`s the label area (BOUND_TEXT_PADDING-expanded) so the unrotated label can be drawn separately. Returns the cache record including `boundTextElementVersion`, `containingFrameOpacity`, `imageCrop`. Side effect: allocates DOM canvases.
- **`const DEFAULT_LINK_SIZE = 14`** — L341 (exported constant).
- **`IMAGE_PLACEHOLDER_IMG` / `IMAGE_ERROR_PLACEHOLDER_IMG`** — L343-L359. Module-level `<img>` elements with inline-SVG data URIs (gray image / error glyph), guarded for non-browser (SSR) with a mock `{src:""}`.
- **`drawImagePlaceholder(element, context, theme)`** — L361-L385. Fills element rect with theme-dependent gray (#2E2E2E dark / #E7E7E7 light) and centers the placeholder/error img at `min(minDim, min(minDim*0.4, 100))` size.
- **`drawElementOnCanvas(element, rc, context, renderConfig)`** — L387-L601. The actual per-type draw into a (already transformed) context. rectangle/iframe/embeddable/diamond/ellipse → `rc.draw(ShapeCache.generateElementShape(...))` with round line join/cap (L394-L404). arrow/line → iterates the Drawable[] shapes (L405-L416). freedraw → iterates shapes; string entries are filled `Path2D` (dark-mode filtered strokeColor), Drawable entries `rc.draw` (L417-L437). image → clips to roundRect if `element.roundness` (L449-L459), draws from cache honoring `element.crop` source rect; dark-mode SVG inversion path: on Safari does a manual per-pixel `255 - channel` invert via a temp canvas (L474-L522), otherwise sets `context.filter = DARK_THEME_FILTER` (L523-L539); falls back to `drawImagePlaceholder` (L540-L542). text (default branch via `isTextElement`) → RTL handling temporarily appends the canvas to `document.body` when not connected (L548-L555), sets font/fillStyle/textAlign, splits text on `\r\n?`/`\n`, computes `horizontalOffset` by alignment (center→w/2, right→w, else 0), uses `getLineHeightInPx` and `getVerticalOffset` for baseline, `fillText` per line (L562-L595). Throws on unimplemented types (L597).
- **`const elementWithCanvasCache = new WeakMap<ExcalidrawElement, ExcalidrawElementWithCanvas>()`** — L603-L606 (exported). Per-element rasterization cache; keyed by element identity (regenerated on mutation since elements are immutable copies).
- **`generateElementWithCanvas(element, elementsMap, renderConfig, appState)`** — L608-L663. Cache wrapper: returns cached record unless zoom changed (and not `shouldCacheIgnoreZoom`), theme changed, `boundTextElementVersion` changed, `imageCrop` changed, `containingFrameOpacity` changed, or (arrow with bound text and angle changed — because the cached arrow canvas is reused under rotation but labels must stay unrotated). Otherwise regenerates and stores.
- **`drawElementFromCanvas(elementWithCanvas, context, renderConfig, appState, allElementsMap)`** — L665-L754. Blits the cached canvas to the target context. Computes element center in device px including `scrollX/Y` (L676-L677). Scales context by `1/DPR` (since cache is at DPR). Arrow-with-bound-text branch translates to center and draws the `boundTextCanvas` with offset compensation (L684-L700). General branch: translate to center, `rotate(element.angle)`, apply image `scale[0]/scale[1]` (only when image and not pending) (L708-L716), translate back, then `drawImage` at `(x1+scrollX)*DPR - padding` scaled by `1/scale` (L721-L729). Optional debug bound-text bounding-box stroke gated by `VITE_APP_DEBUG_ENABLE_TEXT_CONTAINER_BOUNDING_BOX` (L731-L749). Coordinate-space note: all positioning in device px; rotation origin is element center.
- **`renderSelectionElement(element, context, appState, selectionColor)`** — L756-L778 (exported). Draws the marquee/selection rect: translucent blue fill `rgba(0,0,200,0.04)`, 1px (zoom-adjusted) stroke in `selectionColor`, offset by `0.5/zoom.value` for crisp 1px lines.
- **`renderElement(element, elementsMap, allElementsMap, rc, context, renderConfig, appState)`** — L780-L1072 (exported). Top-level dispatcher. Sets `context.globalAlpha` from `getRenderOpacity`, reducing alpha to `DEFAULT_REDUCED_GLOBAL_ALPHA` when an elementLinkSelector dialog is open and the element is neither selected nor hovered (L789-L800). frame/magicframe → draws outline rect (round if `FRAME_STYLE.radius`), magic frames get a teal stroke (L803-L845). freedraw and the shape/text/image/arrow group each branch on `renderConfig.isExporting`: when exporting, draws directly with manual center-translate + rotate + shift (so no cache), with a dedicated arrow-with-bound-text temp-canvas path that scales by `appState.exportScale` and clears the label area (L890-L985); when not exporting, uses `generateElementWithCanvas` + `drawElementFromCanvas`, disabling `imageSmoothingEnabled` for angle 0 or right angles to sharpen (esp. text) (L1000-L1021), and renders a 10%-opacity uncropped ghost when cropping the active image (L1023-L1050). Resets `globalAlpha = 1` at end (L1071). Throws on unimplemented type.
- **`getFreedrawOutlineAsSegments(element, points, elementsMap): LineSegment<GlobalPoint>[]`** — L1074-L1128 (exported function). Builds a sequence of rotated world-space `lineSegment`s from a freedraw outline. Uses non-rotated bounds (`angle:0`) to find the rotation center, then for each point translates by `element.x/y` and applies `pointRotateRads(..., center, element.angle)`. `invariant(points.length >= 2)`. Reduce seeds with the first segment (points[0]→points[1]) then chains from `points.slice(2)`, each new segment starting at the previous segment's end. Used for eraser/collision against the freehand outline.

---

### packages/element/src/resizeElements.ts

The resize/rotate transform engine: converts a pointer position + active transform handle into new element geometry for single and multiple selections, handling aspect-ratio lock, resize-from-center, flipping, linear-point rescaling, bound-text font scaling, image scale flips, and elbow-arrow fixed-point mirroring.

- **`transformElements(originalElements, transformHandleType, selectedElements, scene, shouldRotateWithDiscreteAngle, shouldResizeFromCenter, shouldMaintainAspectRatio, pointerX, pointerY, centerX, centerY): boolean`** — L87-L201 (exported). Entry point; returns true if a transform happened. Single selection: "rotation" → `rotateSingleElement` (skips elbow arrows) + `updateBoundElements`; else computes `getNextSingleWidthAndHeightFromPointer` then `resizeSingleElement`; text elements get `updateBoundElements`. Multi selection: "rotation" → `rotateMultipleElements`; else `getNextMultipleWidthAndHeightFromPointer` then `resizeMultipleElements`. Side effects: mutates scene via `scene.mutateElement`.
- **`rotateSingleElement(element, scene, pointerX, pointerY, shouldRotateWithDiscreteAngle)`** — L203-L266. Computes center from abs coords; angle = `(5π/2 + atan2(pointerY-cy, pointerX-cx))` (the `5π/2` offset makes 0 = up). Discrete snapping rounds to `SHIFT_LOCKING_ANGLE` increments (adds half then subtracts modulo). `normalizeRadians`. Frame-likes forced to angle 0. Unbinds arrow start/end bindings if a binding element. Mutates element angle; also rotates the bound text (matching angle + repositioned via `computeBoundTextPosition`) except for arrows.
- **`rescalePointsInElement(element, width, height, normalizePoints)`** — L268-L283 (exported). For linear/freedraw, returns `{ points }` rescaled along x to `width` then y to `height` via `rescalePoints`; else `{}`.
- **`measureFontSizeFromWidth(element, elementsMap, nextWidth): { size } | null`** — L285-L308 (exported). Returns proportional font size = `fontSize * (nextWidth / width)` (width = bound-text max width if contained). Returns null if below `MIN_FONT_SIZE`.
- **`resizeSingleTextElement(origElement, element, scene, transformHandleType, shouldResizeFromCenter, nextWidth, nextHeight)`** — L310-L402 (exported). N/S handles scale font by height (width derived proportionally), repositions origin via `getResizedOrigin`. E/W handles re-wrap text to `max(minWidth, nextWidth)`, re-measure height, set `autoResize:false`. Mutates element.
- **`rotateMultipleElements(originalElements, elements, scene, pointerX, pointerY, shouldRotateWithDiscreteAngle, centerX, centerY)`** — L404-L488. Rotates each non-frame element around the GROUP center `(centerX, centerY)`: computes a group `centerAngle` (same `5π/2 + atan2` convention, optional discrete snap), rotates each element's own center by `(centerAngle + origAngle - element.angle)` to get its new center, then sets `x/y` shifted by `(rotatedC - c)` and `angle = normalize(centerAngle + origAngle)`. Elbow arrows instead just re-route via `getArrowLocalFixedPoints`. Unbinds bindings whose target isn't in the rotated set. Repositions bound text. Calls `scene.triggerUpdate()` once at end (batched).
- **`getResizeOffsetXY(transformHandleType, selectedElements, elementsMap, x, y): [number, number]`** — L490-L547 (exported). Computes the pointer offset (in rotated element space) from the relevant handle/edge, used to seed resize. De-rotates the pointer around the bbox center by `-angle`, then per-handle returns the rotated delta from the corresponding corner/edge midpoint.
- **`getResizeArrowDirection(transformHandleType, element): "origin" | "end"`** — L549-L560 (exported). For a 2-point linear element, decides whether the grabbed corner corresponds to resizing the origin or end based on sign of the second point and handle quadrant.
- **type `ResizeAnchor`** — L562-L571. Union: corners, sides (`west/north/east/south-side`), `center`.
- **`getResizeAnchor(handleDirection, shouldMaintainAspectRatio, shouldResizeFromCenter): ResizeAnchor`** — L573-L612. Maps handle → anchor (opposite corner/edge). `shouldResizeFromCenter` → "center". With aspect-ratio lock, side handles anchor to the opposite SIDE; corners anchor to the opposite corner. Without lock, e/se/s → top-left, n/nw/w → bottom-right, ne → bottom-left, else top-right.
- **`getResizedOrigin(prevOrigin, prevWidth, prevHeight, newWidth, newHeight, angle, handleDirection, shouldMaintainAspectRatio, shouldResizeFromCenter): { x; y }`** — L614-L720. KEY MATH for parity: given a resize anchored at one corner/edge/center while the element is rotated by `angle`, computes the new top-left so the anchor stays put. Each anchor case has a closed-form `x/y` expression mixing `(prevW-newW)/2`, `(prevH-newH)/2` with `cos(angle)`/`sin(angle)` terms (e.g. top-left at L634-L646). Center case is rotation-independent: `x - (newW-prevW)/2, y - (newH-prevH)/2`. This is the rotation-aware origin compensation a Canvas reimplementation must reproduce exactly.
- **`resizeSingleElement(nextWidth, nextHeight, latestElement, origElement, originalElementsMap, scene, handleDirection, { shouldInformMutation=true, shouldMaintainAspectRatio=false, shouldResizeFromCenter=false } = {})`** — L722-L926 (exported). Delegates text to `resizeSingleTextElement`. Bound text: with aspect-ratio lock, scales font via `measureFontSizeFromWidth` on the container's new max width (bails if null); without lock, clamps `nextWidth/Height` to approx min line width/height. Rescales linear points (`normalizePoints=true`) and re-derives origin offset from linear bounds (L806-L842). Negative width/height flips origin by adding the (negative) dimension (L845-L850). Image `scale` flip sign handling (L852-L860). Arrow bound-text font scaling under aspect lock (L862-L873). Only mutates if dims nonzero and origin finite; uses `Math.abs(width/height)`, attaches rescaled points, nulls/unbinds bindings (L888-L905), then `scene.mutateElement` with `{ informMutation, isDragging:false }`, updates bound-text font, calls `handleBindTextResize` and `updateBoundElements`.
- **`getNextSingleWidthAndHeightFromPointer(latestElement, origElement, handleDirection, pointerX, pointerY, { shouldMaintainAspectRatio, shouldResizeFromCenter } = {}): { nextWidth; nextHeight }`** — L928-L1020. Computes new dims from cursor. Uses `getResizedElementAbsoluteCoords` for orig and latest bounds; de-rotates pointer around START center by `-origElement.angle`. Per-axis scale from rotated pointer vs the start corner; `nextWidth = latestElement.width * scaleX` (uses ON-SCREEN dims so linear scaling matches the cursor). Resize-from-center: `2*next - orig`. Aspect-ratio lock: single-letter handles cross-multiply width/height ratios; 2-letter handles take `max(widthRatio,heightRatio)` preserving sign. Note: result dims can be negative (flip) — that's handled downstream.
- **`getNextMultipleWidthAndHeightFromPointer(selectedElements, originalElementsMap, elementsMap, handleDirection, pointerX, pointerY, { shouldMaintainAspectRatio, shouldResizeFromCenter } = {})`** — L1022-L1147. Builds the common bounding box of ORIGINAL elements plus manually-resolved bound-text positions (because `getCommonBoundingBox` returns null bound text for pointerDownState copies, L1041-L1068). `anchorsMap` gives the opposite corner/edge per handle (L1078-L1087); anchor = center if resize-from-center. Computes `nextWidth/Height` from `|pointer - anchor|` (×2 for center). `flipConditionsMap` (L1117-L1134) decides per-axis flip booleans by which side of the anchor the pointer crossed. Returns `{ originalBoundingBox, nextWidth, nextHeight, flipByX, flipByY }`.
- **`resizeMultipleElements(selectedElements, elementsMap, handleDirection, scene, originalElementsMap, { shouldMaintainAspectRatio, shouldResizeFromCenter, flipByX, flipByY, nextHeight, nextWidth, originalBoundingBox } = {})`** — L1149-L1500 (exported). Resizes/flips a group about a common anchor. Bails on all-undefined or zero dims. Recomputes `originalBoundingBox` if not passed (including bound text). Computes per-axis `scaleX/scaleY` (or uniform `scale` for corners / when `keepAspectRatio`). `keepAspectRatio` is forced if any element is rotated, is text, or is in a group (L1306-L1313) — important parity rule. Flip factors `[flipByX?-1:1, flipByY?-1:1]`. For each non-bound-text element computes new `width/height = orig.dim*scale`, flipped `angle = normalize(orig.angle*flipFactorX*flipFactorY)`, and new `x/y = anchor + flipFactor*(offset*scale + shift)` where `shift` is width/height for non-linear flips (linear/freedraw mirror via `rescalePointsInElement` with signed dims). Elbow arrows mirror `startBinding/endBinding.fixedPoint` (`-fp+1`) and remap `fixedSegments` to rescaled points (L1382-L1418). Images flip `scale` sign. Text recomputes font size. Bound text font scaled by `scale` under aspect lock. Applies all updates in a loop with `updateBoundElements`, unbinds out-of-set bindings, then a single `scene.triggerUpdate()`.

---

### packages/element/src/resizeTest.ts

Hit-testing for resize/rotation: given a scene-space pointer, returns which transform handle (or side) it lies on, plus cursor-shape helpers.

- **`isInsideTransformHandle(transformHandle, x, y): boolean`** — L39-L47. AABB containment test against a `TransformHandle` tuple `[x, y, w, h]`.
- **`resizeTest<Point>(element, elementsMap, appState, x, y, zoom, pointerType, editorInterface): MaybeTransformHandleType`** — L49-L128 (exported). Returns false if the element isn't selected. Gets handles via `getTransformHandles` (omitting sides per `getOmitSidesForEditorInterface`); checks rotation handle first, then the corner/edge handles. If `canResizeFromSides`, expands abs coords by `SPACING` (0 for images, else `SIDE_RESIZING_THRESHOLD/zoom`) and tests the pointer against the four rotated selection borders via `pointOnLineSegment` with threshold `SIDE_RESIZING_THRESHOLD/zoom`. Skips side-resize for linear elements with ≤2 points. Coordinate-space: x/y are scene coords; thresholds are divided by zoom to stay constant in screen px.
- **`getElementWithTransformHandleType(elements, appState, scenePointerX, scenePointerY, zoom, pointerType, elementsMap, editorInterface)`** — L130-L156 (exported). Reduces over elements, returning the first `{ element, transformHandleType }` whose `resizeTest` hits.
- **`getTransformHandleTypeFromCoords<Point>([x1,y1,x2,y2]: Bounds, scenePointerX, scenePointerY, zoom, pointerType, editorInterface): MaybeTransformHandleType`** — L158-L217 (exported). Same as `resizeTest` but for an arbitrary axis-aligned (angle 0) bounds rect (e.g. group selection box); builds handles via `getTransformHandlesFromCoords` and tests sides with `SPACING = SIDE_RESIZING_THRESHOLD/zoom`.
- **`const RESIZE_CURSORS = ["ns", "nesw", "ew", "nwse"]`** — L219.
- **`rotateResizeCursor(cursor, angle): string`** — L220-L227. Rotates a resize-cursor name by `round(angle/(π/4))` steps around the 4-entry cursor ring (so a rotated element shows the visually-correct resize cursor).
- **`getCursorForResizingElement(resizingElement: { element?, transformHandleType }): string`** — L232-L275 (exported). Maps handle → base cursor (n/s→ns, w/e→ew, corners→nwse/nesw, rotation→"grab"). Swaps the corner cursors when `sign(height)*sign(width) === -1` (flipped element). Applies `rotateResizeCursor` by `element.angle`. Returns `"${cursor}-resize"`.
- **`getSelectionBorders<Point>([x1,y1], [x2,y2], center, angle)`** — L277-L294. Rotates the four corners around `center` by `angle` and returns the four edges as `{ n, e, s, w }` segment endpoint pairs. Used by both resizeTest functions for side hit-testing.

---

### packages/element/src/Scene.ts

The reactive in-memory document model: holds the ordered element list + derived maps (non-deleted, frames), a memoized selected-elements cache, an update callback set, a render-invalidation nonce, and the single `mutateElement` entry that triggers re-render.

- **`getNonDeletedElements<T>(allElements): { elementsMap; elements }`** — L50-L65 (module-private). Splits an element list into a non-deleted array + a branded `NonDeletedSceneElementsMap`.
- **`validateIndicesThrottled`** — L67-L79. `lodash.throttle` (60s, leading) wrapper around `validateFractionalIndices`; runs only in dev/test or when `window.DEBUG_FRACTIONAL_INDICES`, throwing only in dev/test.
- **`hashSelectionOpts(opts): SelectionHash`** — L81-L102. Builds a stable string hash from `includeBoundTextElement`/`includeElementsInFrames` (with a compile-time `Assert<SameType>` guard ensuring all hashable keys are covered).
- **type `ExcalidrawElementsIncludingDeleted = readonly ExcalidrawElement[]`** — L106 (exported).
- **class `Scene`** — L108-L446 (exported). Fields: `callbacks: Set<SceneStateCallback>` (L113); `nonDeletedElements`, `nonDeletedElementsMap`, `elements` (ordered, includes deleted), `nonDeletedFramesLikes`, `frames`, `elementsMap` (L115-L125); `selectedElementsCache` `{ selectedElementIds, elements, cache: Map<SelectionHash, ...> }` (L126-L134); `sceneNonce` (render cache-invalidation nonce, NOT a version) (L141).
  - **`getSceneNonce()`** — L143-L145. Returns the nonce.
  - **`getNonDeletedElementsMap()` / `getElementsIncludingDeleted()` / `getElementsMapIncludingDeleted()` / `getNonDeletedElements()` / `getFramesIncludingDeleted()`** — L147-L165. Plain accessors.
  - **`constructor(elements = null, options?: { skipValidation })`** — L167-L176. Calls `replaceAllElements` if seeded.
  - **`getSelectedElements(opts): NonDeleted<ExcalidrawElement>[]`** — L178-L222. Memoized: returns cached array if both `elements` reference and `selectedElementIds` reference match and the hash is cached; clears cache when operating on latest elements but cache is stale. Delegates to free `getSelectedElements` (from selection.ts). Only caches when not using custom `opts.elements`. PERF: identity-based memoization across renders.
  - **`getNonDeletedFramesLikes()`** — L224-L226.
  - **`getElement<T>(id): T | null`** — L228-L230. Map lookup.
  - **`getNonDeletedElement(id)`** — L232-L240. `getElement` filtered through `isNonDeletedElement`.
  - **`mapElements(iteratee): boolean`** — L254-L269. Maps all elements; only calls `replaceAllElements` if any element reference changed (no-op optimization). Returns whether changed.
  - **`replaceAllElements(nextElements, options?)`** — L271-L301. Converts to array, throttle-validates indices (unless skipped), `syncInvalidIndices`, rebuilds `elementsMap`/frames/non-deleted maps, then `triggerUpdate()`. Trusts map insertion order.
  - **`triggerUpdate()`** — L303-L309. Regenerates `sceneNonce` via `randomInteger()` and invokes every callback (over a snapshot `Array.from`).
  - **`onUpdate(cb): remover`** — L311-L324. Registers a callback (throws on double-add); returns a remover (throws on double-remove).
  - **`destroy()`** — L326-L339. Clears all arrays/maps/cache/callbacks (guards against late fires).
  - **`insertElementsAtIndex(elements, index)`** — L341-L370. Inserts at index (null = end), `syncMovedIndices` for the inserted slice, `replaceAllElements`. Throws on non-finite/negative index.
  - **`insertElement = (element) => ...`** — L372-L375. Appends one element at end.
  - **`getElementIndex(elementId)`** — L377-L379. `findIndex`.
  - **`getContainerElement = (element)`** — L381-L395. Returns the container element by `containerId` or null.
  - **`getElementsFromId = (id)`** — L397-L407. Returns `[element]` if id is an element, else `getElementsInGroup` (group id).
  - **`mutateElement<TElement>(element, updates, options = { informMutation:true, isDragging:false, ... }): TElement`** — L411-L445. THE mutation gate. Delegates to free `mutateElement(element, elementsMap, updates, options)`; calls `triggerUpdate()` only if the element is in the scene map, its version actually changed, and `informMutation`. Returns the (mutated-in-place) element. Invariant: re-render fires only on real version bumps.

---

### packages/element/src/selection.ts

Box-selection geometry (which elements a marquee selects under "contain" vs "overlap" modes), frame/group exclusion rules, and the selected-element accessors used to derive editor selection state.

- **`shouldIgnoreElementFromSelection(element): boolean`** — L52-L54. True for locked or bound-to-container elements.
- **`excludeElementsFromFrames<T>(selectedElements, framesInSelection): T[]`** — L56-L66. Filters out elements whose `frameId` is in the selected-frames set.
- **`excludeElementsInFramesFromSelection<T>(selectedElements): T[]`** — L74-L88 (exported). Collects frame ids in the selection then excludes their child elements (frames + their members shouldn't both be selected).
- **`getElementsWithinSelection(elements, selection, elementsMap, excludeElementsInFrames = true, boxSelectionMode = "contain"): NonDeletedExcalidrawElement[]`** — L90-L367 (exported). The core marquee algorithm. Normalizes the selection rect to `selectionBounds` (min/max) and builds its 4 edge segments (L98-L127). For each element (skipping locked/bound-to-container): tracks top-level group membership; computes `elementAABB` expanded by `strokeWidth/2`; for arrows with bound text computes `labelAABB` from `LinearElementEditor.getBoundTextElementPosition`; CLIPS both AABBs to the containing frame's bounds when the element overlaps its frame (only the visible portion counts, L178-L201). Evaluation order: (1) if `boundsContainBounds(selectionBounds, commonAABB)` → selected regardless of mode (PERF note L216-L218: AABB-wrap trick valid only for axis-aligned selection + convex shapes); (2) overlap mode + label intersects → selected; (3) overlap mode + AABB intersects → precise test: for linear/freedraw checks if any rotated point is inside the selection; for others checks 4 rotated edge-midpoints; falls back to `intersectElementWithLineSegment` against each selection edge (stop-at-first-hit). After collection: removes frame children when their frame is selected; in "overlap" mode adds ALL group members of any selected element; in "contain" mode removes elements whose group isn't fully contained (L340-L363). Returns in original element order. Geometry-heavy; central to parity.
- **`getVisibleAndNonSelectedElements(elements, selectedElements, appState, elementsMap)`** — L369-L389 (exported). Filters to elements in viewport (`isElementInViewport`) and not selected.
- **`isSomeElementSelected`** — L392-L425 (exported IIFE-closure with `.clearCache()`). Memoized boolean: returns whether any element is selected, caching on `(elements, selectedElementIds)` identity. `clearCache()` resets.
- **`getSelectedElements(elements, appState, opts?)`** — L427-L469 (exported). Builds the selected-element array from `selectedElementIds`; optionally includes bound text whose container is selected, and optionally includes frame children (`getFrameChildren`) when a frame is selected.
- **`getTargetElements(elements, appState)`** — L471-L484 (exported). Returns `[editingTextElement]` if editing, else `[newElement]` if drawing, else `getSelectedElements` (including bound text).
- **`makeNextSelectedElementIds(nextSelectedElementIds, prevState)`** — L490-L499 (exported). Returns prev reference if shallow-equal (preserves identity for memoization).
- **`_getLinearElementEditor(targetElements, allElements): LinearElementEditor | null`** — L501-L519 (module-private). If exactly one linear element is selected (and only it + its bound elements), returns a new `LinearElementEditor`.
- **`getSelectionStateForElements(targetElements, allElements, appState)`** — L521-L545 (exported). Builds `{ selectedLinearElement, ...selectGroupsForSelectedElements(...) }`, with selected ids derived from frame-excluded, non-bound-to-container targets.
- **`getActiveTextElement(selectedElements, appState)`** — L550-L561 (exported). Returns the editing text element, or the single selected text element, else null.

---

### packages/element/src/shape.ts

The roughjs shape generator and its `WeakMap` cache: turns an element into roughjs `Drawable`(s) (or `null` for canvas-rendered types), generates arrowheads, elbow-arrow SVG paths, freedraw stroke paths, line→polygon toggling, and the geometric hit-test shape.

- **class `ShapeCache`** — L81-L164 (exported). `private static rg = new RoughGenerator()` (shared generator); `private static cache = WeakMap<element, { shape, theme }>`.
  - **`static get<T>(element, theme): shape | undefined`** — L92-L103. Returns cached shape if present and theme matches (or theme === null).
  - **`static delete(element)`** — L105-L108. Removes from shape cache AND `elementWithCanvasCache` (renderElement.ts).
  - **`static destroy()`** — L110-L112. Replaces cache with a fresh WeakMap.
  - **`static generateElementShape<T>(element, renderConfig | null): shape`** — L118-L163. When `isExporting`, always regenerates (no cache read). `null` is a valid cached value (= no rough shape, e.g. text/image/frame). On miss: deletes the element-canvas cache entry, calls `_generateElementShape`, caches unless exporting. Default renderConfig = light theme, white bg.
- **`getDashArrayDashed(strokeWidth)`** — L166. `[8, 8+strokeWidth]`.
- **`getDashArrayDotted(strokeWidth)`** — L168. `[1.5, 6+strokeWidth]`.
- **`adjustRoughness(element): number`** — L170-L191. Reduces roughness for small elements: keeps full roughness if both sides relatively big (min≥20 & max≥50), or rounded & min≥15, or a long linear (max≥50); else `min(roughness/(maxSize<10?3:2), 2.5)`. Prevents tiny shapes from looking chaotic.
- **`generateRoughOptions(element, continuousPath = false, isDarkMode = false): Options`** — L193-L264 (exported). Builds roughjs `Options`: seed, dash array by `strokeStyle`, `disableMultiStroke` for non-solid, +0.5 strokeWidth for non-solid, explicit `fillWeight = strokeWidth/2` and `hachureGap = strokeWidth*4` (so they don't track strokeWidth), `roughness = adjustRoughness`, dark-mode-filtered stroke, `preserveVertices` for continuous paths or low roughness. Per-type: fillable shapes set `fillStyle`/`fill` (transparent → undefined), ellipse adds `curveFitting:1`; line/freedraw fill only if `isPathALoop`; arrow returns base; default throws.
- **`modifyIframeLikeForRoughOptions(element, isExporting, embedsValidationStatus)`** — L266-L297. Returns a tweaked element for iframe/embeddable rendering: gray placeholder fill (`#d3d3d3`, roughness 0) when transparent & unvalidated/exporting; iframe defaults to black stroke / `#f4f4f6` bg when transparent.
- **`generateArrowheadCardinalityOne(generator, arrowheadPoints, lineOptions): Drawable[]`** — L299-L311. One perpendicular tick line `(x3,y3)→(x4,y4)`.
- **`generateArrowheadLinesToTip(generator, arrowheadPoints, lineOptions): Drawable[]`** — L313-L328. Two lines from `(x3,y3)`/`(x4,y4)` to the tip `(x2,y2)` (classic arrow `>`).
- **`getArrowheadLineOptions(element, options): Options`** — L330-L347. Solid cap by default (deletes dash), reduced-gap dotted for dotted arrows, roughness capped at 1.
- **`generateArrowheadOutlineCircle(generator, options, strokeColor, arrowheadPoints, fill, diameterScale = 1): Drawable[]`** — L349-L373. A solid-filled circle of `diameter*diameterScale` (used for circle / cardinality-zero markers), roughness capped at 0.5.
- **`getArrowheadShapes(element, shape, position, arrowhead, generator, options, canvasBackgroundColor, isDarkMode): Drawable[]`** — L375-L582. Switch over arrowhead kind producing roughjs Drawables. Outline variants fill with the background color (so they read as hollow). Handles `circle/circle_outline`, `triangle(_outline)` (polygon), `diamond(_outline)` (polygon), ERD cardinality markers (`cardinality_one`, `cardinality_many`, `cardinality_one_or_many`, `cardinality_exactly_one`, `cardinality_zero_or_one`, `cardinality_zero_or_many` — composed of tick lines and small outline circles with offsets like `-0.25`/`-0.5`/`1.5` and `cardinalityZeroCircleScale = 0.8`), and `bar/arrow/default` (lines to tip). All arrowhead points come from `getArrowheadPoints` (bounds.ts).
- **`generateLinearCollisionShape(element, elementsMap): { op; data }[]`** — L584-L749 (exported). Builds a simplified rough path-op list (move/lineTo/bcurveTo) in element-LOCAL coordinates for collision/hit-testing of line/arrow/freedraw. Elbow arrows → `generateElbowArrowShape(points,16)` path ops. Non-rounded → straight move/lineTo per point, each world-rotated by `element.angle` around the element center then de-translated back to local. Rounded → roughjs `curve` ops sliced to point count, converting bezier control points the same rotate-then-localize way. Freedraw → `simplify(points, 0.75)` then same bezier conversion (returns [] if <2 points). Coordinate note: output is local (subtracts `element.x/y` after rotating about world center) so it can be re-placed.
- **`_generateElementShape(element, generator, { isExporting, canvasBackgroundColor, embedsValidationStatus, theme }): ElementShape`** — L758-L1005 (module-private). The big per-type rough generator. rectangle/iframe/embeddable → rounded `path` (hand-written rounded-rect `M..Q..` with `getCornerRadius`) or `generator.rectangle`. diamond → rounded `path` with separate vertical/horizontal corner radii (quadratic-ish C curves at L834-L852) or `generator.polygon` of the 4 diamond points. ellipse → `generator.ellipse(w/2, h/2, w, h, ...)`. line/arrow → empty points seeded with `(0,0)`; elbow arrows guarded against extreme coords (>1e6) else `generator.path(generateElbowArrowShape(points,16))`; non-rounded uses `polygon` (if fill) or `linearPath`; rounded uses `curve`; arrows append start/end arrowhead shapes (default end = "arrow"). freedraw → optional loop-fill rough curve + a `getFreeDrawSvgPath` string (z-ordered bg then stroke). frame/magicframe/text/image → returns `null` (canvas-rendered; cached null avoids regeneration). default → `assertNever`.
- **`generateElbowArrowShape(points, radius): string`** — L1007-L1070. Builds an SVG `d` string for an elbow arrow with rounded corners. For each interior point, determines whether the incoming/outgoing segment is horizontal (`headingForPointIsHorizontal`), computes a `corner = min(radius, half-distance to each neighbor)`, and emits an approach point, the corner point, and a departure point (offset left/right/up/down accordingly). Then assembles `M`, then per corner `L ...` + `Q control, end`, ending with a final `L` to the last point. Geometry-critical for elbow-arrow parity.
- **`getElementShape<Point>(element, elementsMap): GeometricShape<Point>`** — L1076-L1124 (exported). Returns the pure geometric shape (for hit detection): polygon shapes for rect/diamond/frame/embeddable/image/iframe/text/selection; for arrow/line uses the first rough Drawable + element center to build a closed-curve shape (if `shouldTestInside`) or open-curve shape, rotated by `element.angle`; ellipse → `getEllipseShape`; freedraw → `getFreedrawShape` with center and inside-test flag.
- **`toggleLinePolygonState(element, nextPolygonState): { polygon; points } | null`** — L1126-L1168 (exported). Closes/opens a line into a polygon. When closing: bails (null) if `!canBecomePolygon`; if first/last point gap > `LINE_POLYGON_POINT_MERGE_DISTANCE` (or <4 points) appends a closing point equal to the first, else snaps the last point to the first. Returns the new `{ polygon, points }`.
- **`getFreeDrawSvgPath(element): SVGPathString`** — L1175-L1179 (module-private). `getSvgPathFromStroke(getFreedrawOutlinePoints(element))`. NOT cached (for SVG export).
- **`getFreedrawOutlinePoints(element): [number, number][]`** — L1181-L1200 (exported). Runs `perfect-freehand`'s `getStroke` over the points (with pressures unless `simulatePressure`): `size = strokeWidth*4.25`, `thinning:0.6`, `smoothing:0.5`, `streamline:0.5`, `easing = sin(t*π/2)` (easeOutSine), `last:true`. Empty input → a single dot `[[0,0,0.5]]`.
- **`med(A, B)`** — L1202-L1204. Midpoint of two number-array points.
- **`const TO_FIXED_PRECISION` regex** — L1209. Trims SVG path numbers to 2 decimals.
- **`getSvgPathFromStroke(points): string`** — L1211-L1232 (module-private). Converts a closed point ring into a smooth quadratic-bezier SVG path (`M ... Q ...` using midpoints), closing with `L first Z`, then trims precision via the regex. Empty → "".


---

## Cluster: element__src__5

### packages/element/src/showSelectedShapeActions.ts

Single-function predicate deciding whether the shape-actions panel (style controls) should be shown for the current selection/tool state.

- `showSelectedShapeActions(appState: UIAppState, elements: readonly NonDeletedExcalidrawElement[]) => boolean` (L7-L22) — Returns `Boolean(...)` true when the panel should be visible. Logic: not in view mode (L12); the open dialog is not the `elementLinkSelector` (L13); AND either (a) the active tool is not `"custom"` and we're either editing a text element OR using a "shape-creating" tool (anything except `selection`, `lasso`, `eraser`, `hand`, `laser`) (L14-L20), OR (b) there is at least one selected element via `getSelectedElements(elements, appState).length` (L21). Pure; no side effects. Parity note: the negative tool list at L16-L19 is the exact set of tools that do NOT show shape actions on their own — replicate exactly.

### packages/element/src/sizeHelpers.ts

Geometry helpers for element visibility/viewport tests, shift-locked sizing (perfect shapes, angle-snapped linear cursor), and dimension normalization (flipping negative w/h).

- `INVISIBLY_SMALL_ELEMENT_SIZE = 0.1` (L25) — Constant tolerance (px) used as the epsilon for treating a 2-point arrow whose endpoints coincide as invisible.
- `isInvisiblySmallElement(element: ExcalidrawElement): boolean` (L30-L47) — For linear/freedraw elements: true if `points.length < 2`, or it's a 2-point arrow whose first and last points are equal within `INVISIBLY_SMALL_ELEMENT_SIZE` via `pointsEqual` (L33-L44). Otherwise true only if both `width === 0 && height === 0` (L46). Pure. Has a leading TODO about removing invisible elements consistently (L27-L29). Parity note: the equality test uses the epsilon tolerance, not exact equality.
- `isElementInViewport(element, width: number, height: number, viewTransformations: {zoom: Zoom; offsetLeft: number; offsetTop: number; scrollX: number; scrollY: number}, elementsMap: ElementsMap)` (L49-L84) — Computes the element scene bounds `[x1,y1,x2,y2]` via `getElementBounds` (L62), converts the viewport top-left and bottom-right corners to scene coords via `viewportCoordsToSceneCoords` (L63-L76), and returns true if the AABBs overlap (`tl.x<=x2 && tl.y<=y2 && br.x>=x1 && br.y>=y1`, L78-L83). Pure. Parity note: this is an intersection (any overlap) test in SCENE coordinates.
- `isElementCompletelyInViewport(elements: ExcalidrawElement[], width: number, height: number, viewTransformations: {...same shape...}, elementsMap: ElementsMap, padding?: Offsets)` (L86-L122) — Uses `getCommonBounds` for the combined scene bounds (L100), applies optional `padding` offsets (left/top added to top-left corner, right/bottom subtracted from bottom-right before conversion, L103-L112), and returns true only if the bounds are fully contained: `x1>=tl.x && y1>=tl.y && x2<=br.x && y2<=br.y` (L116-L121). Pure. Parity note: containment (all-inside) test, contrast with the overlap test above.
- `getPerfectElementSize(elementType: AppState["activeTool"]["type"], width: number, height: number): { width: number; height: number }` (L127-L154) — Shift-key "perfect shape / locked line" sizing. For `line`/`arrow`/`freedraw`: snaps the line angle to the nearest multiple of `SHIFT_LOCKING_ANGLE` (L140-L142); if locked angle 0 → `height=0` (horizontal), if `PI/2` → `width=0` (vertical), else `height = absWidth * tan(lockedAngle) * sign(height) || height` (L148, the `|| height` guards against a 0 result). For any non-`selection` shape: forces a square by `height = absWidth * sign(height)` (L151). Pure. Parity note: `Math.atan(absHeight/absWidth)` can divide by zero when width is 0 → relies on JS `Infinity`/`atan` behavior; the rounding to `SHIFT_LOCKING_ANGLE` increments is the snap.
- `getLockedLinearCursorAlignSize(originX: number, originY: number, x: number, y: number, customAngle?: number)` (L156-L223) — Computes a width/height delta from origin to a cursor point snapped to the nearest `SHIFT_LOCKING_ANGLE` (full `atan2` over all quadrants, L166-L168). If `customAngle` given, snaps to it when the cursor angle falls in the bracket `[lower, lower+SHIFT_LOCKING_ANGLE]` and is within `SHIFT_LOCKING_ANGLE/6` of the custom angle, otherwise to the nearer bracket edge (L170-L196, uses `radiansBetweenAngles`, `radiansDifference`, `normalizeRadians`). For locked angle 0/PI/2 zeroes height/width respectively (L198-L201). Otherwise projects the cursor onto the locked-angle ray by intersecting the angle line `y = a1*x + b` with a perpendicular line through the cursor and returning the delta to that intersection (L202-L220). Pure. Parity note: this is a perpendicular-projection of the cursor onto the snapped ray (Cramer's-rule line intersection), NOT simple trig like `getPerfectElementSize`; `a2 = -1/a1` is the perpendicular slope.
- `getNormalizedDimensions(element: Pick<ExcalidrawElement, "width" | "height" | "x" | "y">): { width; height; x; y }` (L225-L253) — Returns a copy with negative width/height flipped to positive, shifting `x`/`y` left/up by the magnitude so the visual box is unchanged (L240-L250). Pure (does not mutate input). Parity note: this is the canonical "normalize a drag-created rect drawn right-to-left/bottom-to-top" operation.

### packages/element/src/sortElements.ts

Reorders an element array so grouped elements are contiguous (defragmented by group, first-occurrence order preserved) and bound text elements sit immediately after their containers, without losing any elements.

- `defragmentGroups(elements: readonly ExcalidrawElement[])` (L5-L54) — Internal. Recursively clusters elements by group at each nesting level so members of the same group are contiguous. Inner helpers: `groupIdAtLevel(element, level)` indexes `groupIds` from the END (`length - level - 1`) because `groupIds` is stored innermost-first so level 0 = outermost (L6-L8, L42-L43); `orderLevel(levelElements, level)` builds `slots` of either loose elements or group-id placeholders preserving first-occurrence order, buckets members by group id, then `flatMap`s expanding each group placeholder by recursing into `orderLevel(..., level+1)` (L10-L40). If the result count differs from input it logs `console.error` and returns the original array unchanged (data-loss guard, L48-L51). Side effect: `console.error` on the loss path.
- `normalizeBoundElementsOrder(elements: readonly ExcalidrawElement[])` (L65-L113) — Internal. Uses a `Set<ExcalidrawElement>` to dedupe-and-order: for each element with `boundElements`, adds the container then immediately adds each bound child of `type === "text"` looked up via `arrayToMap` (L77-L86); a text element that has a `containerId` whose container lists it is SKIPPED (it was already emitted after the container, L90-L98); otherwise the element is added (L100). Prefers the container's original z-index (moves bound text after container). Same data-loss guard: on size mismatch logs `console.error` and returns original (L105-L110). Side effect: `console.error` on loss path.
- `normalizeElementOrder(elements: readonly ExcalidrawElement[])` (L115-L119) — Exported. Composes the two: `normalizeBoundElementsOrder(defragmentGroups(elements))`. Pure aside from the internal console.error guards. Parity note: ordering rule = defragment groups first, THEN pull bound text next to containers; both steps are non-destructive (bail to original on any element loss).

### packages/element/src/store.ts

The undo/redo capture store: schedules micro/macro capture actions, snapshots observed appState + elements, computes deltas/changes, and emits durable vs ephemeral increments. Heavy state-machine + structural-sharing perf logic.

- `CaptureUpdateAction` (const, L38-L69) — Enum-like object: `IMMEDIATELY` (capture into undo/redo now; for most local updates), `NEVER` (never recorded; remote/init), `EVENTUALLY` (deferred capture for async multi-step). Drives all action precedence.
- `CaptureUpdateActionType = ValueOf<typeof CaptureUpdateAction>` (type, L71).
- `MicroActionsQueue = (() => void)[]` (type, L73) — Queue of deferred thunks run before the macro action.
- `class Store` (L78-L427) — Captures observed changes, emits `StoreIncrement`s. Holds `onDurableIncrementEmitter` (internal, history) and `onStoreIncrementEmitter` (public API) (L80-L84); `scheduledMacroActions: Set<CaptureUpdateActionType>` and `scheduledMicroActions: MicroActionsQueue` (L86-L87); `_snapshot` initialized to `StoreSnapshot.empty()` (L89). Constructed with the `App` instance (L99).
  - `get/set snapshot` (L91-L97) — Accessor over `_snapshot`.
  - `scheduleAction(action)` (L101-L104) — Adds action to the macro set and asserts the invariant.
  - `scheduleCapture()` (L110-L112) — Convenience: `scheduleAction(IMMEDIATELY)`. Has a TODO noting it's called in suspiciously many places (L109).
  - `scheduleMicroAction(params)` (L117-L175) — Pushes a deferred thunk that will run `processAction`. Three param shapes: pre-built `change`+`delta` (IMMEDIATELY), pre-built `change` only (NEVER/EVENTUALLY), or raw `elements`+`appState`. In the raw case it IMMEDIATELY snapshots the CURRENT scene state (not the store snapshot, to avoid including uncommitted async edits, L144-L150) via `StoreSnapshot.create`, clones via `maybeClone` after `syncInvalidIndicesImmutable(params.elements)` (L152-L161), and creates a `StoreChange` (L163). Side effect: enqueues into `scheduledMicroActions` (L168-L174). Parity note: indices are synced IMMUTABLY so scene elements aren't mutated (otherwise the change would be invisible).
  - `commit(elements, appState)` (L183-L201) — Flushes all micro actions (L189), then runs exactly ONE macro action chosen by `getScheduledMacroAction()` via `processAction` (L194-L195); in `finally` re-asserts invariant and resets `scheduledMacroActions` (L197-L199). Side effect: emits increments. Invariant: many micro (microtask-like), one macro (macrotask-like) per commit.
  - `clear()` (L206-L209) — Resets snapshot to empty and clears scheduled macro actions.
  - `emitDurableIncrement(snapshot, change?, delta?)` (L216-L246) — Builds `StoreChange` (given or `StoreChange.create(prev,next)`) and `StoreDelta` (given or `StoreDelta.calculate`), and only if `!delta.isEmpty()` constructs a `DurableIncrement` and triggers BOTH emitters (L240-L245). Side effect: emitter triggers.
  - `emitEphemeralIncrement(snapshot, change?)` (L253-L270) — Builds change, constructs `EphemeralIncrement`, triggers only `onStoreIncrementEmitter` (L266-L269). Side effect: emitter trigger.
  - `applyChangeToSnapshot(change)` (L272-L281) — Returns `snapshot.applyChange(change)` or `null` if reference-equal (no change).
  - `maybeCloneSnapshot(action, elements, appState)` (L286-L303) — Returns `null` if both inputs absent (L291-L293) or if clone is reference-equal; else the new snapshot.
  - `flushMicroActions()` (L305-L315) — Runs every queued thunk in try/catch (logs `console.error` on failure, L309) then empties the queue. Side effect: executes deferred work, may emit.
  - `processAction(params)` (L317-L386) — Core dispatcher. Perf shortcut: if action is `EVENTUALLY` and there are zero `onStoreIncrement` subscribers, returns early without cloning the snapshot (L335-L340). Computes `nextSnapshot` from a pre-built `change` or by cloning; bails if no change (L342-L357). Switches: `IMMEDIATELY` → `emitDurableIncrement`; `NEVER`/`EVENTUALLY` → `emitEphemeralIncrement`; else `assertNever` (L362-L375). In `finally`, updates `this.snapshot` ONLY for `IMMEDIATELY` and `NEVER` (NOT `EVENTUALLY`) (L376-L385). Parity note: EVENTUALLY never advances the snapshot — key to deferred capture semantics.
  - `getScheduledMacroAction()` (L391-L406) — Precedence resolver: `IMMEDIATELY` > `NEVER` > default `EVENTUALLY` (L394-L403).
  - `satisfiesScheduledActionsInvariant()` (L411-L426) — Asserts at most 3 scheduled actions; logs error and throws in test/dev envs (L419-L424).
- `class StoreChange` (L432-L449) — Holds changed `elements: Record<string, OrderedExcalidrawElement>` and partial `appState` (private ctor, L435-L438). `static create(prev, next)` derives both via `getChangedElements`/`getChangedAppState` (L440-L448). Reference-check based.
- `abstract class StoreIncrement` (L454-L471) — Base with `type: "durable" | "ephemeral"` and `change`. Static type guards `isDurable` (L460-L464) and `isEphemeral` (L466-L470).
- `class DurableIncrement extends StoreIncrement` (L476-L483) — Adds `delta: StoreDelta`; `super("durable", change)`.
- `class EphemeralIncrement extends StoreIncrement` (L488-L492) — `super("ephemeral", change)`, no delta.
- `class StoreDelta` (L497-L637) — Captured delta (`id`, `elements: ElementsDelta`, `appState: AppStateDelta`, private ctor L498-L502).
  - `static create(elements, appState, opts={id: randomId()})` (L507-L517) — New instance; default id from `randomId()`. Side effect: random id generation.
  - `static calculate(prev, next)` (L522-L535) — Calculates `ElementsDelta`/`AppStateDelta` only when `metadata.didElementsChange`/`didAppStateChange`, else `.empty()` (L526-L532). Perf: skips delta math when metadata says nothing changed.
  - `static restore(dto)` (L540-L547) — Rehydrate from a DTO via `ElementsDelta.restore`/`AppStateDelta.restore`.
  - `static load({id, elements:{added,removed,updated}, appState:{delta}})` (L552-L561) — Builds from a remote payload via `ElementsDelta.create`/`AppStateDelta.create`.
  - `static squash(...deltas)` (L566-L575) — Aggregates many deltas into one by mutating an empty delta's `elements`/`appState` via their `.squash` (L569-L572). Side effect: mutates the aggregate.
  - `static inverse(delta)` (L580-L582) — New delta with inverted elements & appState (for undo).
  - `static applyTo(delta, elements, appState, options?): [SceneElementsMap, AppState, boolean]` (L587-L606) — Applies element delta then appState delta, returning next elements, next appState, and whether any VISIBLE change occurred (OR of the two visible-change flags, L602-L603). Parity note: element delta is applied against `StoreSnapshot.empty().elements` as the baseline param.
  - `static applyLatestChanges(delta, prevElements, nextElements, modifierOptions?)` (L611-L628) — Rebases the element delta onto latest remote elements (preserves `id`).
  - `static empty()` (L630-L632) / `isEmpty()` (L634-L636) — Empty delta / emptiness check (both element & appState empty).
- `class StoreSnapshot` (L643-L987) — Immutable snapshot of observed elements + appState, with change/delta detection and structural-sharing clone. Private fields `_lastChangedElementsHash` and `_lastChangedAppStateHash` (L644-L645) used to dedupe EVENTUALLY emissions.
  - private ctor(`elements: SceneElementsMap`, `appState: ObservedAppState`, `metadata`) (L647-L659).
  - `static create(elements, appState, metadata?)` (L661-L677) — Normalizes appState to observed via `getObservedAppState` unless already observed (L674).
  - `static empty()` (L679-L689) — Empty map + default observed appState, `isEmpty: true`.
  - `getChangedElements(prevSnapshot)` (L691-L712) — Diff by reference: elements in prev but not next become `newElementWith(prev, {isDeleted:true})` (L694-L702); elements whose reference differs from prev are included (L704-L709). Relies on structural-clone reference identity from `maybeClone`. Returns `Record<string, OrderedExcalidrawElement>`.
  - `getChangedAppState(prevSnapshot): Partial<ObservedAppState>` (L714-L727) — Reduces `Delta.getRightDifferences` keys into a partial object.
  - `isEmpty()` (L729-L731) — Returns `metadata.isEmpty`.
  - `applyChange(change): StoreSnapshot` (L736-L754) — Returns a NEW snapshot: copies the element map, sets each changed element, merges appState through `getObservedAppState`, with metadata flags derived from non-empty change records (L737-L753).
  - `maybeClone(action, elements, appState)` (L761-L811) — Returns SAME instance if nothing changed (structural sharing). For `EVENTUALLY` sets `shouldCompareHashes=true` so repeated identical updates don't re-emit (L770-L775). Builds next element/appState snapshots, sets `didElementsChange`/`didAppStateChange` by reference comparison, returns `this` if neither changed (L786-L799). Parity note: the hash compare is the dedupe mechanism for deferred captures.
  - `maybeCreateAppStateSnapshot(appState, options?)` (L813-L840) — Returns current appState if absent or unchanged; else the new observed appState.
  - `maybeCreateElementsSnapshot(elements, options?)` (L842-L862) — Returns current elements if absent or no changed elements; else builds via `createElementsSnapshot`.
  - `detectChangedAppState(nextObservedAppState, options?): boolean | undefined` (L864-L899) — `undefined` if reference-equal or no right-difference; with `shouldCompareHashes` also returns `undefined` when the `hashString(JSON.stringify(...))` matches the last hash; otherwise updates `_lastChangedAppStateHash` and returns true (L872-L898). Side effect: mutates hash field. Perf: JSON-stringify hashing.
  - `detectChangedElements(nextElements, options?): SceneElementsMap | undefined` (L904-L965) — Reference-equal → `undefined` (L912). Builds a changed-only map: deleted (in prev not next) → `newElementWith(..., {isDeleted:true})` (L918-L928); added (`!prevElement`) or updated (`prevElement.version < nextElement.version`) included, EXCEPT uninitialized image elements are skipped (`isImageElement && !isInitializedImageElement`) (L930-L947). Empty → `undefined`. With `shouldCompareHashes`, returns `undefined` when `hashElementsVersion` matches last hash; else updates `_lastChangedElementsHash` (L953-L962). Side effect: mutates hash field. Parity note: change detection uses element `version` numbers, not deep equality.
  - `createElementsSnapshot(changedElements): SceneElementsMap` (L970-L986) — Structural clone: keeps all prev element references as-is, and ONLY `deepCopyElement`s the changed ones (L973-L982). Carries TODOs about lazy clone / immutable refs. Perf: deep-copy only changed elements — the core structural-sharing optimization.
- `hiddenObservedAppStateProp = "__observedAppState"` (const, L990) — Non-enumerable marker prop name.
- `getDefaultObservedAppState(): ObservedAppState` (L992-L1004) — Internal. Returns the default observed slice (name, editingGroupId, viewBackgroundColor=white, selection maps, etc.).
- `getObservedAppState(appState): ObservedAppState` (exported, L1006-L1032) — Projects a full `AppState` (or observed) down to the watched subset; `selectedLinearElement` is reduced to `{elementId, isEditing}` (L1018-L1023); defines the non-enumerable `__observedAppState` marker via `Reflect.defineProperty` (L1026-L1029). Parity note: ONLY these listed props participate in undo/redo appState deltas.
- `isObservedAppState(appState): appState is ObservedAppState` (internal, L1034-L1037) — Tests the hidden marker via `Reflect.get`.

### packages/element/src/textElement.ts

Bound-text and text-element geometry: re-laying-out text bounding boxes, wrapping/measuring text into containers, computing bound-text position (incl. rotation), container coordinate insets, and container/min sizing math.

- `redrawTextBoundingBox(textElement: ExcalidrawTextElement, container: ExcalidrawElement | null, scene: Scene)` (L46-L140) — Recomputes a text element's box. Builds `boundTextUpdates` carrying x/y/text/width/height and a resolved `angle` (0 if bound to arrow, else container/element angle, L62-L73). In non-prod asserts text angle is 0 when bound to an arrow (L55-L60). If there is a container OR `!autoResize`, computes `maxWidth` (container's bound-text max width, or the element's own width) and re-wraps `originalText` via `wrapText` (L77-L86). Measures via `measureText` (L88-L92); updates width only when `autoResize` (L95-L97), always updates height (L98). For containers: grows the container height/width via `computeContainerDimensionForBoundText` when metrics exceed max (mutating the container and the original-container cache for non-arrow height, L100-L122), then recomputes text x/y via `computeBoundTextPosition` (L124-L137). Side effects: `scene.mutateElement` (container and text), `updateOriginalContainerCache`. Parity note: width is only re-fit when `autoResize`; bound text always has autoResize true.
- `handleBindTextResize(container: NonDeletedExcalidrawElement, scene: Scene, transformHandleType: MaybeTransformHandleType, shouldMaintainAspectRatio = false)` (L142-L220) — On container resize, re-wraps/re-measures the bound text unless the handle is purely vertical (`n`/`s`) and aspect ratio isn't maintained (L166-L184). Resets the original-container cache (L153). If text height exceeds container max height, grows the container height and, when resizing from a north handle (`ne`/`nw`/`n`, non-arrow), shifts `y` up by the diff to keep the bottom anchored (L186-L205). Mutates the text's text/width/height (L207-L211) and, for non-arrow containers, repositions text via `computeBoundTextPosition` (L213-L218).
