A one-element array whose single entry has `uri: LiberationSansRegular` (the imported `LiberationSans-Regular.woff2` URL, L3) and **no `descriptors`** — meaning no `unicodeRange` subsetting and default weight. This is the simplest registration shape: one file covers the whole repertoire. Type imported at L1 (`import { type ExcalidrawFontFaceDescriptor } from "../Fonts"`). No side effects beyond the static woff2 import; the array is consumed by `Fonts.ts` to construct a `FontFace`.

---

### packages/excalidraw/fonts/Lilita/index.ts

Purpose: Registers the Lilita One display font as two Latin subsets (base Latin + Latin Extended) using Google Fonts' standard unicode ranges.

- **`LilitaFontFaces: ExcalidrawFontFaceDescriptor[]`** (constant) — L8-L17. Two-element array. Element 0 (`uri: LilitaLatinExt`, L10-L12) carries `descriptors: { unicodeRange: GOOGLE_FONTS_RANGES.LATIN_EXT }`; element 1 (`uri: LilitaLatin`, L13-L16) carries `descriptors: { unicodeRange: GOOGLE_FONTS_RANGES.LATIN }`. `GOOGLE_FONTS_RANGES` is imported from `@excalidraw/common` (L1). 
- Notable detail: ordering puts Latin-Ext **before** Latin. Because each face declares an explicit `unicode-range`, the browser only downloads the subset whose range contains the rendered codepoint, so the ordering here is registration order, not a precedence fallback. The two woff2 filenames are Google-Fonts content-hashed (L5-L6).

---

### packages/excalidraw/fonts/Nunito/index.ts

Purpose: Registers the Nunito font at weight 500 across five unicode subsets (Cyrillic, Cyrillic-Ext, Vietnamese, Latin-Ext, Latin).

- **`NunitoFontFaces: ExcalidrawFontFaceDescriptor[]`** (constant) — L11-L38. Five-element array; **every** entry sets `weight: "500"` in its `descriptors` alongside a `unicodeRange` drawn from `GOOGLE_FONTS_RANGES` (imported L1). Mapping of entry → range:
  - L12-L18: `CyrilicExt` → `GOOGLE_FONTS_RANGES.CYRILIC_EXT`, weight 500.
  - L19-L22: `Cyrilic` → `GOOGLE_FONTS_RANGES.CYRILIC`, weight 500.
  - L23-L29: `Vietnamese` → `GOOGLE_FONTS_RANGES.VIETNAMESE`, weight 500.
  - L30-L33: `LatinExt` → `GOOGLE_FONTS_RANGES.LATIN_EXT`, weight 500.
  - L34-L37: `Latin` → `GOOGLE_FONTS_RANGES.LATIN`, weight 500.
- Notable detail: the `weight: "500"` on each descriptor is the key parity invariant — Nunito is registered specifically at medium weight, not the default 400. The five woff2 filenames (L5-L9) are Google-Fonts content-hashed and differ only in the final hash segment (one source file per subset). Ordering is Cyrillic-Ext → Cyrillic → Vietnamese → Latin-Ext → Latin.

---

### packages/excalidraw/fonts/Virgil/index.ts

Purpose: Registers Excalidraw's classic hand-drawn "Virgil" font as a single unsubsetted woff2 face (the legacy default sketch font).

- **`VirgilFontFaces: ExcalidrawFontFaceDescriptor[]`** (constant) — L5-L9. One-element array; entry has `uri: Virgil` (the imported `Virgil-Regular.woff2`, L3) with **no `descriptors`** (no `unicodeRange`, default weight). Structurally identical to the Liberation registration. Type imported at L1. No side effects beyond the static woff2 import.

---

### packages/excalidraw/fonts/Xiaolai/index.ts

Purpose: Registers the Xiaolai SC Chinese (CJK) handwriting font, split into **209 separately-downloadable woff2 subsets** (indices `_0`..`_208`), each scoped to a precise `unicodeRange`, so a page only fetches the few subset chunks needed for the CJK glyphs it actually renders.

- **`XiaolaiFontFaces: ExcalidrawFontFaceDescriptor[]`** (constant) — L242-L1324. A large array; each element is `{ uri: _N, descriptors: { unicodeRange: "<range list>" } }`. The 209 woff2 assets are statically imported at L6-L214 (note the imports are **not** in numeric order — e.g. `_80` is the first import line at L6 — but the array body at L243+ is ordered by intended subset index `_0, _1, _2, …`). The type is imported at L4.
- Subsetting / coverage detail (the parity-critical part):
  - `unicodeRange` strings are comma-separated CSS unicode-range tokens, e.g. L246-L247 `"U+f9b8-fa6d,U+fe32,U+fe45-fe4f,U+ff02-ff0b,U+ff0d-ff1e,U+ff20-ff2a"` (CJK compatibility ideographs + fullwidth/halfwidth forms), and very large ranges further down such as the dense codepoint lists at L1300 and L1314/L1321 enumerating hundreds of individual CJK codepoints.
  - Entries cover Hangul syllable blocks (e.g. L300-L312 ranges like `U+11fb-11ff`, `U+ad2d-ae01`), CJK Unified Ideographs, compatibility ideographs (`U+f900-f9b7`, L257), and enclosed/fullwidth symbol ranges (`U+20dd-20de,U+25ef`, L254).
  - No `weight` is set on any entry — Xiaolai is registered at default weight; the only descriptor is `unicodeRange`.
- Provenance / non-obvious notes (from the header comments):
  - L1-L2: generated via chinese-font.netlify.app online-split, then hand-rewritten from `@font-face` rules into TS leveraging the FontFace API.
  - L216-L233: a generator banner from `cn-font-split@5.2.2` recording the origin font metadata (family "Xiaolai SC" / 小賴字體 SC, version 3.11, SIL OFL license).
  - L235-L240: a documented modification note — the original font was altered by (a) removing non-CJK codepoints, (b) reducing Hangul side-bearings to 40%, and (c) re-centering improperly-centered CJK codepoints. These metric changes (side-bearing reduction, centering) are advance-width/positioning modifications baked into the woff2 binaries themselves, so a Canvas reimplementation that ships the same woff2 files inherits them automatically; one that re-subsets from an unmodified Xiaolai source would get different CJK spacing.
- Performance detail: the 209-way split is purely a network/lazy-load optimization. Because each `FontFace` declares a disjoint `unicode-range`, the browser defers fetching any given subset woff2 until a glyph in that range is laid out — critical for a multi-megabyte CJK font. A parity implementation must preserve both the per-subset `unicode-range` boundaries and the file-to-range mapping, or it will either over-fetch (one giant font) or render tofu for codepoints whose subset never loads.


---

## Cluster: excalidraw__hooks__0

This cluster contains seven small React hook modules from `packages/excalidraw/hooks/`. They are infrastructure hooks (appState subscription, callback refs, portal containers, emitter bridging, library-item SVG rendering/caching, copy-status, and outside-click detection) rather than canvas/geometry code. Only `useLibraryItemSvg.ts` touches rendering (delegating to the SVG exporter), and `useOutsideClick.ts` has the only non-trivial DOM event logic. No file is empty; none is types-only (each has runtime exports), though `useAppStateValue.ts` and `useOutsideClick.ts` carry significant type declarations.

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useAppStateValue.ts

Provides hooks to subscribe a component to a narrowed slice of the editor `AppState`, re-rendering (or invoking a callback) only when the selected slice changes.

- `type AppStateSelector` (L9-L12) — union of `keyof AppState | (keyof AppState)[] | ((appState: AppState) => unknown)`. The three accepted selector forms (single key, key array, selector function). Used throughout the file to drive narrowing.
- `getSelectedValue(appState: AppState, selector: AppStateSelector)` (L14-L22) — internal. Resolves a selector against a concrete `appState`: calls the function form, returns the whole `appState` for the array form (the array is used only as a re-render trigger key, not for projection), or indexes by key for the single-key form. No side effects.
- `getLatestValue(api, selector, _internal)` (L24-L52) — internal. Pulls the current `appState` from the Excalidraw API and projects it via `getSelectedValue`. Guards: returns `undefined` if `api.isDestroyed` (L29-L31) or if `api.getAppState()` is falsy and `_internal` is false (L34-L37). For internal callers (`_internal === true`) with a missing appState it logs a warning (L39-L41) and falls back to a synthesized appState `Object.assign({ width:0, height:0, offsetLeft:0, offsetTop:0 }, getDefaultAppState())` (L45-L48) so internal components never receive `undefined` on init. Invariant: internal components are expected to render inside the `<Excalidraw>` tree.
- `useAppStateValue(...)` — overloaded hook with three public signatures (L70-L81): `(prop: K) => AppState[K]`, `(props: (keyof AppState)[]) => AppState`, `(selector: (appState)=>T) => T`; implementation signature `(selector: AppStateSelector, _internal = true) => unknown` (L82-L120). Behavior: grabs the API via `useExcalidrawAPI()` (L86), keeps a throwaway `useState(0)` counter purely as a re-render trigger (L87, `rerender`), and stores subscription state in a lazily-initialized `useRef` holding `{ selector, isInitialized, latestValue }` (L89-L100). On every render it refreshes `stateRef.current.selector` (L101) and, if not yet initialized but the API is now ready, computes and caches the latest value (L102-L105). A `useEffect` keyed on `[api]` (L107-L117) subscribes via `api.onStateChange(selector, cb)`; the callback writes the new value into the ref and bumps the rerender counter (L114-L115). Returns the cached `latestValue` (L119). Side effects: subscription registration/teardown. Performance note: component re-renders only when the subscribed slice actually changes (driven by the API's `onStateChange` diffing), not on every appState update; the array-selector form re-renders on any appState change since it returns the whole state.
- `useOnAppStateChange(...)` — overloaded hook with three public signatures (L128-L139), all returning `undefined`; implementation `(selector, callback) => undefined` (L140-L172). Same subscription model but invokes a `callback` instead of re-rendering. Stores `{ selector, callback }` in a `useRef` (L146-L149) refreshed each render (L150-L151). The `useEffect` keyed on `[api]` (L153-L169) first invokes the callback once with the current value (using `_internal=true`) and the full appState (L158-L161) — so consumers can initialize — then subscribes via `api.onStateChange`, forwarding `(newValue, state)` to the stored callback (L163-L168). Side effect: never triggers a component re-render (returns `undefined`).

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useCallbackRefState.ts

Tiny hook that bridges React callback-refs to state so a ref'd DOM/instance value can be used reactively.

- `useCallbackRefState<T>()` (L3-L7) — returns a tuple `[refValue, refCallback] as const` where `refValue` is `T | null` state (initialized `null`, L4) and `refCallback` is a stable `useCallback`'d setter (L5) suitable for use as a `ref={refCallback}` prop. Setting the ref updates state, triggering a re-render so consumers react to the node becoming available. No other side effects.

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useCopiedIndicator.ts

Manages a transient "copied successfully" UI indicator with auto-reset.

- `const TIMEOUT = 2000` (L3) — indicator visible duration in milliseconds.
- `useCopyStatus()` (L5-L27) — holds `copyStatus: "success" | null` state (L6) and a `timeoutRef` (`useRef<number>(0)`, L7). `onCopy` (L9-L16): clears any pending timeout, sets status to `"success"`, and schedules a `window.setTimeout` (stored in the ref) to reset to `null` after `TIMEOUT` ms. `resetCopyStatus` (L18-L20): stable `useCallback` that immediately sets status to `null`. Returns `{ copyStatus, resetCopyStatus, onCopy }` (L22-L26). Side effect: a window timer; invariant — the prior timer is always cleared before a new one is set so the success window resets on repeated copies. Note: `onCopy` is not memoized (recreated each render).

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useCreatePortalContainer.ts

Creates and manages a detached `<div>` portal container appended to the DOM, themed/classed to match the Excalidraw editor.

- `useCreatePortalContainer(opts?: { className?: string; parentSelector?: string })` (L8-L52) — returns the created `HTMLDivElement | null`. State: `div` (L12). Reads `editorInterface` via `useEditorInterface()` (L14), `theme` via `useUIAppState()` (L15), and `excalidrawContainer` via `useExcalidrawContainer()` (L17).
  - First `useLayoutEffect` (L19-L29, deps `[div, theme, editorInterface.formFactor, opts?.className]`): when `div` exists it resets `className`, re-adds the base `"excalidraw"` class plus any split `opts.className` tokens, toggles `"excalidraw--mobile"` when `editorInterface.formFactor === "phone"`, and toggles `"theme--dark"` when `theme === THEME.DARK`. Keeps the portal's styling in sync with editor theme/form-factor.
  - Second `useLayoutEffect` (L31-L49, deps `[excalidrawContainer, opts?.parentSelector]`): chooses the parent (the element matched by `opts.parentSelector` inside the excalidraw container, else `document.body`), creates a fresh `<div>`, appends it, and stores it in state. Cleanup removes the div from its parent (L46-L48). Invariant: the container is recreated whenever the parent/selector changes; bails out if no parent is found (L36-L38). Side effect: direct DOM mutation (createElement/appendChild/removeChild).

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useEmitter.ts

Bridges a `@excalidraw/common` `Emitter` to React state so the latest emitted event drives re-renders.

- `useEmitter<TEvent>(emitter: Emitter<[TEvent]>, initialState: TEvent)` (L5-L22) — returns the most recent event value (`TEvent`). Holds `event` state seeded with `initialState` (L9). A `useEffect` keyed on `[emitter]` (L11-L19) subscribes via `emitter.on(cb)` where the callback stores the emitted event into state (L12-L14); cleanup calls the returned `unsubscribe()` (L16-L18). Side effect: emitter subscription lifecycle. Invariant: re-subscribes if the `emitter` identity changes.

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useLibraryItemSvg.ts

Renders a library item's elements to an `<svg>` (with caching) and injects it into a target DOM node; also exposes a shared SVG cache atom.

- `type SvgCache = Map<LibraryItem["id"], SVGSVGElement>` (L10) — exported cache type keyed by library item id.
- `const libraryItemSvgsCache = atom<SvgCache>(new Map())` (L12) — exported editor-jotai atom holding the shared SVG cache (a single `Map`).
- `exportLibraryItemToSvg(elements)` (L14-L27) — internal async helper. Calls `exportToSvg` from `@excalidraw/utils/export` with a fixed export appState: `exportBackground: false`, `viewBackgroundColor: COLOR_PALETTE.white`, `files: null`, `renderEmbeddables: false`, `skipInliningFonts: true` (L17-L26). TODO comments note theme is not yet passed (relies on CSS filter) and that font inlining is skipped. Returns the produced `SVGSVGElement`. This is the only file in the cluster that performs actual rendering work.
- `useLibraryItemSvg(id, elements, svgCache, ref)` (L29-L85) — returns `SVGSVGElement | undefined`. State: `svg` (L35).
  - Effect 1 (L37-L66, deps `[id, elements, svgCache, setSvg]`): when `elements` exist and an `id` is present, tries `svgCache.get(id)`; cache hit sets state directly (L43-L44), otherwise exports asynchronously, strips the `.style-fonts` `<style>` node from the result (L50), writes it into the cache, and sets state (L52-L55). When no `id` (e.g. ad-hoc canvas selection) it always exports fresh without caching (L59-L63). Note: async IIFEs are not cancellable, so a late resolution can set state for a stale id (no abort guard).
  - Effect 2 (L68-L82, deps `[svg, ref]`): imperatively injects the SVG into the provided `ref` node via `node.innerHTML = svg.outerHTML` (L75-L76); cleanup clears the node's `innerHTML` (L79-L81). Side effect: direct DOM `innerHTML` mutation.
- `useLibraryCache()` (L87-L101) — reads the `libraryItemSvgsCache` atom (L88) and returns `{ clearLibraryCache, deleteItemsFromLibraryCache, svgCache }`. `clearLibraryCache` (L90) clears the whole map; `deleteItemsFromLibraryCache(items)` (L92-L94) removes the listed ids. These mutate the shared `Map` in place (the atom value is not reassigned), so consumers reading the same `Map` reference see the change. Neither helper is memoized.

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useOutsideClick.ts

Generic "click outside the referenced element" detector that handles edge cases around Radix portals, detached targets, and opt-out containers.

- `useOutsideClick<T extends HTMLElement>(ref, callback, isInside?)` (L5-L87) — no return value. Params: `ref` (`React.RefObject<T | null>`); `callback: (event: Event & { target: T }) => void` invoked when an outside click is detected (caller should memoize for perf, per L7 comment); optional `isInside(event, container) => boolean | undefined` override (L20-L24) that can force inside/outside classification.
  - Inside a single `useEffect` (deps `[ref, callback, isInside]`, L26-L86) it defines `onOutsideClick(event)` (L27-L76):
    - Casts the event to carry a typed `target` (L28); bails if `ref.current` is unset (L30-L32).
    - Consults `isInside` override (L34): `true` → treat as inside, return (L36-L37); `false` → treat as outside, call `callback` and return (L38-L39); `undefined` → fall through to default logic.
    - Default classification: returns (no callback) if the target is contained within `ref.current`, OR if the target is no longer attached to `document.documentElement` (L43-L49) — the detached-target case is explicitly handled because an element can be removed on a `pointerup` fired before this handler runs.
    - Radix-portal heuristic (L53-L60): treats clicks inside `[data-radix-portal]`, or on the `<html>` element while `document.body.style.pointerEvents === "none"` (Radix modal mode), as "part of the UI" and returns without firing the callback (L66-L68). Comments flag this as an intentional hack with minor downside.
    - Opt-out: returns if the target is inside a `[data-prevent-outside-click]` ancestor (L71-L73).
    - Otherwise fires `callback(_event)` (L75).
  - Listeners are attached to `EVENT.POINTER_DOWN` and `EVENT.TOUCH_START` on `document` (L79-L80), with matching removal on cleanup (L82-L85). Comment (L78) explains `click` is deliberately avoided because it reports an incorrect `event.target`. Side effects: document-level event listener lifecycle. This hook carries the only meaningful DOM/event-coordination logic in the cluster — relevant for parity since correct outside-click classification (portal/detached/opt-out handling) governs popup/menu dismissal behavior.


---

## Cluster: excalidraw__hooks__1

This cluster contains five small React hook/utility modules from `packages/excalidraw/hooks/`. They are infrastructure helpers (scroll tracking, stable references/callbacks, text-editor caret management, and a `useTransition` polyfill) rather than canvas/geometry code. No file is empty; one (`useTextEditorFocus.ts`) is the largest and exports both a hook and several standalone DOM utilities.

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useScrollPosition.ts

Hook that tracks the vertical scroll offset of a referenced scrollable element and publishes it into a shared jotai atom.

- Module constant `scrollPositionAtom = atom<number>(0)` (L6): a jotai atom holding the latest `scrollTop` value, initialized to 0. Shared at module scope, so all consumers of `useScrollPosition` read/write the same global scroll value (not per-element/per-instance).
- `useScrollPosition = <T extends HTMLElement>(elementRef: React.RefObject<T | null>) => number` (L8-L33): React hook.
  - Reads/writes the shared atom via `useAtom(scrollPositionAtom)` → `[scrollPosition, setScrollPosition]` (L11).
  - In a `useEffect` (L13-L30), grabs `elementRef.current`; bails (returns undefined cleanup) if the element is null (L14-L17).
  - Defines `handleScroll` as a `lodash.throttle` of a function that reads `element.scrollTop` and calls `setScrollPosition(scrollTop)`, throttled to **200ms** (L19-L22). This is the key performance detail: scroll events are leading/trailing-throttled to at most once per 200ms.
  - Attaches the listener via `element.addEventListener("scroll", handleScroll)` (L24).
  - Cleanup (L26-L29) calls `handleScroll.cancel()` (cancels any pending trailing throttle invocation) and removes the listener.
  - Effect deps are `[elementRef, setScrollPosition]` (L30); `setScrollPosition` is a stable jotai setter so the effect effectively re-runs only when the ref identity changes.
  - Returns the current `scrollPosition` number (L32).
  - Side effects: DOM event listener registration, global atom mutation. Invariant: only `scrollTop` (vertical) is tracked; horizontal scroll is ignored.

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useStable.ts

Hook that returns a single persistent object whose fields are continuously overwritten with the latest values, giving callers a stable reference that always reflects current data.

- `useStable = <T extends Record<string, any>>(value: T) => T` (L3-L7): React hook.
  - Holds the original `value` in a `useRef<T>(value)` so the ref object identity is created once (L4).
  - On every render, `Object.assign(ref.current, value)` mutates the persisted object in place to copy the latest `value`'s enumerable own properties onto it (L5).
  - Returns `ref.current` (L6) — the same object reference across renders, but with up-to-date field values.
  - Notable behavior/invariant: this is a shallow merge that only **adds/overwrites** keys present in the new `value`; keys removed from `value` between renders are NOT deleted from `ref.current` (they persist). Useful for passing "always current" data into closures/effects without retriggering them on reference change. No cleanup, no subscriptions.

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useStableCallback.ts

Hook that returns a function with a stable identity across renders that always invokes the latest version of the supplied callback.

- `useStableCallback = <T extends (...args: any[]) => any>(userFn: T) => T` (L6-L18): React hook (file-level JSDoc "Returns a stable function of the same type." at L3-L5).
  - Stores `{ userFn, stableFn? }` in a `useRef` initialized with the current `userFn` (L9).
  - On every render updates `stableRef.current.userFn = userFn` so the latest callback is always referenced (L10).
  - Lazily creates `stableFn` exactly once (guarded by `if (!stableRef.current.stableFn)`, L12): a wrapper `(...args) => stableRef.current.userFn(...args)` cast to `T` (L13-L14).
  - Returns the memoized `stableFn` (L17), which never changes identity for the component's lifetime.
  - Invariant/behavior: classic "latest ref" pattern — the returned function reference is stable (safe in deps arrays / event handlers) while always calling the freshest closure. No cleanup; no React 18 `useEffectEvent` dependency.

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useTextEditorFocus.ts

Utilities and a hook for saving/restoring the caret (text-selection) position of the WYSIWYG `<textarea>` text editor, used to preserve cursor state across UI actions (e.g. opening popovers in compact mode).

- Type `CaretPosition = { start: number; end: number }` (L4-L7): exported type describing a textarea selection range.
- `getTextEditor = (): HTMLTextAreaElement | null` (L10-L12): internal helper; `document.querySelector(".excalidraw-wysiwyg")` cast to `HTMLTextAreaElement`. Side effect: DOM query against the well-known WYSIWYG editor class. Invariant: assumes at most one such element.
- `saveCaretPosition = (): CaretPosition | null` (L15-L24): exported. Returns `{ start: textEditor.selectionStart, end: textEditor.selectionEnd }` of the current text editor, or `null` if no editor is present.
- `restoreCaretPosition = (position: CaretPosition | null): void` (L26-L37): exported. Inside a `setTimeout(..., 0)` (deferring to the next macrotask so focus/selection apply after the current event loop), focuses the editor and, if `position` is non-null, sets `selectionStart`/`selectionEnd` to restore the caret. Side effect: focuses DOM element + mutates selection. The 0ms timeout is the key non-obvious detail — needed so focus/selection survive re-renders/blur happening synchronously.
- `withCaretPositionPreservation = (callback: () => void, isCompactMode: boolean, isEditingText: boolean, onPreventClose?: () => void): void` (L39-L61): exported wrapper that runs `callback` while preserving caret state under compact-mode editing.
  - If `isCompactMode && onPreventClose`, calls `onPreventClose()` first to stop a popover from closing (L46-L48).
  - Saves caret position only when `isCompactMode && isEditingText` (else `null`) (L51-L52).
  - Executes `callback()` (L55).
  - Restores caret position when `isCompactMode && isEditingText` (L58-L60).
  - Invariant: caret save/restore is conditional on compact mode AND active text editing; otherwise it's a plain `callback()` pass-through.
- `useTextEditorFocus = ()` (L64-L97): exported hook returning an object of caret-management helpers.
  - State: `savedCaretPosition` via `useState<CaretPosition | null>(null)` (L65-L66).
  - `saveCaretPositionToState = useCallback(() => { setSavedCaretPosition(saveCaretPosition()); }, [])` (L68-L71): captures current caret into state. Stable identity (empty deps).
  - `restoreCaretPositionFromState = useCallback(() => {...}, [savedCaretPosition])` (L73-L85): inside `setTimeout(..., 0)` focuses the editor and, if a position is stored, restores `selectionStart`/`selectionEnd` then clears the stored position via `setSavedCaretPosition(null)` (L81). Re-created whenever `savedCaretPosition` changes.
  - `clearSavedPosition = useCallback(() => setSavedCaretPosition(null), [])` (L87-L89): resets stored caret state.
  - Returns `{ saveCaretPosition: saveCaretPositionToState, restoreCaretPosition: restoreCaretPositionFromState, clearSavedPosition, hasSavedPosition: !!savedCaretPosition }` (L91-L96) — note the returned keys re-alias the internal callbacks and expose a boolean `hasSavedPosition`.
  - Side effects: focus/selection DOM mutation, React state updates.
- `temporarilyDisableTextEditorBlur = (duration: number = 100): void` (L100-L112): exported. Captures `textEditor.onblur`, sets `textEditor.onblur = null`, and restores the original handler after `duration` ms (default **100ms**) via `setTimeout` (L105-L110). Used to prevent the editor's blur handler from firing during a brief window (e.g. clicking a toolbar control). Side effect: temporary mutation of the element's `onblur` property. Invariant: assumes the editor exists for the whole window; if removed, restoration targets the (possibly stale) captured element.

### packages/excalidraw/hooks/useTransition.ts

Provides `React.useTransition` when available, with a no-op polyfill for React 17.

- `useTransitionPolyfill(): readonly [false, (callback: () => void) => void]` (L4-L7): internal fallback. Returns a tuple `[isPending=false, startTransition]` where `startTransition` is a `useCallback` that synchronously invokes its callback (no actual transition/deferral). Marked as "noop polyfill for v17. Subset of API available" (L3).
- `useTransition = React.useTransition || useTransitionPolyfill` (L9): exported. Picks the native React 18 `useTransition` if present, otherwise the polyfill. Behavior detail: under the polyfill, `isPending` is always `false` and transitions are not actually deferred, so any code relying on low-priority scheduling degrades to synchronous execution on React 17.


---

## Cluster: excalidraw__lasso

This cluster implements Excalidraw's **lasso selection tool**: a freehand-drawn path (animated trail) that selects elements either by *enclosing* them or by *intersecting* their geometry. The cluster has two files — `lasso/index.ts` (the `LassoTrail` controller class) and `lasso/utils.ts` (the pure geometry/selection-test functions). All coordinates are in **world/scene space** (`GlobalPoint`); the lasso path comes from the underlying `AnimatedTrail`'s captured points and is compared against per-element line segments and bounds.

---

### packages/excalidraw/lasso/index.ts

Purpose: Defines `LassoTrail`, a subclass of `AnimatedTrail` that captures a freehand drag, renders it as a translucent purple animated stroke, and continuously recomputes the selected element set (handling text-container promotion, frame-child de-duplication, group selection, and single-linear-element editor activation).

Top-of-file declarations:
- `type CanvasTranslate = { scrollX: number; scrollY: number; zoom: number }` (L34-L38) — snapshot of the camera/viewport state, used to cache element segment computations and invalidate that cache when the view pans/zooms.

Class `LassoTrail extends AnimatedTrail` (L40-L218). Private instance fields (L41-L46):
- `intersectedElements: Set<ExcalidrawElement["id"]>` — ids whose geometry the lasso path crosses (mutated in `utils.ts`).
- `enclosedElements: Set<ExcalidrawElement["id"]>` — ids fully inside the lasso polygon (mutated in `utils.ts`).
- `elementsSegments: Map<string, LineSegment<GlobalPoint>[]> | null` — cache of each visible element's line segments, keyed by element id; `null` until first computed/after `endPath`.
- `canvasTranslate: CanvasTranslate | null` — last camera state for which `elementsSegments` was built; used to detect when to rebuild the cache.
- `keepPreviousSelection: boolean` — when true (e.g. additive/modifier drag), merges the lasso result with the pre-existing selection instead of replacing it.

Methods:

- **`constructor(app: App)`** (L48-L69) — Calls `super(app, {...})` with the `AnimatedTrail` config that defines the lasso's visual identity. `animateTrail: true`; `streamline: 0.4` (smoothing factor for the freehand curve). `sizeMapping(c)` computes per-point trail thickness via two decay factors: a time decay `t = max(0, 1 - (now - c.pressure)/DECAY_TIME)` where `DECAY_TIME = Infinity` (so `t` is effectively always 1), and a length decay `l = (DECAY_LENGTH - min(DECAY_LENGTH, c.totalLength - c.currentIndex)) / DECAY_LENGTH` with `DECAY_LENGTH = 5000`; returns `min(easeOut(l), easeOut(t))`. Fill is `rgba(105,101,219,0.05)` (faint interior tint), stroke is `rgba(105,101,219)` (solid purple). Notable: because `DECAY_TIME = Infinity`, the time term degenerates to 1 and the visible taper is driven entirely by the length term — points within the last 5000 units of trail length fade toward the tip.

- **`startPath(x: number, y: number, keepPreviousSelection = false)`** (L71-L88) — Begins a new lasso gesture. First defensively calls `this.endPath()` to clear any stray trail, then `super.startPath(x, y)`. Clears `intersectedElements` and `enclosedElements`, records `keepPreviousSelection`. If NOT keeping previous selection, immediately resets app state (`selectedElementIds: {}`, `selectedGroupIds: {}`, `selectedLinearElement: null`). Side effect: mutates app state via `this.app.setState`.

- **`selectElementsFromIds = (ids: string[])`** (L90-L162) — Arrow-function field that translates a raw id list into the canonical selection state through `app.setState((prevState) => {...})`. Algorithm: (1) build `nextSelectedElementIds` map from `ids` (L92-L95); (2) if `keepPreviousSelection`, OR-in all ids from `prevState.selectedElementIds` (L97-L101); (3) **text-container promotion** — for each selected text element that has a container, select the container and delete the text element id (L103-L116); (4) **frame-child de-dup** — for each selected frame-like element, remove all of its children from the selection so the frame represents them (L118-L131); (5) run `selectGroupsForSelectedElements(...)` to expand to full group selection (L133-L142); (6) derive `selectedIds`/`selectedGroupIds` arrays (L143-L144); (7) return new state, and if exactly one element is selected, no groups, and that element is linear, instantiate a `LinearElementEditor` for it, else `selectedLinearElement: null` (L146-L160). Invariants: text inside a container is never selected independently of its container; frame children are subsumed by their frame.

- **`addPointToPath = (x: number, y: number, keepPreviousSelection = false)`** (L164-L170) — Arrow-function field. Calls `super.addPointToPath(x, y)` to extend the trail, updates `keepPreviousSelection`, then triggers `this.updateSelection()`. This is the per-pointer-move hot path.

- **`private updateSelection = ()`** (L172-L209) — Arrow-function field; the core recompute. (1) Reads the current trail's `originalPoints` and maps them to `GlobalPoint[]` via `pointFrom` (L173-L175). (2) Builds `currentCanvasTranslate` from `scrollX/scrollY/zoom` (L177-L181). (3) **Segment cache invalidation**: if `elementsSegments` is null OR the camera changed (`!isShallowEqual(currentCanvasTranslate, this.canvasTranslate ?? {})`), rebuild the cache by computing `getElementLineSegments(element, visibleElementsMap)` for every visible element (L183-L194). (4) If a `lassoPath` exists, call `getLassoSelectedElementIds({...})` with `simplifyDistance: 5 / zoom` (so the path simplification tolerance is constant in *screen* pixels regardless of zoom), then apply the result via `selectElementsFromIds` (L196-L208). Performance detail: the per-element segment cache means segments are only recomputed when the viewport transform changes, not on every pointer move; `intersectedElements`/`enclosedElements` Sets are passed in and reused.

- **`endPath(): void`** (L211-L217) — Ends the gesture: `super.endPath()`, `super.clearTrails()`, clears both id Sets, and nulls out `elementsSegments` so the next gesture rebuilds the cache. Side effect: releases the cached segment map.

Coordinate-space note: everything operates in world space (`GlobalPoint`); the lasso path points come straight from `AnimatedTrail.originalPoints`. The only zoom-dependent quantity is the path-simplification tolerance (L204), which is intentionally scaled by `1/zoom` to keep it perceptually constant.

---

### packages/excalidraw/lasso/utils.ts

Purpose: Pure helper module computing which elements a lasso path selects, via an AABB broad-phase filter followed by an enclosure test (polygon-contains-point) and an intersection test (segment-vs-element-shape).

- **`getLassoSelectedElementIds(input)`** (L22-L90) — Exported. Signature: `(input: { lassoPath: GlobalPoint[]; elements: readonly ExcalidrawElement[]; elementsMap: ElementsMap; elementsSegments: ElementsSegmentsMap; intersectedElements: Set<...id>; enclosedElements: Set<...id>; simplifyDistance?: number }) => { selectedElementIds: string[] }`. Algorithm: (1) optionally simplify the path with `simplify(lassoPath, simplifyDistance)` from `points-on-curve` to cut point count (L42-L46); (2) filter out locked elements (`unlockedElements`, L47); (3) clear both `enclosedElements` and `intersectedElements` Sets up front, because as the path changes a previously-enclosed shape may no longer be enclosed (L48-L50); (4) compute `lassoBounds` as an AABB `[minX, minY, maxX, maxY]` by reducing over the *original unsimplified* `lassoPath` (L51-L61); (5) for each unlocked element, broad-phase test `doBoundsIntersect(lassoBounds, getElementBounds(element, elementsMap))` (and skip if already classified) — only if the AABBs overlap does it run the expensive shape tests (L62-L82); (6) run `enclosureTest` first; if enclosed, add to `enclosedElements`, otherwise run `intersectionTest` and add to `intersectedElements` on a hit (L73-L81); (7) return `selectedElementIds = [...intersectedElements, ...enclosedElements]` (L85-L89). Side effects: mutates the passed-in `intersectedElements`/`enclosedElements` Sets (these are the caller's caches). Performance: the AABB broad-phase (`doBoundsIntersect`) is explicitly chosen because it is far cheaper than per-shape geometry tests (comment L63-L65). Subtle detail: bounds are computed from `lassoPath` (raw) while enclosure/intersection use `path` (possibly simplified).

- **`enclosureTest(lassoPath, element, elementsSegments): boolean`** (L92-L108) — Internal. Builds a polygon from the lasso path via `polygonFromPoints(lassoPath)`, fetches the element's precomputed `segments` from `elementsSegments`; returns `false` if no segments exist. Returns true if *any* point of *any* of the element's line segments lies inside the lasso polygon, tested with `polygonIncludesPointNonZero(point, lassoPolygon)` (non-zero winding-number containment rule). Note: this treats an element as "enclosed" if even one of its segment endpoints is inside the polygon — it is a containment heuristic over segment vertices, not a strict full-shape-inside test.

- **`intersectionTest(lassoPath, element, elementsMap): boolean`** (L110-L143) — Internal. Converts the lasso path into a closed sequence of `lineSegment`s: pairs each point with its predecessor (`lassoPath.slice(1).map(...lineSegment(lassoPath[index], point))`) and appends a closing segment from the last point back to the first (L115-L118). Fetches any bound text element via `getBoundTextElement`. Returns true if *any* lasso segment intersects the element's shape (`intersectElementWithLineSegment(element, elementsMap, lassoSegment, 0, true).length > 0`) OR intersects the element's bound text element (positioned via `computeBoundTextPosition`, spread onto the bound text) (L122-L142). The `0` arg is the intersection tolerance/offset and `true` enables the relevant intersection mode flag of `intersectElementWithLineSegment`. Geometry detail: the lasso polygon is explicitly closed by the appended final segment so a shape edge crossing the "open mouth" of the freehand stroke is still detected.

Imports / dependencies of note: `simplify` from `points-on-curve` (path decimation); `polygonFromPoints`, `lineSegment`, `polygonIncludesPointNonZero` from `@excalidraw/math`; `computeBoundTextPosition`, `doBoundsIntersect`, `getBoundTextElement`, `getElementBounds`, `intersectElementWithLineSegment` from `@excalidraw/element`. Types `ElementsSegmentsMap`/`GlobalPoint` (math) and `ElementsMap`/`ExcalidrawElement` (element).


---

## Cluster: excalidraw__renderer__0

This cluster covers the Canvas-2D rendering layer of Excalidraw: the static element canvas, the interactive overlay canvas (selection borders, transform/crop handles, linear-element point handles, binding highlights), the in-progress "new element" canvas, snap-guide rendering, a shared animation scheduler, low-level canvas helpers, and a rounded-rect path primitive. All coordinate work happens in world-space after `context.scale(zoom)` and `context.translate(scrollX, scrollY)`; line widths and handle sizes are divided by `zoom.value` so they stay screen-constant.

---

### packages/excalidraw/renderer/animation.ts

Purpose: A static, key-keyed animation scheduler that drives stateful per-frame callbacks via `requestAnimationFrame` (or `setTimeout` when render-throttling is disabled), used by the interactive scene's animated binding highlight.

- `type Animation<R extends object> = (params: { deltaTime: number; state?: R }) => R | null | undefined` (L3-L6) — the callback contract: receives the elapsed `deltaTime` (ms) and prior `state`, returns next state to continue or a falsy value to stop. Returning `null`/`undefined` removes the animation from the loop.
- `class AnimationController` (L8-L126) — singleton-style controller; all members are `static`. Holds two static fields:
  - `scheduledFrame` (L9-L12): tagged union `{id, type:"raf"} | {id, type:"timeout"} | null` tracking the single pending frame across ALL animations (one shared frame, not one per animation).
  - `animations` (L13-L20): `Map<string, {animation, lastTime, state}>` keyed by caller-supplied string key, so duplicate `start()` calls for the same key are idempotent.
  - `static start<R extends object>(key: string, animation: Animation<R>)` (L22-L41) — if `key` already registered, no-op (L23-25). Otherwise invokes the animation once with `deltaTime:0, state:undefined` to get an initial state (L27-30); only if that initial state is truthy does it register the entry and schedule the next frame (L32-40). Invariant: an animation that returns falsy on first call never enters the map.
  - `private static scheduleNextFrame()` (L43-L59) — guards against double-scheduling (L44-46); picks `requestAnimationFrame(tick)` when `isRenderThrottlingEnabled()` else `setTimeout(tick, 0)` (L48-58). Performance note: rAF coalesces to display refresh; the timeout path runs as fast as the event loop allows.
  - `private static cancelScheduledFrame()` (L61-L73) — cancels the pending rAF/timeout by tag and nulls `scheduledFrame`.
  - `private static cancelScheduledFrameIfIdle(): boolean` (L75-L82) — returns false (and does nothing) if any animations remain; otherwise cancels the frame and returns true. Used to short-circuit the tick loop when the map empties.
  - `private static tick()` (L84-L116) — the loop body. Clears `scheduledFrame` (L85), then for each registered animation computes `deltaTime = lastTime===0 ? 0 : now - lastTime` using `performance.now()` per-entry (L89-91), runs the callback, deletes entries whose result is falsy (and early-returns if that empties the map, L98-103), otherwise updates `lastTime`/`state` (L104-107). After the loop, re-schedules a frame unless idle (L110-114). Side effect: mutates the static map in place.
  - `static running(key: string): boolean` (L118-L120) — membership check on the map.
  - `static cancel(key: string)` (L122-L125) — removes a key and cancels the shared frame if nothing is left.

Notable: `lastTime===0` sentinel means the first real frame after registration also reports `deltaTime 0` (the initial probe call already consumed time 0). `performance.now()` is sampled inside the per-entry loop, so multiple animations in one tick can see slightly different `now` values.

---

### packages/excalidraw/renderer/helpers.ts

Purpose: Low-level Canvas-2D primitives shared by every renderer — circle fill, DPR-normalized dimension math, canvas bootstrap (transform reset + background paint), and a rotated stroke-rect.

- `fillCircle(context, cx, cy, radius, stroke, fill = true)` (L6-L22) — `beginPath` + full `arc(0..2π)`; fills if `fill`, strokes if `stroke`. No `save/restore` — caller owns fillStyle/strokeStyle. Used for transform-handle rotation circles and linear-point handles.
- `getNormalizedCanvasDimensions(canvas, scale): [number, number]` (L24-L30) — returns `[canvas.width/scale, canvas.height/scale]`. Converts the DPR-scaled backing-store size to CSS/logical pixels so downstream math works in logical space. Invariant: `scale` here is `window.devicePixelRatio` (the canvas backing scale), NOT the camera zoom.
- `bootstrapCanvas({canvas, scale, normalizedWidth, normalizedHeight, theme?, isExporting?, viewBackgroundColor?}): CanvasRenderingContext2D` (L32-L76) — resets the transform to identity via `setTransform(1,0,0,1,0,0)` then `scale(scale, scale)` to apply DPR (L51-52). Background handling (L55-73): if `viewBackgroundColor` is a string, detects transparency (literal `"transparent"`, 5-char `#RGBA`, 9-char `#RRGGBBA`, or `hsla(/rgba(` regex) and `clearRect`s first when transparent (L56-63); then fills the whole canvas with the color, applying `applyDarkModeFilter` when `theme===THEME.DARK` (L64-70). If no color string given, just `clearRect`s (L71-73). Returns the 2D context. Side effects: clears/paints the entire canvas and leaves the DPR scale applied (the only transform on entry to scene rendering).
- `strokeRectWithRotation_simple(context, x, y, width, height, cx, cy, angle, fill = false, radius = 0)` (L78-L106) — draws a rectangle rotated `angle` radians about pivot `(cx, cy)`. Algorithm: `save` → `translate(cx,cy)` → `rotate(angle)` → draw with coords shifted by `-cx,-cy` (so the rect is positioned relative to the rotated origin) → `restore` (L91-105). Optionally `fillRect` first (L94-96). If `radius` and `context.roundRect` exist, uses a rounded path + stroke; else `strokeRect` (L97-104). The `radius` param comment says it "should account for zoom" — callers pass `radius/zoom.value`. Coordinate-space detail: pivot rotation here is the canonical way Excalidraw renders rotated selection borders/handles.

---

### packages/excalidraw/renderer/roundRect.ts

Purpose: A single rounded-rectangle path primitive (manual quadratic-curve corners) used for scrollbars and as a fallback when the native `context.roundRect` is unavailable.

- `roundRect(context, x, y, width, height, radius, strokeColor?)` (L11-L41) — builds a rounded-rect path manually: `moveTo(x+radius, y)`, four straight `lineTo` edges joined by four `quadraticCurveTo` corners with the rectangle's actual corners as control points (L20-35), then `closePath`, `fill`, optionally set `strokeStyle`, and `stroke` (L36-40). Always both fills and strokes. Geometry note: corner control point is the true rect corner (e.g. `(x+width, y)`), giving a circular-ish quadratic fillet of `radius`. Attribution comment cites stackoverflow.com/a/3368118. Invariant: assumes `radius <= min(width,height)/2`; no clamping.

---

### packages/excalidraw/renderer/renderSnaps.ts

Purpose: Renders alignment/spacing snap guides (point lines, pointer lines, gap lines) on the interactive canvas, with zoom-invariant sizing and theme/zen-mode color logic.

Module constants (L8-L11): `SNAP_COLOR_LIGHT = "#ff6b6b"`, `SNAP_COLOR_DARK = "#ff0000"`, `SNAP_WIDTH = 1`, `SNAP_CROSS_SIZE = 2`.

- `renderSnaps(context, appState: InteractiveCanvasAppState)` (L13-L61) — early-return if no snap lines (L17-19). Chooses color: light/zen → light color, else dark color (the comment at L21-23 explains dark-mode color inversion is avoided in zen mode because only crosses are drawn) (L24-27). Computes `snapWidth = (zen ? SNAP_WIDTH*1.5 : SNAP_WIDTH)/zoom.value` (L29-31). `save` + `translate(scrollX, scrollY)` (L33-34), then dispatches per snap line by `type`: `"pointer"` → `drawPointerSnapLine`, `"gap"` → `drawGapLine`, `"points"` → `drawPointsSnapLine`, each setting `lineWidth`/`strokeStyle` first (L36-58). `restore` at L60.
- `drawPointsSnapLine(pointSnapLine, context, appState)` (L63-L78) — unless zen mode, draws a single line from first to last point (L68-73), then a cross at every point (L75-77).
- `drawPointerSnapLine(pointerSnapLine, context, appState)` (L80-L89) — always draws a cross at `points[0]`; unless zen mode also draws a line from `points[0]` to `points[1]`.
- `drawCross<Point>([x,y], appState, context)` (L91-L110) — draws an X: size = `(zen ? SNAP_CROSS_SIZE*1.5 : SNAP_CROSS_SIZE)/zoom.value` (L97-99); two diagonal strokes from `(x-size,y-size)→(x+size,y+size)` and `(x+size,y-size)→(x-size,y+size)` (L102-107). `save/restore` wrapped.
- `drawLine<Point>(from, to, context)` (L112-L121) — `beginPath` then two `lineTo` calls (note: starts with `lineTo(from)` not `moveTo` — relies on current path position being reset by `beginPath`, so effectively a degenerate first segment from the path origin; in practice the prior subpath is empty so it behaves as a move). Strokes the line.
- `drawGapLine<Point>(from, to, direction, appState, context)` (L123-L209) — renders the equal-gap indicator with end-caps and a doubled mid-tick. Sizes are zoom-invariant: `FULL = 8/zoom`, `HALF = FULL/2`, `QUARTER = FULL/4` (L136-138). For `"horizontal"` (L140-173): computes `halfPoint` at the x-midpoint (L141); draws cap (1) at `from`, two mid-ticks (3) offset by ±QUARTER around the half point, cap (4) at `to`, and the connecting line (2) — caps and connector are suppressed in zen mode, mid-ticks always drawn (L143-172). The `"vertical"` branch mirrors the geometry swapping x/y roles (L174-208). The ASCII diagram at L130-134 documents the four tick groups. Uses `pointFrom` from `@excalidraw/math` to build points.

---

### packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts

Purpose: The interactive overlay canvas renderer — selection borders, transform/crop handles, linear-element point & midpoint handles, binding highlights (simple and animated "complex" variants), focus-point indicators, text bounding box, search-match highlights, remote cursors, and scrollbars.

- `renderElbowArrowMidPointHighlight(context, appState)` (L115-L131) — asserts `selectedLinearElement` and its `segmentMidPointHoveredCoords` are non-null via `invariant`, translates by scroll, and draws a `highlightPoint` at the hovered elbow segment midpoint.
- `renderLinearElementPointHighlight(context, appState, elementsMap: ElementsMap)` (L133-L165) — highlights the hovered linear point unless it's already selected-while-editing (L139-146) or the element is being dragged (L147-149). Resolves the element via `LinearElementEditor.getElement`, gets the global point at `hoverPointIndex` via `getPointAtIndexGlobalCoordinates`, translates by scroll and draws the highlight.
- `highlightPoint<Point>(point, context, appState)` (L167-L181) — fills a translucent purple circle (`rgba(105,101,219,0.4)`) of radius `POINT_HANDLE_SIZE/zoom.value` at the point. Zoom-invariant radius.
- `renderFocusPointHighlight(context, appState, focusPoint: GlobalPoint)` (L183-L194) — scroll-translated `highlightPoint` at an arrow binding focus point.
- `renderSingleLinearPoint<Point>(context, appState, point, radius, isSelected, isPhantomPoint, isOverlappingPoint)` (L196-L224) — draws one linear-element vertex handle. Stroke `#5e5ad8`; fill varies: selected → `rgba(134,131,226,0.9)`, phantom → `rgba(177,151,252,0.7)`, else white-ish (L205-212). Final radius is scaled: overlapping points get `radius * (editing?1.5:2)` else `radius`, divided by zoom (L218-220); fill enabled unless phantom; stroke enabled unless overlapping-and-unselected (L221-222).
- `renderBindingHighlightForBindableElement_simple(context, suggestedBinding, elementsMap, appState, pointerCoords)` (L226-L556) — the non-feature-flagged binding highlight. If the element sits in a frame, clips to the (rounded) frame rect first (L233-256). For `frame/magicframe` it strokes the frame outline in blue (L258-292). Otherwise it rotates the context about the element center (L294-300), translates to the element origin, sets a zoom-clamped `lineWidth` (`clamp(1.75, strokeWidth, 4)/max(0.25, zoom)`, L304-306) and blue stroke, then per shape: `ellipse` via `context.ellipse` (L313-326); `diamond` and default `rectanguloid` by deconstructing the element into line `segments` + bezier `curves` (`deconstructDiamondElement`/`deconstructRectanguloidElement`) and stroking each segment/curve with coords offset by `-element.x/-element.y` (L327-409). Then, if midpoint snapping is enabled and the element is frame-like/bindable (L416-555), it computes 4 cardinal midpoints (diamond uses `bezierEquation(curve,0.5)` of base corners rotated by `pointRotateRads`; others use RIGHT/BOTTOM/LEFT/TOP base points rotated about center) (L446-489), finds the closest midpoint to `pointerCoords` via a `reduce` (L491-512), and draws highlighted (blue) or shown (gray) midpoint dots of radius `4/zoom`, gated by `maxBindingDistance_simple(zoom)+strokeWidth/2` thresholds and elbow-arrow special-casing (L514-551). Heavy geometry/coordinate-space code — central to binding-UI parity.
- `renderBindingHighlightForBindableElement_complex(app, context, element, allElementsMap, appState, deltaTime, state?)` (L558-L888) — the animated (feature-flagged `COMPLEX_BINDINGS`) variant. Computes an `opacity` from a `BIND_MODE_TIMEOUT` countdown driven by `state.runtime` and `app.state.bindMode==="orbit"` (L567-573); uses an `offset = strokeWidth/2` to grow the outline (L574). Frame clipping (L576-597) and per-shape outline drawing mirror the simple variant but include `appState.scrollX/scrollY` in the center/origin translation (L633-643) and inflate ellipse/segment/curve geometry by `offset` (L653-757). Below the timeout it also draws an animated dashed center "snap area" ellipse with `lineDashOffset` driven by progress ratio (L766-808) and, if midpoint snapping enabled, clears cutout squares around midpoints and paints midpoint dots (L810-882). Returns `{runtime: (state?.runtime ?? 0) + deltaTime}` to advance the animation (L885-887). This is the only renderer function that returns animation state.
- `renderBindingHighlightForBindableElement(app, context, suggestedBinding, allElementsMap, appState, deltaTime, state?)` (L890-L931) — dispatcher: returns early if no binding; if `getFeatureFlag("COMPLEX_BINDINGS")` delegates to the complex variant (returning its state); otherwise `save`/`translate(scroll)`, builds `pointerCoords` from `app.lastPointerMoveCoords`, calls the simple variant, `restore`.
- `type ElementSelectionBorder` (L933-L945) — shape describing one selection box: `angle, x1,y1,x2,y2, selectionColors[], dashed?, cx,cy, activeEmbeddable, padding?`.
- `renderSelectionBorder(context, appState, elementProperties)` (L947-L1000) — draws a (possibly multi-color, possibly dashed) selection rectangle. Padding defaults to `DEFAULT_TRANSFORM_HANDLE_SPACING*2`, all sizes divided by zoom (`linePadding`, `lineWidth=8/zoom`, `spaceWidth=4/zoom`) (L967-972). For each color it sets a dash pattern that interleaves the N colors (each color's dash is offset by `(lineWidth+spaceWidth)*index` so multiple remote/local colors form a single multicolor dashed border) (L979-998), drawing via `strokeRectWithRotation_simple`. `activeEmbeddable` thickens the line to 4/zoom.
- `renderFrameHighlight(context, appState, frame, elementsMap)` (L1002-L1030) — strokes a blue (`rgb(0,118,255)`) rounded rect around a frame using `getElementAbsoluteCoords` and `strokeRectWithRotation_simple` with `FRAME_STYLE.radius/zoom`.
- `renderElementsBoxHighlight(context, appState, elements, config?)` (L1032-L1078) — draws box highlights for a set of elements: splits into ungrouped individuals vs grouped (L1039-1045); builds an `ElementSelectionBorder` per group (via `getCommonBounds` of `getElementsInGroup`) and per individual element, then renders each with `renderSelectionBorder`. Default color `rgb(0,118,255)`, optional dashed. Used for frame-drop and locked-element highlights.
- `renderLinearPointHandles(context, appState, element, elementsMap: RenderableElementsMap)` (L1080-L1203) — renders all vertex and midpoint handles for a selected linear element. Returns early if no `selectedLinearElement` (L1086-88). Radius is `POINT_HANDLE_SIZE` when editing else half (L1098-1100). Gets global point coords; for elbow arrows only renders first/last vertices (L1106-1108). Detects overlapping points (coincident within `2/zoom`) (L1110-1117), computes selection state including the polygon last-point-overlaps-first-point special case (L1119-1134), and calls `renderSingleLinearPoint` per vertex (L1136-1145). Then renders segment midpoints: for elbow arrows, midpoints of non-too-short segments as phantom points (L1148-1175); for others, `getEditorMidPoints` filtered, rendered as phantom points when editing or 2-point line (L1176-1200).
- `renderFocusPointConnectionLine(context, appState, fromPoint, toPoint)` (L1205-L1224) — dashed purple (`rgba(134,131,226,0.6)`) line from an arrow endpoint to its binding focus point; dash `4/zoom`, width `1/zoom`.
- `renderFocusPointCicle(context, appState, point, radius, isHovered)` (L1226-L1251) — [sic name] filled+stroked purple circle at the focus point, brighter fill when hovered; radius `radius/zoom`.
- `renderFocusPointIndicator({arrow, appState, type, context, elementsMap})` (L1253-L1343) — for an arrow's `"start"`/`"end"` binding, resolves the bound element and its global fixed focus point (`getGlobalFixedPointForBindableElement`), bails if the element is missing/deleted/not bindable or the focus point isn't visible (`isFocusPointVisible`) (L1266-1296). Optionally renders the hovered highlight (L1307-1312), then (unless the point is selected-and-dragging) draws the connection line from the arrow endpoint to the focus point plus the focus circle at `FOCUS_POINT_SIZE/1.5` (L1317-1342).
- `renderTransformHandles(context, renderConfig, appState, transformHandles: TransformHandles, angle): void` (L1345-L1386) — iterates handle keys; for each defined handle `[x,y,width,height]` sets `lineWidth=1/zoom` and `strokeStyle=selectionColor`. The `"rotation"` handle is drawn as a filled+stroked circle (`fillCircle`) (L1362-1364); other handles prefer native `context.roundRect` (radius `2/zoom`) else fall back to `strokeRectWithRotation_simple` with `fill:true` so the square is white-filled then stroked (L1365-1382).
- `renderCropHandles(context, renderConfig, appState, croppingElement: ExcalidrawImageElement, elementsMap): void` (L1388-L1491) — draws the four L-shaped image-crop corner brackets. Computes half-width/height from absolute coords plus a zoomed line width (`LINE_WIDTH=3`, `LINE_LENGTH=20`, both /zoom) (L1400-1416). Builds a hand-coded array of 4 corner descriptors, each holding the corner origin and two line segments (horizontal + vertical) whose lengths are clamped to the available half-dimension (L1423-1469). Each corner is drawn rotated about the element center `(cx,cy)` by `croppingElement.angle` (L1471-1488). Non-obvious geometry: the bracket arms point inward and are offset by half the line width so strokes don't overhang the image edge.
- `renderTextBox(text, context, appState, selectionColor)` (L1493-L1515) — dashed translucent (alpha 0.5) selection rect around a text element being edited; padding from `getTextBoxPadding(zoom)`, rotated about the text center, dash `[6/zoom, 4/zoom]`.
- `renderResetAutoResizeHandle(text, context, appState, selectionColor, formFactor)` (L1517-L1550) — draws the small "reset auto-resize" handle line (from `getTextAutoResizeHandle`) with rounded caps, alpha 0.5, width `1.5/zoom`; bails if no handle.
- `_renderInteractiveScene(config: InteractiveSceneRenderConfig): { scrollBars?, atLeastOneVisibleElement, elementsMap, animationState? }` (L1552-L2076) — THE main interactive-canvas pass. Sequence: bail if no canvas (L1571-73); compute normalized dims; `bootstrapCanvas` (no background — overlay is transparent) (L1581-86); `save` + `scale(zoom)` (L1589-90). Finds the currently-editing linear element from `visibleElements` (to avoid stale `ShapeCache` versions during collab, per the L1596-1598 comment) and renders its point handles (L1592-1616). Renders the marquee selection element (L1618-1630); the text auto-resize handle for an active non-auto-resize text element (L1632-1642); the editing-text bounding box (L1644-1656). Computes the binding highlight into `nextAnimationState.bindingHighlight` when binding is enabled, else clears it (L1658-1676). Frame highlight (L1678-1685), elements-box highlight (L1687-1689), active-locked highlight in gray dashed (L1691-1700). Re-renders linear point handles for a single editing linear selection (L1709-1720). Renders elbow/linear point hover highlights and arrow focus-point indicators for the selected linear element (L1722-1760). The big selection block (L1763-1985, gated on not multiElement/newElement/editing-linear): computes `showBoundingBox` via `hasBoundingBox`; for a single linear selection renders its handles; iterates ALL `elementsMap` values building per-element selection borders combining local selection color and remote-collaborator colors (skipping bound elbow arrows), with locked elements drawn gray dashed and image/cropping elements getting zero padding (L1791-1859); adds dashed group-bound selections for selected groups and the editing group (L1861-1887); renders all selection borders (L1889-1891). Then transform handles: single selection uses `getTransformHandles` (hidden in view mode / while text-editing / while cropping) and optional crop handles (L1897-1935); multi-selection (>1, not rotating, none locked) draws a dashed common-bounds rect and `getTransformHandlesFromCoords` at angle 0, omitting rotation when a frame is selected (L1936-1983). Search matches are painted as translucent yellow/orange rects, rotated about each matched element's center, with a frame-specific zoom factor (L1987-2028). `renderSnaps`, then `restore` to undo the zoom (L2030-2032). `renderRemoteCursors` is called AFTER restore (it manages its own transform) (L2034-2040). Scrollbars are drawn last via `getScrollBars` + `roundRect` when `renderScrollbars` (L2042-2068). Returns scrollbars, visibility flag, the elementsMap, and the next animation state (L2070-2075). Key coordinate detail: the entire body runs inside one `scale(zoom)`; nearly every nested draw additionally `translate(scrollX, scrollY)` so element world coords map to screen. Performance: builds selection borders by iterating the whole `elementsMap` (not just selected) to also catch remote selections.
- `renderInteractiveScene<U>(renderConfig): ReturnType<U>` (L2082-L2090) — public entry; calls `_renderInteractiveScene`, invokes `renderConfig.callback(ret)` (used by `Renderer` to push scrollbars/state back into the app), and returns the result cast to `U`.

---

### packages/excalidraw/renderer/renderNewElementScene.ts

Purpose: Renders the single element currently being created/dragged on its own dedicated canvas (separate from the static scene so the rest of the document isn't redrawn each pointermove), with optional frame clipping and rAF throttling.

- `_renderNewElementScene({canvas, rc, newElement, elementsMap, allElementsMap, scale, appState, renderConfig})` (L16-L85) — bails if no canvas. Normalizes dims, `bootstrapCanvas` (transparent overlay), `save`, `scale(zoom)` (L27-42). If `newElement` exists and isn't a `"selection"`: returns early (skipping render) if `isInvisiblySmallElement` (e.g. arrow below drag threshold) (L49-51); resolves a frame from `newElement.frameId` or `frameToHighlight` and, when frame rendering+clip are enabled and `shouldApplyFrameClip`, applies `frameClip` (L53-68); then `renderElement` draws the in-progress element (L70-78). If there's no element (or it's a selection), `clearRect`s the canvas (L79-81). `restore` (L83). Note: the early `return` at L50 leaves `context` un-restored — acceptable because the canvas is rebuilt each frame.
- `renderNewElementSceneThrottled` (L87-L91) — `throttleRAF`-wrapped `_renderNewElementScene`, so rapid pointer events coalesce to one draw per animation frame.
- `renderNewElementScene(renderConfig, throttle?)` (L93-L103) — public entry; routes to the throttled version when `throttle`, else calls `_renderNewElementScene` synchronously.

---

### packages/excalidraw/renderer/staticScene.ts

Purpose: The main non-interactive element canvas renderer — paints the background grid, all visible elements (with frame clipping, bound-text, and link icons), embeddables/iframes on top, and pending flowchart nodes; rAF-throttled.

Module-level: `GridLineColor` (L45-L54) — per-theme bold/regular grid colors, dark values precomputed via `applyDarkModeFilter`. `linkIconCanvasCache` (L160-L166) — module-level cache of two offscreen `LinkIconCanvas` (regular vs element link), each tagged with the `zoom` it was rendered at so it's only re-rasterized on zoom change (performance: avoids re-drawing the link bitmap every frame).

- `strokeGrid(context, gridSize, gridStep, scrollX, scrollY, zoom, theme, width, height)` (L56-L130) — draws the dotted/solid grid. Computes start offsets `(scroll % gridSize) - gridSize` (L69-70) and `actualGridSize = gridSize*zoom` (L72). Crispness hack (L78-84): only at `zoom===1` does it translate by 0.5px per-axis (and only on an axis whose offset isn't already fractional) so 1px lines land on pixel centers. Vertical loop (L87-107) and horizontal loop (L109-128): a line is `bold` when `gridStep>1` and its world coordinate is an exact multiple of `gridStep*gridSize`; non-bold regular lines are skipped entirely when `actualGridSize<10` (too dense to see); `lineWidth = min(1/zoom, isBold?4:1)`; bold lines are solid, regular lines use a computed `lineDash`. Coordinate-space: runs inside the caller's `scale(zoom)`, so positions are world coords. Lines extend `gridSize*2` beyond the viewport to avoid edge gaps.
- `frameClip(frame, context, renderConfig, appState)` (L132-L156) — sets a clipping region to a frame's bounds: translates to `frame.(x,y)+scroll`, builds a rounded (`FRAME_STYLE.radius/zoom`) or plain rect path, `clip()`, then translates back. Exported and reused by `renderNewElementScene`. Side effect: mutates the clip region (caller must `save`/`restore` around it).
- `type LinkIconCanvas = HTMLCanvasElement & { zoom: number }` (L158) — offscreen canvas tagged with its render zoom.
- `renderLinkIcon(element, context, appState, elementsMap)` (L168-L228) — draws the hyperlink badge for an element that has a `link` and isn't selected (L174). Computes the handle rect from `getLinkHandleFromCoords`, translates to the badge center + scroll, rotates by element angle (L175-186). Lazily rasterizes the icon into a DPR×zoom-scaled offscreen canvas (filled with the view background, then `EXTERNAL_LINK_IMG` or `ELEMENT_LINK_IMG` drawn) cached per zoom (L191-223). Draws the cached bitmap at `element.opacity/100` alpha (L224-225). Performance detail: the expensive image draw happens once per zoom level; subsequent frames just `drawImage` the cache.
- `_renderStaticScene({canvas, rc, elementsMap, allElementsMap, visibleElements, scale, appState, renderConfig})` (L229-L479) — THE main static pass. Bails if no canvas. Destructures `renderGrid=true, isExporting`. `bootstrapCanvas` WITH theme/background/isExporting (this canvas paints the document background) (L250-258); `scale(zoom)` (L261). Draws grid if enabled (passing viewport dims divided by zoom so the grid covers world-space) (L264-276). First pass (L278-296) computes `groupsToBeAddedToFrame` — group ids whose elements overlap the highlighted frame (so an entire group highlights together). Main element pass (L301-385): skips iframe-likes (rendered later) and bound text (rendered with its container); for each element, `save`, optionally `frameClip` to its target frame, `renderElement`, then render its bound text element, `restore`, and (unless exporting) `renderLinkIcon`; per-element try/catch logs element id+geometry on error. Embeddable pass (L388-461): renders iframe-likes on top, drawing a placeholder label for invalid/exporting embeds, with the same frame-clip logic. Flowchart pending-nodes pass (L463-478): renders `renderConfig.pendingFlowchartNodes` with try/catch. Coordinate-space: single `scale(zoom)`; `renderElement` and `frameClip` apply scroll internally. Ordering invariant: non-iframe elements first, then iframes/embeddables on top, then flowchart ghosts.
- `renderStaticSceneThrottled` (L482-L486) — `throttleRAF`-wrapped `_renderStaticScene` ("throttled to animation framerate" per L481).
- `renderStaticScene(renderConfig, throttle?)` (L491-L501) — public entry; throttled or synchronous depending on `throttle`.

Notable cross-file performance pattern: both the static and new-element scenes use `throttleRAF` so high-frequency state changes collapse to one paint per frame; the interactive scene is throttled by its caller (`Renderer`) instead and additionally returns animation state so the `AnimationController` can keep re-invoking it for the animated binding highlight.
